title: Architecture
code: +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | API Layer (13 files)                                                 | | fr_table -> fr_cols -> fr_header -> fr_titles -> ... -> fr_render    | | Each verb: validate -> modify fr_spec -> return fr_spec              | +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Infrastructure Layer (10 files)                                      | | ard.R         fr_wide_ard(): ARD-to-wide converter                   | | classes.R     S3 constructors (fr_spec, fr_col, fr_rule, ...)        | | columns.R     Width estimation, AFM metrics, auto-distribution       | | config.R      YAML discovery, loading, merging                       | | constants.R   fr_env: fonts, paper sizes, presets, colors            | | helpers.R     Error conditions, text normalisation                   | | markup.R      Inline rich text: fr_super() -> sentinel tokens        | | tokens.R      {thepage}, {program}, {datetime} resolution            | | units.R       Twips, half-points, row height calculation             | | validate.R    Type-safe validation helpers                           | +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Render Layer (8 files)                                               | | render.R          Dispatch: finalize -> prepare_pages -> backend     | | render-common.R   Shared: cell grid, borders, escaping, colors       | | render-rtf.R      RTF 1.9.1 backend                                  | | render-html.R     HTML backend (file, viewer, knitr/pkgdown)         | | render-latex.R    tabularray/XeLaTeX backend                         | | render-pdf.R      PDF wrapper (generates .tex, compiles twice)       | | render-figure.R   Figure rendering (single + multi-page)             | | decimal.R         Stat-display decimal alignment engine              | | paginate.R        R-side pagination (group-aware page breaks)        | +----------------------------------------------------------------------+

title: Architecture
text: Every verb reads writes single S3 object:

title: Architecture
code: fr_spec   |   v finalize_spec()            <- Resolve all deferred computations   |  - Build default columns for unmentioned columns   |  - Distribute widths (auto/fit/equal/percent)   |  - Resolve N-count header labels   |  - Inject group_label header rows (if set)   |  - Insert blank_after rows   |  - Apply indent_by offsets (single or multi-level)   |  - Compute decimal geometry   v prepare_pages()            <- Split data into page groups   |  - Split by page_by values (preserving data order)   |  - Each group: {label, data, n_counts}   v compute_col_panels()     <- Handle wide tables   |  - If .split=TRUE and width > page: split into panels   |  - Stub columns repeat in each panel   v render_<format>()          <- Backend-specific rendering      RTF:   render_rtf(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)      LaTeX: render_latex(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path)      PDF:   render_pdf() -> render_latex() + xelatex x2

title: Architecture
text: Key operations: Width distribution: "auto" measures content AFM font metrics. total exceeds printable area, columns scale proportionally. N-count labels: .n + .n_format fr_cols() resolve final column labels. Group label injection: group_label inserts header rows group boundaries, placing group value target column. Style indices remapped account injected rows. Blank rows: blank_after inserts empty rows group boundaries. Indent: indent_by applies indentation — either single-level (character vector) multi-level (named list key/col/levels). Decimal geometry: 15-type stat display engine analyses cell values, computes space-padded alignment offsets.

title: Architecture
text: backend must implement one function: Register :
code: render_<format>(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) render_html <- function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) {   # ... generate HTML ...   writeLines(html, path)   path } fr_register_backend("html", extensions = "html", render_fn = render_html)  # Now this works: spec |> fr_render("output.html")

title: Architecture
text: render-common.R provides backend-agnostic helpers: build_cell_grid() — data frame rendered cell matrix build_header_cell_grid() — header row resolved N-counts resolve_borders() — rules list per-cell border spec collect_colors() — scan styles color table rtf_escape_and_resolve() / latex_escape_and_resolve() — escape special chars resolve markup sentinels

title: Architecture
text: Inline markup survives string operations via sentinel tokens: Sentinel types: SUPER, SUB, BOLD, ITALIC, UNDERLINE, NEWLINE, UNICODE.
code: User writes:       "P{fr_super('a')}-value < 0.05"                           | glue evaluation:   "P\x01SUPER:a\x02-value < 0.05"                           | RTF backend:       "P{\super a}-value < 0.05" LaTeX backend:     "P\textsuperscript{a}-value < 0.05"

title: Architecture
text: Pre-parsed Adobe Font Metrics 12 font variants R/sysdata.rda: Helvetica ‘M’ = 833 units vs ‘’ = 222 units (3.75x). Courier fixed 600 units. compute_col_width() uses accurate auto-widths.

title: Architecture
code: fr_backends() #>   format extensions                   description #> 1    rtf   rtf, doc              Rich Text Format #> 2  latex        tex     LaTeX source (tabularray) #> 3    pdf        pdf               PDF via XeLaTeX #> 4   html  html, htm HTML preview (self-contained)

title: Architecture
text: HTML backend (render-html.R) works three modes:

title: Architecture
text: pkgdown uses Bootstrap 5. Bootstrap adds class="table" every <table> element post-processing. Bootstrap’s .table class sets borders, padding, display: block, CSS variables conflict arframe’s styles. also breaks Bootstrap’s col-md-9 / col-md-3 grid layout, pushing “page” sidebar bottom page.

title: Architecture
text: arframe solves way gt : Every output wrapped <div id="arframe-XXXXX"> unique timestamp-based ID. CSS scoped ID via scope_css(): .ar-table { } becomes #arframe-XXXXX .ar-table { }. ID-scoped selector higher specificity Bootstrap’s class selector (.table), arframe styles always win without !important. Bootstrap CSS variables explicitly zeroed .ar-table: --bs-table-bg: transparent, --bs-table-border-color: transparent, etc. Knitr output returns htmltools::tags$div() — proper htmltools tag object, raw HTML string. knitr native support htmltools objects via knit_print.shiny.tag, avoids post-processing breaks Bootstrap grid. Using knitr::asis_output() raw HTML original cause sidebar bug.

title: Architecture
text: adding CSS html_embedded_css(): Use .ar-* class names — get auto-scoped scope_css() Plain HTML elements (table, td, etc.) also get scoped automatically Comma-separated selectors split part gets scoped Never use !important — ID scoping provides sufficient specificity Override Bootstrap targeting CSS variables (--bs-table-*)

title: Get Started with arframe
text: ’s complete, renderable table. produce file: spec, format. file extension controls output.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics") |>   fr_hlines("header") spec spec |> fr_render("Table_14_1_1.rtf") spec |> fr_render("Table_14_1_1.pdf") spec |> fr_render("Table_14_1_1.html")

title: Get Started with arframe
text: Real pharma tables need column labels, N-counts, footnotes, page chrome. Start setting shared formatting fr_theme() — every table session inherits : Now table specifies unique : Notice table program: header styling, hlines, page headers/footers, N-format, footnote separator — theme handles . production study programs work. See Automation & Batch full pattern.
code: # ── Set once per study program (or use _arframe.yml) ── fr_theme(   font_size   = 9,   font_family = "Times New Roman",   orientation = "landscape",   hlines      = "header",   header      = list(bold = TRUE, align = "center"),   n_format    = "{label}\n(N={n})",   footnote_separator = FALSE,   pagehead    = list(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   pagefoot    = list(left = "{program}",                      right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") )  n_itt <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135) spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     .width = "fit",     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     total          = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = n_itt   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group") |>   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on number of subjects per treatment group.") fr_validate(spec)

title: Get Started with arframe
text: Every pharma table structural regions. ’s map arframe verbs: 10 verbs, 10 regions. Learn verbs, build table.

title: Get Started with arframe
text: Every verb takes spec, returns spec. Verb order doesn’t matter — arframe resolves everything (widths, N-count labels, page breaks) render time.
code: data |>   fr_table()    |>   # create spec   fr_titles()   |>   # add titles   fr_cols()     |>   # configure columns, N-counts   fr_header()   |>   # style the header row   fr_rows()     |>   # row grouping, pagination   fr_hlines()   |>   # horizontal rules   fr_styles()   |>   # cell-level formatting   fr_render("out.rtf")  # produce output

title: Automation & Batch
text: arframe resolves settings four tiers, lowest highest priority: SAS: Tier 2 = autoexec.sas + PROC TEMPLATE. Tier 3 = OPTIONS. Tier 4 = inline STYLE()=.
code: +----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | 4. Per-table verbs         fr_page(font_size = 10)          <- wins  | | 3. Session theme           fr_theme(font_size = 9)                   | | 2. Project config          _arframe.yml                              | | 1. Package defaults        inst/defaults/_arframe.yml       <- lowest| +----------------------------------------------------------------------+

title: Automation & Batch
text: Place _arframe.yml project root. Every table inherits settings: Custom tokens defined YAML resolve fr_pagehead() fr_pagefoot().
code: page:   paper: letter   orientation: landscape   margins: [1.0, 0.75, 1.0, 0.75]   font_family: "Times New Roman"   font_size: 9   continuation: "(continued)"  header:   bold: false   valign: bottom   n_format: "{label}\n(N={n})"  pagehead:   left: "Protocol TFRM-2024-001"   right: "CONFIDENTIAL"  pagefoot:   left: "{program}"   right: "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"  rules:   hlines: header  footnotes:   separator: false  spacing:   titles_after: 1   footnotes_before: 1  tokens:   study: "TFRM-2024-001"   cutoff: "15MAR2025" fr_config() cfg <- fr_config_get() cfg$page$font_size #> [1] 9 fr_config_reset()

title: Automation & Batch
text: fr_theme() sets session-level defaults. Call top program: Every table now inherits theme: Override per-table:
code: fr_theme(   font_size   = 9,   font_family = "Times New Roman",   orientation = "landscape",   hlines      = "header",   pagehead    = list(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   pagefoot    = list(left = "{program}",                      right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") ) spec1 <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") spec2 <- tbl_disp |> fr_table() |> fr_titles("Table 14.1.4", "Disposition") fr_get_page(spec1)$font_size #> [1] 9 fr_get_page(spec2)$font_size #> [1] 9 spec3 <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "portrait") fr_get_page(spec3)$orientation #> [1] "portrait" fr_get_page(spec3)$font_size #> [1] 9 fr_theme_reset()

title: Automation & Batch
text: Recipes capture verb chains portable, composable objects:
code: company_layout <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}",               right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"),   fr_hlines("header") ) company_layout #>  #> ── fr_recipe (4 verbs) #> 1. fr_page(orientation="landscape", font_size=9) #> 2. fr_pagehead(left="TFRM-2024-001", right="CONFIDENTIAL") #> 3. fr_pagefoot(left="{program}", right="Page {thepage} of {tot...) #> 4. fr_hlines("header") spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(company_layout) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") fr_get_page(spec)$font_size #> [1] 9

title: Automation & Batch
text: c() merges recipes. Later recipes override replacing verbs; appending verbs (fr_spans, fr_styles) accumulate:
code: ae_style <- fr_recipe(   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center"),   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)") ) ae_combined <- c(company_layout, ae_style) spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(ae_combined) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE by SOC/PT") fr_get_page(spec)$continuation #> [1] "(continued)"

title: Automation & Batch
code: # ── Population N-counts (define once) ── n_itt <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)  # Layer 1: Company standard company <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_family = "Times New Roman", font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"),   fr_hlines("header"),   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") )  # Layer 2: Study-specific study <- fr_recipe(   fr_pagehead(left = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001"),   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "15MAR2025")) )  # Layer 3: Table-type (only what varies by table type) demog_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_cols(.n = n_itt, .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"),   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on N per treatment arm.") )  # Usage: only titles and columns are unique per table tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(c(company, study, demog_recipe)) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_render("output/Table_14_1_1.rtf")

title: Automation & Batch
text: Combine metadata table recipes loop: SAS: replaces macro library + %include pattern. metadata CSV macro call dataset, recipes macros, loop %block.
code: metadata <- data.frame(   table_id  = c("14.1.1", "14.1.4", "14.3.1", "14.2.1"),   title1    = c("Demographics", "Subject Disposition",                 "AE by SOC/PT", "Time to Study Withdrawal"),   title2    = c("ITT Population", "All Randomized",                 "Safety Population", "ITT Population"),   data_name = c("tbl_demog", "tbl_disp", "tbl_ae_soc", "tbl_tte"),   recipe    = c("demog", "demog", "ae", "tte"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) metadata #>   table_id                   title1            title2  data_name recipe #> 1   14.1.1             Demographics    ITT Population  tbl_demog  demog #> 2   14.1.4      Subject Disposition    All Randomized   tbl_disp  demog #> 3   14.3.1             AE by SOC/PT Safety Population tbl_ae_soc     ae #> 4   14.2.1 Time to Study Withdrawal    ITT Population    tbl_tte    tte for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {   row <- metadata[i, ]   data <- get(row$data_name)   recipe <- recipes[[row$recipe]]    data |>     fr_table() |>     fr_apply(c(company, study, recipe)) |>     fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1, row$title2) |>     fr_render(sprintf("output/Table_%s.rtf", gsub("\\.", "_", row$table_id))) }

title: Automation & Batch
text: Linux font fallback: systems without Microsoft fonts, PDF rendering automatically falls back Adobe open-source fonts — Source Serif 4, Source Sans 3, Source Code Pro (SIL Open Font License). extra font installation needed. Custom font directory: Set ARFRAME_FONT_DIR environment variable directory containing .ttf .otf files. arframe passes XeLaTeX via OSFONTDIR, making fonts discoverable family name — system-wide installation required. ideal Docker containers, CI pipelines, project-local font directories:
code: # Shell: export ARFRAME_FONT_DIR=/path/to/project/fonts # R: Sys.setenv(ARFRAME_FONT_DIR = "/path/to/project/fonts") spec |> fr_page(font_family = "Courier New") |> fr_render("output.pdf") install.packages("tinytex") tinytex::install_tinytex()

title: Automation & Batch
text: fr_validate() checks columns, widths, styles, spans, fonts, row references. Use strict = TRUE CI pipelines.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) fr_validate(spec)

title: Automation & Batch
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL.") |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE))  stopifnot(length(fr_get_titles(spec)) == 2) stopifnot(grepl("ADSL", fr_get_footnotes(spec)[[1]]$content)) stopifnot(fr_get_page(spec)$orientation == "landscape")

title: Automation & Batch
code: # Programmer 1 spec1 <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_render("output/t_14_1_1_prod.rtf")  # Programmer 2 (QC) spec2 <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_render("output/t_14_1_1_qc.rtf")  # Same data + same verbs = identical output

title: Automation & Batch
code: # render_all.R library(arframe) fr_config("_arframe.yml") metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {   row <- metadata[i, ]   data <- get(row$data_name)   recipe <- readRDS(sprintf("recipes/%s.rds", row$recipe))   data |>     fr_table() |>     fr_apply(recipe) |>     fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1) |>     fr_render(sprintf("output/Table_%s.rtf", gsub("\\.", "_", row$table_id))) } FROM rocker/r-ver:4.3.3 RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y libxml2-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev RUN R -e "install.packages(c('cli','glue','rlang','tidyselect','vctrs','yaml','stringi'))" RUN R -e "install.packages('tinytex'); tinytex::install_tinytex()" # Adobe open-source fonts serve as fallback --- no Microsoft fonts needed  # Optional: bundle project-specific fonts (e.g., Times New Roman) COPY fonts/ /opt/fonts/ ENV ARFRAME_FONT_DIR=/opt/fonts

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: Place _arframe.yml project root. Every table program directory (subdirectories) inherits settings automatically — fr_theme() call needed: arframe discovers file walking working directory (like .Rprofile). never need specify path.
code: # _arframe.yml page:   paper: letter   orientation: landscape   margins: [1.0, 0.75, 1.0, 0.75]   font_family: "Times New Roman"   font_size: 9   continuation: "(continued)"  header:   bold: true   align: center   n_format: "{label}\n(N={n})"  pagehead:   left: "Protocol TFRM-2024-001"   right: "CONFIDENTIAL"  pagefoot:   left: "{program}"   right: "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"  rules:   hlines: header  footnotes:   separator: false  spacing:   titles_after: 1   footnotes_before: 1  tokens:   study: "TFRM-2024-001"   cutoff: "15MAR2025"

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: four-tier precedence:
code: Package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < per-table verbs

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: don’t YAML file (quick scripts, interactive work), set session defaults fr_theme(): Now every table inherits :
code: fr_theme(   font_size   = 9,   font_family = "Times New Roman",   orientation = "landscape",   hlines      = "header",   header      = list(bold = TRUE, align = "center"),   n_format    = "{label}\n(N={n})",   footnote_separator = FALSE,   pagehead    = list(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   pagefoot    = list(left = "{program}",                      right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") ) spec1 <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") spec2 <- tbl_disp |> fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.4", "Disposition") fr_get_page(spec1)$font_size #> [1] 9 fr_get_page(spec2)$font_size #> [1] 9

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: Recipes capture verb chains portable objects: Apply table:
code: # Company standard company <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}",               right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"),   fr_hlines("header"),   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") )  # AE tables need continuation text ae_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)") )  # Compose with c() ae_combined <- c(company, ae_recipe) spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(ae_combined) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE by SOC/PT") fr_get_page(spec)$continuation #> [1] "(continued)"

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: n_itt <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)  # Layer 1: Company standard (shared across all studies) company <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_family = "Times New Roman",           font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}",               right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"),   fr_hlines("header"),   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") )  # Layer 2: Study-specific study <- fr_recipe(   fr_pagehead(left = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001"),   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "15MAR2025")) )  # Layer 3: Table-type (only what varies) demog_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_cols(.n = n_itt, .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"),   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on N per treatment arm.") )  # One table: only titles and columns are unique tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(c(company, study, demog_recipe)) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_render("output/Table_14_1_1.rtf")

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: minimal pipeline: Build progressively: Validate rendering:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_hlines("header") spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     .width = "fit",     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     total     = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on number of subjects per treatment group.") fr_validate(spec)

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: Combine metadata table recipes loop:
code: metadata <- data.frame(   table_id  = c("14.1.1", "14.1.4", "14.3.1"),   title1    = c("Demographics", "Subject Disposition", "AE by SOC/PT"),   title2    = c("ITT Population", "All Randomized", "Safety Population"),   data_name = c("tbl_demog", "tbl_disp", "tbl_ae_soc"),   recipe    = c("demog", "demog", "ae"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  recipes <- list(demog = demog_recipe, ae = ae_recipe)  for (i in seq_len(nrow(metadata))) {   row <- metadata[i, ]   data <- get(row$data_name)   recipe <- recipes[[row$recipe]]    data |>     fr_table() |>     fr_apply(c(company, study, recipe)) |>     fr_titles(paste("Table", row$table_id), row$title1, row$title2) |>     fr_render(sprintf("output/Table_%s.rtf",                       gsub("\\.", "_", row$table_id))) }

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: cards package produces Analysis Results Data (ARD). fr_wide_ard() converts ARD output wide data frame ready fr_table().

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: library(cards)  demog_ard <- ard_stack(   data = adsl_saf,   .by = "TRT01A",   ard_continuous(variables = "AGE"),   ard_categorical(variables = c("AGEGR1", "SEX", "RACE")),   .overall = TRUE )  demog_wide <- fr_wide_ard(   demog_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous = c(       "n"         = "{N}",       "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})",       "Median"    = "{median}",       "Q1, Q3"    = "{p25}, {p75}",       "Min, Max"  = "{min}, {max}"     ),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ),   decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, median = 1, p25 = 1, p75 = 1, p = 1),   label = c(     AGE    = "Age (years)",     AGEGR1 = "Age Group, n (%)",     SEX    = "Sex, n (%)",     RACE   = "Race, n (%)"   ) )  demog_wide |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     variable   = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     stat_label = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     .align = "decimal"   ) |>   fr_rows(     group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat_label"),     group_style = list(bold = TRUE)   )

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: ae_ard <- ard_stack(   data = ae_subj,   .by = "TRT01A",   ard_dichotomous(     variables = c(any_teae, any_sae, any_death),     value = list(any_teae = TRUE, any_sae = TRUE, any_death = TRUE)   ),   .overall = TRUE )  fr_wide_ard(ae_ard, statistic = "{n} ({p}%)", decimals = c(p = 1))

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: output contains soc, pt, row_type columns hierarchical grouping fr_rows().
code: ae_ard <- ard_stack_hierarchical(   data        = adae_teae,   variables   = c(AEBODSYS, AEDECOD),   by          = TRT01A,   denominator = adsl_saf,   id          = USUBJID,   overall     = TRUE,   over_variables = TRUE ) |>   sort_ard_hierarchical(sort = "descending")  ae_wide <- fr_wide_ard(   ae_ard,   statistic = "{n} ({p}%)",   decimals  = c(p = 1),   label     = c(     "..ard_hierarchical_overall.." = "TOTAL SUBJECTS WITH AN EVENT"   ) )

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: fr_wide_ard(   demog_ard,   statistic = list(     AGE = c(       "n"         = "{N}",       "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})"     ),     SEX = "{n} ({p}%)",     .default = "{n} ({p}%)"   ),   decimals = list(     AGE = c(mean = 1, sd = 2),     .default = c(p = 1)   ) )

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: result <- fr_wide_ard(   demog_ard,   statistic = "{n} ({p}%)",   big_n = "N" )  # N-counts extracted to attribute result |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(.n = attr(result, "n_counts"), .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})")

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) fr_validate(spec)

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: stopifnot(length(fr_get_titles(spec)) == 2) stopifnot(fr_get_page(spec)$orientation == "landscape")

title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
code: # Programmer 1 spec1 <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_render("output/t_14_1_1_prod.rtf")  # Programmer 2 (QC) spec2 <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") |>   fr_render("output/t_14_1_1_qc.rtf")  # Same data + same verbs = identical output

what: Custom stat types for decimal alignment
title: Best Practices & Pharmaverse Integration
text: Register custom formats data uses stat patterns covered 18 built-types: registration, align = "decimal" automatically detects aligns new format alongside standard types.
code: fr_register_stat_type(   name    = "ratio_ci",   pattern = "^-?\\d+\\.?\\d*\\s*\\(-?\\d+\\.?\\d*,\\s*-?\\d+\\.?\\d*\\)$",   family  = "compound",   richness = 4L )

title: Columns & Headers
text: named argument fr_cols() fr_col() spec: Columns don’t mention get auto-generated defaults. Set visible = FALSE keep column logic (grouping, indentation) without rendering .
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", align = "right"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total", align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) names(fr_get_columns(spec)) #> [1] "characteristic" "placebo"        "zom_50mg"       "zom_100mg"      #> [5] "total"          "group"

title: Columns & Headers
text: SAS: DEFINE col / DISPLAY "Label" STYLE(column)=[width=2.5in just=right];

title: Columns & Headers
text: .width argument fr_cols() controls global width strategy: Percentage widths work per-column: pharma tables, "fit" explicit inch widths work best.
code: fr_cols(.width = NULL, ...)     # Same as "auto": estimate from content using font metrics fr_cols(.width = "auto", ...)   # Estimate from content using font metrics fr_cols(.width = "fit", ...)    # Auto + scale to fill the page fr_cols(.width = "equal", ...)  # Divide page equally among unfixed columns spec <- data.frame(a = 1) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(a = fr_col("Alpha", width = "25%")) fr_get_col(spec, "a")$width #> [1] 0.25 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_pct"

title: Columns & Headers
text: Clinical tables display mixed statistics column. align = "decimal" auto-detects stat type aligns decimal points: engine recognises counts, floats, n (%), n/N (%), estimates spreads, CIs, range pairs, p-values, missing values.
code: decimal_data <- data.frame(   stat  = c("n", "Mean (SD)", "Median", "Min, Max", "p-value"),   value = c("45", "68.2 (7.1)", "68.0", "52, 84", "<0.001"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) spec <- decimal_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     stat  = fr_col("Statistic", width = 1.5),     value = fr_col("Value", width = 2.0, align = "decimal")   )

title: Columns & Headers
text: Instead naming every column individually, can use formula selector ~ fr_col(...) apply spec multiple columns . left-hand side tidyselect expression; right-hand side fr_col() apply: Formulas named arguments can mixed freely call. Named arguments take precedence formulas column matched .
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     starts_with("zom") ~ fr_col(width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total   = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group   = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) names(fr_get_columns(spec)) #> [1] "characteristic" "placebo"        "zom_50mg"       "zom_100mg"      #> [5] "total"          "group"

title: Columns & Headers
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     .label_fn = function(x) gsub("_", " ", tools::toTitleCase(x)),     group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) fr_get_columns(spec)$characteristic$label #> [1] "Characteristic"

title: Columns & Headers
text: Setting group = "label" fr_col() compact alternative calling fr_spans() separately. Columns share group string automatically grouped spanning header label: equivalent adding fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")) fr_cols(). can still supply .n .n_format time — group names matched N-count auto-routing logic, N appears span: Use group= already naming every column fr_cols() want avoid separate fr_spans() call. Use fr_spans() directly prefer tidyselect column selection, multi-level spans, fine-grained control .hline .gap.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  width = 1.5, align = "right", group = "Zomerane"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right", group = "Zomerane"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  width = 1.5, align = "right", group = "Zomerane"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right", group = "Zomerane"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = c(Zomerane = 90, placebo = 45, total = 135),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center")

title: Columns & Headers
text: fr_header() controls header presentation — bold, alignment, colors, font size. handle N-counts (go fr_cols()). fr_header() parameters: align, valign ("top" / "middle" / "bottom"), bold, background, color, font_size, repeat_on_page (default TRUE — set FALSE print header first page).
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center")

title: Columns & Headers
text: Target different columns different alignments: SAS: STYLE(header)=[just=center font_weight=bold];
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") |>   fr_cols(characteristic = fr_col(header_align = "left"))

title: Columns & Headers
text: N-counts go fr_cols(.n = ..., .n_format = ...). Names matched case-insensitively — first column display label, data column name fallback. Use column names avoid repeating labels:

title: Columns & Headers
text: .n_format template two tokens: {label} (display label) {n} (count). Column header becomes "Placebo\n(N=45)". Tip: study programs, set .n_format fr_header() via fr_theme(n_format = ..., header = list(bold = TRUE, align = "center")) never repeat per-table. See Automation & Batch.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center")

title: Columns & Headers
text: page_by splits table, N-counts may differ per page:
code: fr_cols(   ...,   .n = list(     "Systolic BP (mmHg)"  = c(placebo = 42, zom_50mg = 44, zom_100mg = 43),     "Diastolic BP (mmHg)" = c(placebo = 42, zom_50mg = 44, zom_100mg = 43)   ),   .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})" )

title: Columns & Headers
text: Pre-compute N-counts pass data frame. 2-column data frame (treatment + count) gives N every page. 3-column data frame (page_by group + treatment + count) gives per-page N-counts:
code: # 2-column: global N (same on every page) n_global <- data.frame(   trt = c("Placebo", "Zomerane 50mg"),   n   = c(45, 44) ) fr_cols(..., .n = n_global, .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})")  # 3-column: per-page N (for page_by tables) n_per_page <- aggregate(   USUBJID ~ PARAM + TRTA, data = advs[advs$AVISIT == "Baseline", ],   FUN = function(x) length(unique(x)) ) fr_cols(..., .n = n_per_page, .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})")

title: Columns & Headers
text: fr_col(n = ...) overrides .n specific column:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50 mg", n = 45),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo", n = 45),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center")

title: Columns & Headers
text: SAS: replaces PROC FREQ + PROC TRANSPOSE build N headers, manual COMPUTE blocks attach . arframe one fr_cols() call.

title: Columns & Headers
text: Spanning headers additional header rows sit regular column labels, grouping related columns shared label. standard way show treatment arms, dose groups, timepoint periods regulatory tables. fr_spans() appends spans spec (unlike verbs replace). Call multiple times build multi-level structures.

title: Columns & Headers
text: span label (“Zomerane”) sits two Zomerane columns. Columns covered span (placebo, total) display empty cell span row.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = c(zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, placebo = 45, total = 135),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"))

title: Columns & Headers
text: Build inner outer. Level 1 sits directly column labels; level 2 sits level 1: fr_spans() call .level = 1L places “10 mg” “25 mg” respective column pairs. second-level call places “Zomerane” level-1 spans.
code: dose_data <- data.frame(   param    = "Weight",   low_n    = "43", low_pct  = "31.9%",   high_n   = "47", high_pct = "34.8%",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) spec <- dose_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     param    = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     low_n    = fr_col("n",   width = 1.0, align = "right"),     low_pct  = fr_col("%",   width = 1.0, align = "right"),     high_n   = fr_col("n",   width = 1.0, align = "right"),     high_pct = fr_col("%",   width = 1.0, align = "right")   ) |>   fr_spans("10 mg"    = c("low_n",  "low_pct"),  .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("25 mg"    = c("high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("low_n", "low_pct", "high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 2L)

title: Columns & Headers
text: default thin horizontal line drawn span visually separate column labels . Set .hline = FALSE suppress :
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"), .hline = FALSE)

title: Columns & Headers
text: two spans sit side side level, fr_spans() inserts narrow invisible gap column default (.gap = TRUE). creates clean visual break without relying border trimming. Set .gap = FALSE remove gap produce continuous span hline:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane"  = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),     "Reference" = "placebo",     .gap = FALSE   )

title: Columns & Headers
text: column list right-hand side named argument accepts tidyselect expressions well character vectors: Supported helpers include starts_with(), ends_with(), contains(), matches(), tidyselect expression.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",   width = 1.5, align = "right"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = starts_with("zom_"))

title: Columns & Headers
text: SAS: Spanning headers require COMPUTE block PROC REPORT STYLE(SPANNING). arframe handles positioning automatically single fr_spans() call.

title: Columns & Headers
text: table many columns one page, .split = TRUE creates panels stub columns repeating : stub = TRUE columns repeat every panel. .width = "fit" scales panel fill page.
code:
spec <- tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24" &                 tbl_vs$param == "Systolic BP (mmHg)", ] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     param     = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     timepoint = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     statistic = fr_col("Statistic", width = 1.2, stub = TRUE),     placebo_base  = fr_col("Placebo
Baseline", width = 1.0),     placebo_value = fr_col("Placebo
Value", width = 1.0),     placebo_chg   = fr_col("Placebo
CFB", width = 1.0),     zom_50mg_base  = fr_col("Zom 50mg
Baseline", width = 1.0),     zom_50mg_value = fr_col("Zom 50mg
Value", width = 1.0),     zom_50mg_chg   = fr_col("Zom 50mg
CFB", width = 1.0),     zom_100mg_base  = fr_col("Zom 100mg
Baseline", width = 1.0),     zom_100mg_value = fr_col("Zom 100mg
Value", width = 1.0),     zom_100mg_chg   = fr_col("Zom 100mg
CFB", width = 1.0),     .split = TRUE, .width = "fit"   )

title: Fonts
text: package default Times New Roman 9 pt — FDA-recommended serif font submission documents. applies backends: RTF, PDF, HTML.
code: # inst/defaults/_arframe.yml (shipped with arframe) page:   font_family: "Times New Roman"   font_size: 9

title: Fonts
text: arframe needs font, checks availability order: Priority 1 covers Windows macOS box. bare Linux/Docker, Microsoft fonts typically absent, arframe moves next tier. Priority 2 checks directory pointed ARFRAME_FONT_DIR environment variable. Place .ttf .otf files arframe find — system-wide installation needed. recommended approach Docker, CI, reproducible environments: Priority 3 uses Adobe Source font family (SIL Open Font License 1.1). free, high-quality, designed pair together. Install : Source Serif 4 — serif Source Sans 3 — sans-serif Source Code Pro — monospace via Google Fonts Priority 4 (HTML ) CSS generic family keyword. RTF/PDF viewers substitute closest available system font.
code: export ARFRAME_FONT_DIR="/opt/fonts" # Place SourceSerif4-Regular.ttf etc. in /opt/fonts/

title: Fonts
text: arframe classifies every font one three families. family maps correct AFM metrics (auto-width columns), RTF family keyword, LaTeX font command: Unknown fonts default monospace family.

title: Fonts
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(font_family = "Calibri", font_size = 10)

title: Fonts
code: fr_theme(font_family = "Arial", font_size = 10) # All subsequent fr_table() calls use Arial 10pt

title: Fonts
text: Place _arframe.yml project root:
code: page:   font_family: "Source Sans 3"   font_size: 10

title: Fonts
text: Package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < fr_page().

title: Fonts
text: Courier New remains standard code listings column alignment matters. Use fr_page(font_family = "Courier New") listing outputs:
code: spec <- listing_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(font_family = "Courier New", font_size = 8)

title: Fonts
text: Source: FDA PDF Specifications, ICH E3 Structure Content Clinical Study Reports.

title: Listings & Figures
text: fr_listing() creates spec optimised record-level patient listings. uses pipeline verbs fr_table() different defaults:

title: Listings & Figures
code: ae_list <- adae[1:40, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD",                          "AESEV", "AESER", "AEREL", "AEACN")] ae_listing_spec <- ae_list |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_titles(     "Listing 16.2.7.1",     list("Adverse Events", bold = TRUE),     "Safety Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID  = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.3),     AEBODSYS = fr_col("System Organ Class", width = 1.8),     AEDECOD  = fr_col("Preferred Term", width = 1.5),     AESEV    = fr_col("Severity", width = 0.8),     AESER    = fr_col("Serious", width = 0.7),     AEREL    = fr_col("Related", width = 0.8),     AEACN    = fr_col("Action", width = 1.2)   ) |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD"),           suppress = "USUBJID") |>   fr_footnotes(     "Related = Probable or Possible.",     "Action = Action taken with study drug."   ) fr_validate(ae_listing_spec) ae_listing_spec |> fr_render("output/Listing_16_2_7_1.rtf")

title: Listings & Figures
text: sort_by orders rows named columns: suppress suppresses repeated values (like SAS NOREPEAT): wrap = TRUE enables automatic text wrapping (default listings):
code: spec <- adae[1:10, c("USUBJID", "AEDECOD", "AESEV")] |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "AEDECOD")) spec <- adae[1:10, c("ARM", "USUBJID", "AEDECOD")] |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("ARM", "USUBJID"),           suppress = c("ARM", "USUBJID")) spec <- adae[1:5, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS")] |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_rows(wrap = TRUE)

title: Listings & Figures
text: fr_figure() wraps ggplot2 base R plot pipeline, giving titles, footnotes, page headers, footers:
code: # fr_figure() wraps a ggplot2 or base R plot # pagehead/pagefoot are inherited from fr_theme() --- no need to repeat my_plot |>   fr_figure(width = 8, height = 5) |>   fr_titles(     "Figure 14.2.1",     "Kaplan-Meier Plot of Time to Study Withdrawal"   )

title: Listings & Figures
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   library(ggplot2)   km_plot <- ggplot(adtte, aes(x = AVAL, colour = ARM)) +     stat_ecdf(geom = "step") +     labs(x = "Days", y = "Proportion Event-Free") +     theme_minimal()    fig_spec <- km_plot |>     fr_figure(width = 8, height = 5) |>     fr_titles(       "Figure 14.2.1",       "Kaplan-Meier Plot of Time to Study Withdrawal",       "Intent-to-Treat Population"     ) |>     fr_footnotes("Tick marks indicate censored observations.") } fig_spec |> fr_render("output/Figure_14_2_1.pdf")

title: Listings & Figures
text: Pass list plots create multi-page figure. Add meta data frame inject per-page tokens titles footnotes: meta column name becomes {token} resolved dynamically per page. second title line renders “Subgroup: Adults (N = 80)” page 1, “Subgroup: Pediatrics (N = 55)” page 2, .
code: # Three KM plots --- one per subgroup km_plots <- list(p_adults, p_pediatrics, p_geriatrics) page_meta <- data.frame(   subgroup = c("Adults", "Pediatrics", "Geriatrics"),   n        = c(80, 55, 30) )  km_plots |>   fr_figure(meta = page_meta) |>   fr_titles(     "Figure 14.1.1 Kaplan-Meier Curve",     "Subgroup: {subgroup} (N = {n})"   ) |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADTTE") |>   fr_render("km_by_subgroup.rtf")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: unique value param gets page section. label appears header section.
code: vs_wk24 <- tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24",                    c("param", "statistic", "placebo_value",                      "zom_50mg_value", "zom_100mg_value")] spec <- vs_wk24 |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(param = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: Page_by labels plain text default. Style rows = "page_by": Set study-wide defaults via fr_theme(): SAS: param; PROC REPORT #BYVAL titles.
code: spec <- vs_wk24 |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(param = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "page_by", bold = TRUE)   ) fr_theme(page_by_style = list(bold = TRUE, align = "left"))

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: group_by keeps groups together pagination. blank_after inserts empty row group.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: indent_by indents named column’s values 2 characters beneath group header. SOC terms appear flush-left; PTs indented.
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt       = fr_col("SOC / Preferred Term", width = 3.0),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc", indent_by = "pt")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: deeper hierarchies (SOC / HLT / PT), pass named list key column determines indent level per row: level adds 2 space-character widths (~0.17 9pt). SOC rows get 0 indent, HLT rows get 1 level, PT rows get 2 levels.
code: ae_hierarchy <- data.frame(   soc      = rep("Gastrointestinal disorders", 5),   term     = c("Gastrointestinal disorders", "GI signs and symptoms",                "Nausea", "Vomiting", "Diarrhoea"),   row_type = c("soc", "hlt", "pt", "pt", "pt"),   total    = c("72 (53.3)", "54 (40.0)", "24 (17.8)", "18 (13.3)", "12 (8.9)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  spec <- ae_hierarchy |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     term     = fr_col("SOC / HLT / Preferred Term", width = 3.5),     total    = fr_col("Total\nn (%)")   ) |>   fr_rows(     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = list(       key    = "row_type",       col    = "term",       levels = c(soc = 0, hlt = 1, pt = 2)     )   )

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: group names display values live separate columns (e.g., demographics "Sex" group "Male" / "Female" statistics), group_label auto-injects group value header row target display column: inserts "Sex" "Age (years)" header rows stat column group boundary. Detail rows automatically indented underneath (indent_by set, inferred label).
code: demog_long <- data.frame(   variable = c("Sex", "Sex", "Age (years)", "Age (years)", "Age (years)"),   stat     = c("Female", "Male", "Mean (SD)", "Median", "Min, Max"),   value    = c("27 (60.0)", "18 (40.0)", "75.0 (6.8)", "74.0", "65, 88"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  spec <- demog_long |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(variable = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat")   )

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: Bold group headers group_style: full control, use fr_styles() rows = "group_headers": approaches work leaf hierarchies . Target specific levels per-level group_style rows = "group_headers:soc":
code: spec <- demog_long |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(variable = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat"),     group_style = list(bold = TRUE)   ) spec <- demog_long |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(variable = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat")) |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "group_headers", bold = TRUE, background = "#F0F0F0")   ) fr_rows(   group_by = list(cols = c("soc", "pt"), leaf = "pt"),   group_style = list(soc = list(bold = TRUE)) )

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: default, group_by keeps groups together page. Set group_keep = FALSE visual-grouping (indent, group headers) without page-keeping — useful long groups want renderer break freely:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", group_keep = FALSE)

what: wrap: text wrapping in body cells
title: Rows & Page Layout
text: listings long text fields, set wrap = TRUE enable text wrapping body cells. Without wrapping, long values overflow cell boundary; , cell grows vertically fit: wrap pairs naturally suppress listings first column (e.g., subject ID) appear per block:
code: adae[1:10, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AEOUT")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(wrap = TRUE) adae[1:30, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AESEV", "ASTDT")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     sort_by     = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD"),     suppress = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS"),     wrap        = TRUE   )

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: Use list form page_by visible = FALSE get page breaks group boundaries without visible label headers:
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(soc = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(page_by = list(cols = "soc", visible = FALSE))

title: Rows & Page Layout
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt       = fr_col("SOC / PT", width = 3.0),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total    = fr_col("Total")   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc", indent_by = "pt")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: listings, sort_by controls row order suppress suppresses repeated values: suppress prints value changes (like NOREPEAT SAS).
code: ae_list <- adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "ARM", "AEDECOD", "AESEV")] spec <- ae_list |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("ARM", "USUBJID"),           suppress = "ARM")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: package defaults — need fr_page() overriding.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(     orientation = "landscape",     paper       = "letter",     font_family = "Times New Roman",     font_size   = 9,     margins     = c(top = 1.0, right = 0.75, bottom = 1.0, left = 0.75)   ) pg <- fr_get_page(spec) pg$orientation #> [1] "landscape"

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: orphan_min widow_min prevent isolated rows page boundaries. orphan_min (default 3L) minimum number body rows must remain bottom page group; fewer remain, entire group moves next page. widow_min (default 3L) minimum number rows must carry top next page: Set either 1L disable corresponding control. settings take effect group_by active. Custom fonts PDF: Set ARFRAME_FONT_DIR directory .ttf/.otf files XeLaTeX discovers name — system-wide installation needed. See vignette("automation") Docker/CI examples.
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc") |>   fr_page(orphan_min = 2L, widow_min = 2L)

title: Rows & Page Layout
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Protocol TFRM-2024-001",     right = "CONFIDENTIAL"   ) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left   = "{program}",     center = "{datetime}",     right  = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   )

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: SAS: TITLE j=l "Protocol..." j=r "CONFIDENTIAL"; FOOTNOTE j=l "&_SASPROGRAMFILE" j=r "Page ^{thispage} ^{lastpage}";
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "15MAR2025")) |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "Study: {study}", right = "Cutoff: {cutoff}")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: multi-page tables, continuation appends text titles page 2+:
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "TEAEs by SOC and Preferred Term") |>   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)")

title: Rows & Page Layout
text: fr_spacing() controls blank lines structural sections:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spacing(     titles_after     = 1,     footnotes_before = 1,     pagehead_after   = 0,     pagefoot_before  = 0,     page_by_after    = 1   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header")

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: Width options: "hairline" (0.25pt), "thin" (0.5pt), "medium" (1pt), "thick" (1.5pt), numeric value points. SAS: STYLE(header)=[borderbottomstyle=solid borderbottomwidth=1pt];
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("open", width = "thick", color = "#003366", linestyle = "dashed")

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: Vertical presets: "box" (outer edges ), "" (every column + outer), "inner" (columns ), "void" (none). fr_grid() shorthand combined horizontal + vertical rules:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("inner") spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_grid("box/all", width = "thin", color = "#999999")

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: fr_spans() groups columns shared label: Unlike verbs, fr_spans() appends repeated calls.
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_spans("Active Treatment" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"))

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: Build inner outer (lowest level first): Level 1 sits column labels; level 2 sits level 1.
code: spec |>   fr_spans("10 mg"  = c("low_n", "low_pct"),  .level = 1) |>   fr_spans("25 mg"  = c("high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 1) |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("low_n", "low_pct",                            "high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 2)

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = starts_with("zom_"))

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: default, narrow gap separates adjacent spans level. Disable .gap = FALSE:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),     "Reference" = "placebo",     .gap = FALSE   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: Apply visual overrides rows, columns, individual cells:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total     = fr_col("Total"),     group     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = 1, bold = TRUE),     fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB"),     fr_style(rows = 3, cols = "placebo", color = "#CC0000")   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: fr_style() targets specific region: "body" (default), "header", "stub". valign controls vertical placement within cell: "top", "middle", "bottom". Like fr_spans(), fr_styles() appends repeated calls.

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: fr_rows() creates group headers (via group_by label leaf), style group_style fr_rows() rows = "group_headers" fr_styles(): leaf hierarchies, target specific levels rows = "group_headers:soc":
code: demog_long <- data.frame(   variable = c("Sex", "Sex", "Age", "Age"),   stat     = c("Female", "Male", "Mean (SD)", "Median"),   value    = c("27 (60.0)", "18 (40.0)", "75.0 (6.8)", "74.0"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Quick path: group_style on fr_rows() spec <- demog_long |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(variable = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat"),     group_style = list(bold = TRUE)   ) # Full path: fr_styles() with group_headers selector spec <- demog_long |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(variable = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat")) |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "group_headers", bold = TRUE, background = "#F0F0F0")   ) spec |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "group_headers:soc", bold = TRUE),     fr_row_style(rows = "group_headers:hlt", italic = TRUE)   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: Page_by labels (fr_rows(page_by = "param")) plain text default across backends. Style rows = "page_by": Supported properties: bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_size, align. Consistent across RTF, HTML, PDF.
code: spec <- tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24", ] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(param = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           timepoint = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "page_by", bold = TRUE)   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: fr_rows_matches() selects rows data values instead row numbers: Regex patterns work :
code: spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt       = fr_col("SOC / PT", width = 3.0),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = fr_col("Placebo"),     zom_50mg = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg"),     total    = fr_col("Total")   ) |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "soc"), bold = TRUE     ),     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "total"),       bold = TRUE, background = "#D5E8D4"     )   ) spec <- tbl_tte |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("statistic", pattern = "^[A-Z]"),       bold = TRUE     )   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, background = "#003366",              color = "#FFFFFF", align = "center")   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
code: spec <- tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = seq(1, nrow(tbl_disp), 2), background = "#F5F5F5")   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: fr_style_if() applies styles based cell values rather row numbers. condition one-sided formula using .x pronoun, plain function. evaluated render time actual data, works correctly even row positions unknown advance. Bold “Randomized” row — match exact text column: Zebra striping without hard-coded indices — cols = NULL, .x receives row indices: Highlight significant p-values — numeric comparison character column: Colour matching cells — use apply_to = "cell" (default) instead "row": condition argument also accepts plain function instead formula: SAS: COMPUTE category; category = "Randomized" CALL DEFINE(_ROW_, "STYLE", "STYLE=[FONT_WEIGHT=BOLD BACKGROUND=GRAY]"); ENDCOMP; fr_style_if() objects passed fr_styles() alongside fr_row_style(), fr_col_style(), fr_style(). four constructor types can mixed single fr_styles() call.
code: spec <- tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "category",       condition = ~ .x == "Randomized",       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, background = "#E8E8E8"     )   ) spec <- tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       condition = ~ (.x %% 2) == 0,       apply_to = "row",       background = "#F5F5F5"     )   ) pval_data <- data.frame(   characteristic = c("Age", "Sex", "Weight"),   treatment = c("50 (23.5)", "30 (14.1)", "45 (21.1)"),   placebo   = c("55 (25.8)", "28 (13.1)", "52 (24.4)"),   pvalue    = c("0.042", "0.310", "0.003"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  spec <- pval_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "pvalue",       condition = ~ as.numeric(.x) < 0.05,       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, color = "#CC0000"     )   ) spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "soc",       condition = ~ grepl("SKIN|GASTROINTESTINAL", .x, ignore.case = TRUE),       apply_to = "row",       background = "#FFF3CD"     )   ) is_randomized <- function(x) x == "Randomized"  tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "category",       condition = is_randomized,       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, background = "#E8E8E8"     )   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: multiple styles target cell, later styles win. Order broadest specific:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_styles(     fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#F0F4F8"),       # broadest     fr_row_style(rows = 1, bold = TRUE),                # narrower     fr_style(rows = 1, cols = "total",              background = "#003366", color = "#FFFFFF")             # most specific   )

title: Rules, Spans & Styles
text: fr_style_explain() shows resolution chain specific cell:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = 1, bold = TRUE),     fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB")   ) fr_style_explain(spec, row = 1, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total" #> Content: "135" #> 2 matching styles: #> [1] row: bold=TRUE #> [2] col: background="#EBF5FB" #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, color=#000000, background=#EBF5FB, align=left, #> valign=top, indent=0

title: Table Cookbook
text: Real programs don’t repeat settings every table. Define shared formatting top study program (_arframe.yml): Every table inherits settings automatically. Individual tables specify unique : data, titles, columns, footnotes, table-specific row logic. Principle: ’re writing verb two tables, belongs theme recipe — table program.
code: # ── Study-wide theme (set once, inherited by all tables) ── fr_theme(   font_size   = 9,   font_family = "Times New Roman",   orientation = "landscape",   hlines      = "header",   header      = list(bold = TRUE, align = "center"),   n_format    = "{label}\n(N={n})",   footnote_separator = FALSE,   pagehead    = list(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   pagefoot    = list(left = "{program}",                      right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") )

title: Table Cookbook
text: Define N-counts per population. Reuse vector every table shares population:
code: # ── Population N-counts (reusable across tables) ── n_itt    <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135) n_safety <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)

title: Table Cookbook
text: Note : fr_header(), fr_hlines(), fr_pagehead(), fr_pagefoot(), .n_format, .separator — inherited theme.
code: demog_spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     .width = "fit",     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     total          = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = n_itt   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group") |>   fr_footnotes(     "Percentages based on number of subjects per treatment group.",     "MMSE = Mini-Mental State Examination."   ) fr_validate(demog_spec) demog_spec |> fr_render("output/Table_14_1_1.rtf")

title: Table Cookbook
text: group variable names statistic values separate columns, group_label auto-injects group headers display column: label = "statistic" group_by list injects "Sex", "Age (years)", "Race" header rows statistic column. Detail rows automatically indented (inferred label). Style header rows via fr_styles() bold formatting.
code: # ── Sample data: long-form demographics ── demog_long <- data.frame(   variable = c(     "Sex", "Sex",     "Age (years)", "Age (years)", "Age (years)", "Age (years)",     "Race", "Race", "Race"   ),   statistic = c(     "Female", "Male",     "Mean (SD)", "Median", "Min, Max", "n",     "White", "Black or African American", "Asian"   ),   placebo = c(     "18 (40.0)", "27 (60.0)",     "67.8 (6.9)", "68.0", "52, 84", "45",     "38 (84.4)", "5 (11.1)", "2 (4.4)"   ),   zom_50mg = c(     "21 (46.7)", "24 (53.3)",     "68.2 (7.1)", "69.0", "54, 82", "45",     "36 (80.0)", "6 (13.3)", "3 (6.7)"   ),   zom_100mg = c(     "20 (44.4)", "25 (55.6)",     "68.0 (7.3)", "67.0", "53, 83", "45",     "37 (82.2)", "4 (8.9)", "4 (8.9)"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  demog_gl_spec <- demog_long |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.2",     "Demographics by Category (Long-Form Layout)",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     .width = "fit",     variable  = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     statistic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     .n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45)   ) |>   fr_rows(     group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "statistic")   ) |>   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on N per treatment arm.") fr_validate(demog_gl_spec)

title: Table Cookbook
code: disp_spec <- tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.4",     list("Subject Disposition", bold = TRUE),     "All Randomized Subjects"   ) |>   fr_cols(     category  = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     total     = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     .n = n_itt   ) |>   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on number of subjects randomized per arm.") fr_validate(disp_spec)

title: Table Cookbook
text: Multi-page table continuation text, SOC/PT indentation, content-based row styling: fr_page(continuation = "(continued)") added continuation text specific multi-page table.
code:
ae_soc_spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.3.1",     list("Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",          bold = TRUE),     "Safety Population"   ) |>   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)") |>   fr_cols(     soc       = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     pt        = fr_col("System Organ Class
  Preferred Term", width = 3.5),     row_type  = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane
50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane
100mg", align = "decimal"),     total     = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     .n = n_safety   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "soc", indent_by = "pt") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "total"), bold = TRUE),     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "soc"), bold = TRUE)   ) |>   fr_footnotes(     "MedDRA version 26.0.",     "Subjects counted once per SOC and Preferred Term.",     "Sorted by descending total incidence."   ) fr_validate(ae_soc_spec)

what: 14.3.2 AE by SOC / HLT / PT (Three-Level Hierarchy)
title: Table Cookbook
text: Multi-level indent_by key column drives indent depth: levels vector maps row_type values indent depth: SOC = 0 (flush left), HLT = 1 level, PT = 2 levels.
code: # ── Sample data: three-level AE hierarchy ── ae_3level <- data.frame(   soc = c(     rep("Gastrointestinal disorders", 5),     rep("Nervous system disorders", 4)   ),   term = c(     "Gastrointestinal disorders", "GI signs and symptoms",     "Nausea", "Vomiting", "Diarrhoea",     "Nervous system disorders", "Headaches",     "Headache", "Migraine"   ),   row_type = c(     "soc", "hlt", "pt", "pt", "pt",     "soc", "hlt", "pt", "pt"   ),   placebo = c(     "28 (62.2)", "20 (44.4)", "12 (26.7)", "5 (11.1)", "3 (6.7)",     "18 (40.0)", "14 (31.1)", "10 (22.2)", "4 (8.9)"   ),   zom_100mg = c(     "32 (71.1)", "24 (53.3)", "14 (31.1)", "6 (13.3)", "4 (8.9)",     "22 (48.9)", "16 (35.6)", "12 (26.7)", "4 (8.9)"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ae_3level_spec <- ae_3level |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.3.2",     "TEAEs by SOC, HLT, and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     soc      = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     term     = fr_col("SOC / HLT / Preferred Term", width = 3.5),     placebo  = fr_col("Placebo\n(N=45)", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg\n(N=45)", align = "decimal")   ) |>   fr_rows(     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = list(       key    = "row_type",       col    = "term",       levels = c(soc = 0, hlt = 1, pt = 2)     )   ) |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "soc"), bold = TRUE     )   ) |>   fr_footnotes(     "MedDRA version 26.0.",     "Subjects counted once per SOC, HLT, and Preferred Term."   ) fr_validate(ae_3level_spec) #> Warning: ! 1 validation issue found: #> • `indent_by` column not found in data: c(soc = 0, hlt = 1, pt = 2).

title: Table Cookbook
text: .n uses local vector column order differs tables (treatment arms exclude total).
code: tte_spec <- tbl_tte |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.2.1",     list("Time to Study Withdrawal", bold = TRUE),     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     section   = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     statistic = fr_col("", width = 3.5),     zom_50mg  = fr_col("Zomerane\n50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg = fr_col("Zomerane\n100mg", align = "decimal"),     placebo   = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     .n = c(zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, placebo = 45)   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "section", blank_after = "section") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("statistic", pattern = "^[A-Z]"),       bold = TRUE     )   ) |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Kaplan-Meier estimate with Greenwood 95% CI.",     "[b] Two-sided log-rank test stratified by age group.",     "[c] Cox proportional hazards model.",     "NE = Not Estimable."   ) fr_validate(tte_spec)

title: Table Cookbook
code: cm_spec <- tbl_cm |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.4.1",     list("Concomitant Medications by Category and Agent", bold = TRUE),     "Safety Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     category   = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     medication = fr_col("Medication Category / Agent", width = 3.0),     row_type   = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     placebo    = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg   = fr_col("Zomerane\n50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg  = fr_col("Zomerane\n100mg", align = "decimal"),     total      = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     .n = n_safety   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "category", indent_by = "medication") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "total"), bold = TRUE),     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", value = "category"), bold = TRUE)   ) |>   fr_footnotes("Subjects counted once per category and medication.") fr_validate(cm_spec)

title: Table Cookbook
text: Multi-parameter table spanning headers per-page N-counts: .n takes 3-column data frame (parameter + treatment + count) automatic per-page N-counts combined page_by.
code: # Pre-compute per-parameter N-counts from ADVS vs_n <- aggregate(   USUBJID ~ PARAM + TRTA, data = advs[advs$AVISIT == "Baseline", ],   FUN = function(x) length(unique(x)) )  vs_spec <- tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24", ] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.3.6",     "Vital Signs --- Week 24 Summary",     "Safety Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     param     = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     timepoint = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     statistic         = fr_col("Statistic", width = 1.2),     placebo_base      = fr_col("Baseline"),     placebo_value     = fr_col("Value"),     placebo_chg       = fr_col("CFB"),     zom_50mg_base     = fr_col("Baseline"),     zom_50mg_value    = fr_col("Value"),     zom_50mg_chg      = fr_col("CFB"),     zom_100mg_base    = fr_col("Baseline"),     zom_100mg_value   = fr_col("Value"),     zom_100mg_chg     = fr_col("CFB"),     .n = vs_n   ) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param") |>   fr_spans(     "Placebo"        = c("placebo_base", "placebo_value", "placebo_chg"),     "Zomerane 50mg"  = c("zom_50mg_base", "zom_50mg_value", "zom_50mg_chg"),     "Zomerane 100mg" = c("zom_100mg_base", "zom_100mg_value", "zom_100mg_chg")   ) |>   fr_footnotes("CFB = Change from Baseline.") fr_validate(vs_spec)

title: Table Cookbook
text: columns exceed page width, .split = TRUE creates panels:
code:
wide_spec <- tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24" &                      tbl_vs$param == "Systolic BP (mmHg)", ] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.6", "Systolic BP --- Column Split") |>   fr_cols(     param     = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     timepoint = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     statistic = fr_col("Statistic", width = 1.2, stub = TRUE),     placebo_base      = fr_col("Placebo
Baseline", width = 1.0),     placebo_value     = fr_col("Placebo
Value", width = 1.0),     placebo_chg       = fr_col("Placebo
CFB", width = 1.0),     zom_50mg_base     = fr_col("Zom 50mg
Baseline", width = 1.0),     zom_50mg_value    = fr_col("Zom 50mg
Value", width = 1.0),     zom_50mg_chg      = fr_col("Zom 50mg
CFB", width = 1.0),     zom_100mg_base    = fr_col("Zom 100mg
Baseline", width = 1.0),     zom_100mg_value   = fr_col("Zom 100mg
Value", width = 1.0),     zom_100mg_chg     = fr_col("Zom 100mg
CFB", width = 1.0),     .split = TRUE, .width = "fit"   ) fr_validate(wide_spec)

title: Titles & Footnotes
text: argument fr_titles() one title line, centered default: Calling fr_titles() replaces previous titles. SAS: TITLE1 "Table 14.1.1"; TITLE2 "Demographics...";
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) length(fr_get_titles(spec)) #> [1] 3

title: Titles & Footnotes
text: Pass named list control alignment bold per line: List fields: align ("left", "center", "right"), bold (logical), font_size (numeric).
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     list("Sponsor: Acme Pharma", align = "left"),     list("Protocol: TFRM-2024-001", align = "left"),     "Table 14.1.1",     list("Demographics and Baseline Characteristics", bold = TRUE),     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) length(fr_get_titles(spec)) #> [1] 5

title: Titles & Footnotes
text: .align .bold set defaults lines:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     .bold = TRUE, .align = "center"   ) fr_get_titles(spec)[[1]]$bold #> [1] TRUE

title: Titles & Footnotes
text: SAS: FOOTNOTE1 "Percentages...";
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "Percentages based on number of subjects per treatment group.",     "MMSE = Mini-Mental State Examination.",     .separator = FALSE   ) length(fr_get_footnotes(spec)) #> [1] 2

title: Titles & Footnotes
text: Use {fr_*()} expressions inside text string — titles, footnotes, column labels, data cells:
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "{fr_super('a')} Fisher's exact test.",     "{fr_super('b')} Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test.",     "BMI = Body Mass Index (kg/m{fr_super(2)})."   ) fr_get_footnotes(spec)[[3]]$content #> [1] "BMI = Body Mass Index (kg/m\001SUPER:2\002)."

title: Titles & Footnotes
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     placebo = fr_col("{fr_bold('Placebo')}")   )

title: Titles & Footnotes
text: Markup works anywhere string appears, including data : sentinel tokens survive paste(), sprintf(), glue() — resolved per-backend render time.
code: d <- data.frame(stat = "P{fr_super('a')}-value", val = "<0.001",                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE) spec <- d |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     stat = fr_col("Statistic", width = 2),     val  = fr_col("Result", width = 1.5)   )
