what: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
text: Regulatory-grade clinical tables, listings, figures RTF, PDF, HTML single specification. arframe R package pharmaceutical submissions. describe output ; arframe renders RTF, PDF, HTML — change file extension, output adapts.
code: tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics") |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     total          = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)   ) |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_render("Table_14_1_1.rtf")  # Change to .pdf — same output, different format

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
text: pharmaverse excellent tools data derivation (admiral), analysis (Tplyr, rtables, tern), table decoration (docorator, r2rtf). arframe occupies different position: native rendering engine writes RTF control words directly (RTF 1.9.1 spec) generates LaTeX/tabularray natively, formats first-class outputs.

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("vthanik/arframe")

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
text: arframe ships synthetic CDISC ADaM datasets every example runs box.

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
code: library(arframe)  tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_render(tempfile(fileext = ".rtf"))

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
code: # Set shared formatting once — inherited by all tables fr_theme(   font_size   = 9,   font_family = "Times New Roman",   orientation = "landscape",   hlines      = "header",   header      = list(bold = TRUE, align = "center"),   n_format    = "{label}\n(N={n})",   pagehead    = list(left = "TFRM-2024-001", right = "CONFIDENTIAL"),   pagefoot    = list(left = "{program}",                      right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") )  n_itt <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Intent-to-Treat Population"   ) |>   fr_cols(     .width = "fit",     characteristic = fr_col("", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", align = "decimal"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", align = "decimal"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", align = "decimal"),     total          = fr_col("Total", align = "decimal"),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE),     .n = n_itt   ) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group") |>   fr_footnotes("Percentages based on number of subjects per treatment group.")  # Render both formats from the same spec fr_render(spec, tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")) fr_render(spec, tempfile(fileext = ".pdf"))

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
text: arframe writes output directly — intermediate gt huxtable layer: RTF: Writes RTF 1.9.1 control words directly. Cell-level formatting, decimal alignment, \trkeep group protection, R-side pagination. PDF: Generates tabularray LaTeX, compiles XeLaTeX. Falls back Adobe open-source fonts (Source Serif 4, Source Sans 3, Source Code Pro — SIL OFL) Linux/Docker without Microsoft fonts. Set ARFRAME_FONT_DIR directory .ttf/.otf files project-local fonts without system-wide installation. HTML: Self-contained document paper simulation (orientation-aware page dimensions, margins). Auto-preview RStudio/Positron viewer via print(). Inline rendering Rmd/Quarto via knit_print(). Font metrics: Real Adobe Font Metrics (AFM) 12 font variants — accurate column width estimation without rendering.
code: fr_spec → finalize_spec() → RTF backend  → .rtf file                             ├→ LaTeX backend → .tex → XeLaTeX → .pdf                             └→ HTML backend  → .html file

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
text: Get Started — first table production-ready output Columns & Headers — Widths, alignment, N-counts, decimal alignment Titles & Footnotes — Titles, footnotes, inline markup Rows & Page Layout — Grouping, pagination, page chrome Rules, Spans & Styles — Borders, spanning headers, conditional formatting Table Cookbook — 8 complete ICH E3 table programs Listings & Figures — Patient listings embedded figures Automation & Batch — Themes, recipes, YAML config, batch rendering Architecture — Internal design backend interface

title: arframe — A Regulatory Frame for TFLs
text: arframe designed complement pharmaverse ecosystem: admiral — ADaM dataset creation Tplyr / rtables — Analysis table summarization (upstream arframe) r2rtf — RTF-focused table rendering docorator — gt-based document decoration

title: Adverse Events Analysis Dataset (ADAE) — adae
text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM Adverse Events (ADAE) dataset study TFRM-2024-001. Contains one row per subject per adverse event. Used : AE SOC/PT table (Table 14.3.1): incidence body system preferred term, within treatment arm Overall AE summary: subjects TEAEs seriousness, relatedness, severity, action taken adverse events dataset treatment-emergent (TRTEMFL = "Y"). Gastrointestinal nervous system events dose-related, reflecting cholinergic class effect Zomerane.

title: Adverse Events Analysis Dataset (ADAE) — adae
code: adae

title: Adverse Events Analysis Dataset (ADAE) — adae
text: data frame approximately 750 rows 27 variables: STUDYID Study identifier USUBJID Unique subject identifier ARM Planned treatment arm TRTA Actual treatment arm TRTAN Actual treatment numeric code AGE Subject age time event SEX Subject sex RACE Subject race SAFFL Safety population flag AEBODSYS MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC), e.g. "Gastrointestinal disorders", "Nervous system disorders" AEDECOD MedDRA Preferred Term (PT), e.g. "Nausea", "Dizziness" AESEV Severity: "MILD", "MODERATE", "SEVERE" AETOXGR CTCAE toxicity grade: "1", "2", "3", "4". Derived AESEV: MILD maps 1–2, MODERATE 2–3, SEVERE 3–4 AESER Serious adverse event flag: "Y" "N" AEREL Relationship study drug: "PROBABLE", "POSSIBLE", "REMOTE", "NONE" AEACN Action taken study treatment: "DOSE CHANGED", "DRUG INTERRUPTED", "DRUG WITHDRAWN", "DOSE REDUCED", "APPLICABLE" AEOUT Outcome: "RECOVERED/RESOLVED" "RECOVERED/RESOLVED" ASTDT AE start date AENDT AE end date ASTDY AE start day relative treatment start AENDY AE end day relative treatment start ADURN AE duration days TRTEMFL Treatment-emergent flag ("Y" records) AESEQ AE sequence number within subject AOCCFL First-occurrence flag (AE, per subject): "Y" NA AOCCSFL First SOC occurrence flag (per subject per SOC): "Y" NA AOCCPFL First PT occurrence flag (per subject per PT): "Y" NA

title: Concomitant Medications Analysis Dataset (ADCM) — adcm
text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM Concomitant Medications (ADCM) dataset study TFRM-2024-001. Contains one row per subject per concomitant medication. Used : Concomitant medications table (Table 14.4.1): subjects using medication category individual agent, treatment arm Medications reflect realistic co-morbidities elderly Alzheimer's population: hypertension, diabetes, pain, GI protection, supplements common. Medication use independent treatment assignment.

title: Concomitant Medications Analysis Dataset (ADCM) — adcm
code: adcm

title: Concomitant Medications Analysis Dataset (ADCM) — adcm
text: data frame approximately 600 rows 13 variables: STUDYID Study identifier USUBJID Unique subject identifier ARM Planned treatment arm TRTA Actual treatment arm TRTAN Actual treatment numeric code AGE Subject age SEX Subject sex SAFFL Safety population flag CMDECOD Decoded medication name (Drug Dictionary-style, uppercase), e.g. "PARACETAMOL", "ATORVASTATIN", "VITAMIN D" CMCAT ATC-inspired medication category, e.g. "ANALGESICS", "ANTIHYPERTENSIVES", "LIPID MODIFYING AGENTS", "SUPPLEMENTS" CMSTDT Concomitant medication start date CMENDT Concomitant medication end date (NA ongoing data cut) ONGOING Logical; TRUE medication ongoing data cut

title: Subject Level Analysis Dataset (ADSL) — adsl
text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM Subject Level (ADSL) dataset fictional study TFRM-2024-001, 24-week randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial Zomerane (fictional anti-cholinesterase inhibitor) mild--moderate Alzheimer's Disease. Contains one row per subject (N = 135). Used : Demographics table (Table 14.1.1): age, sex, race, BMI treatment arm Disposition table (Table 14.1.3): completion, discontinuation reasons Variable names follow CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide v1.3.

what: Time to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE) — adtte
title: Time to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE) — adtte
text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM Time--Event (ADTTE) dataset study TFRM-2024-001. Contains one row per subject per time--event parameter. Used : Time event table (Table 14.2.1): Kaplan-Meier estimates, median time--event, hazard ratios treatment arm Two parameters included: "TTWD" — Time Study Withdrawal (event = discontinued, censored = completed) "TTAE" — Time First Adverse Event (event = first AE, censored = AE)

title: Time to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE) — adtte
code: adtte

title: Time to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE) — adtte
text: data frame 270 rows (135 subjects x 2 parameters) 16 variables: STUDYID Study identifier USUBJID Unique subject identifier ARM Planned treatment arm TRTA Actual treatment arm TRTAN Actual treatment numeric code AGE Subject age SEX Subject sex SAFFL Safety population flag ITTFL Intent--treat population flag PARAMCD Parameter code: "TTWD" "TTAE" PARAM Parameter label: "Time Study Withdrawal (Days)" "Time First Adverse Event (Days)" AVAL Analysis value — time days treatment start event censoring CNSR Censoring indicator: 0 = event occurred, 1 = censored STARTDT Treatment start date (used reference/origin date) ADT Date event censoring ADY Relative day event censoring

title: Time to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE) — adtte
text: Synthetic data generated data-raw/create_adam_datasets.R.

title: Time to Event Analysis Dataset (ADTTE) — adtte
code: # Subset to one parameter ttwd <- subset(adtte, PARAMCD == "TTWD") table(ttwd$ARM, ttwd$CNSR)  # 0 = withdrew, 1 = completed #>                  #>                   0  1 #>   Placebo         1 44 #>   Zomerane 100mg  8 37 #>   Zomerane 50mg   4 41  # Median time to first AE by arm (unadjusted) ttae <- subset(adtte, PARAMCD == "TTAE") tapply(ttae$AVAL, ttae$ARM, median) #>        Placebo Zomerane 100mg  Zomerane 50mg  #>             24             13             16

title: Vital Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS) — advs
text: Synthetic record-level vital signs dataset study TFRM-2024-001. Contains one row per subject per parameter per visit. subjects missing parameters create natural N variation across vital sign groups — exactly scenario per-group N-count headers address. Designed source fr_cols() .n data frame parameter display table (tbl_vs) pre-summarized.

title: Vital Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS) — advs
code: advs

title: Vital Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS) — advs
text: data frame approximately 1900 rows 12 variables: STUDYID Study identifier ("TFRM-2024-001") USUBJID Unique subject identifier TRTA Actual treatment arm: "Placebo", "Zomerane 50mg", "Zomerane 100mg" TRTAN Actual treatment numeric code: 0, 50, 100 SAFFL Safety population flag ("Y") PARAM Vital sign parameter: "Systolic BP (mmHg)", "Diastolic BP (mmHg)", "Heart Rate (bpm)", "Weight (kg)", "Temperature (C)" PARAMCD Parameter code: "SYSBP", "DIABP", "HR", "WEIGHT", "TEMP" AVISIT Analysis visit: "Baseline", "Week 12", "Week 24" AVAL Analysis value (measured vital sign) BASE Baseline value (copy AVAL Baseline visit) CHG Change baseline (NA baseline) ABLFL Baseline record flag: "Y" Baseline, NA otherwise

title: Vital Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS) — advs
text: Synthetic data generated data-raw/create_adam_datasets.R using subjects adsl.

title: Vital Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS) — advs
text: subjects every parameter measured. Availability rates decrease dose parameter complexity — Temperature missing data, Blood Pressure least. produces realistic per-group N differences:

title: Vital Signs Analysis Dataset (ADVS) — advs
code: # Subjects per parameter per arm with(   advs[advs$AVISIT == "Baseline", ],   tapply(USUBJID, list(PARAM, TRTA), function(x) length(unique(x))) ) #>                     Placebo Zomerane 100mg Zomerane 50mg #> Diastolic BP (mmHg)      45             43            45 #> Heart Rate (bpm)         40             38            42 #> Systolic BP (mmHg)       45             43            41 #> Temperature (C)          38             36            42 #> Weight (kg)              43             42            41  # Use as N-count source via data frame vs_n <- aggregate(   USUBJID ~ PARAM + TRTA, data = advs[advs$AVISIT == "Baseline", ],   FUN = function(x) length(unique(x)) ) names(vs_n) <- c("param", "trt", "n") tbl_vs |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param") |>   fr_cols(.n = vs_n, .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 60 rows x 12 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (12 visible of 12): #> param "param" 1.16in left #> timepoint "timepoint" 0.58in left #> statistic "statistic" 0.62in left #> placebo_base "placebo_base" 0.77in left #> placebo_value "placebo_value" 0.82in left #> placebo_chg "placebo_chg" 0.72in left #> zom_50mg_base "zom_50mg_base" 0.94in left #> zom_50mg_value "zom_50mg_value" 0.99in left #> ... and 4 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=param #> Rules: 1 hline(s)

what: arframe: A Regulatory Frame for TFLs — arframe-package
title: arframe: A Regulatory Frame for TFLs — arframe-package
text: Submission-ready tables, listings, figures RTF, PDF, HTML single specification. describe output ; arframe renders — change file extension, output adapts. Designed pharmaceutical submissions paginated output, spanning headers, decimal alignment, programmatic page headers/footers, company-specific rule presets. Built disciplined S3 classes clean, composable pipeline API. dplyr, tidyr, purrr dependency — lightweight infrastructure packages (rlang, cli, vctrs).

title: arframe: A Regulatory Frame for TFLs — arframe-package
code: # Study setup (once per program, or use _arframe.yml) fr_theme(   font_size = 9, orientation = "landscape",   hlines = "header", header = list(bold = TRUE),   n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})",   pagehead = list(left = "TFRM-2024-001",                   right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") ) n_itt <- c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, total = 135)  # Per-table: only what's unique tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics") |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total", width = 1.5, align = "right"),     .n = n_itt   ) |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL") |>   fr_render("output/t_14_1_1.rtf")

title: arframe: A Regulatory Frame for TFLs — arframe-package
text: fr_ prefix avoids base R clashes (col, text, sub, page) Glue-string markup titles, footnotes, column labels, cell data: "{fr_super(1)} chi-square test" Titles/footnotes always body: page header/footer via fr_pagehead() / fr_pagefoot() {token} placeholders page headers/footers: {thepage}, {total_pages}, {program}, {datetime}, plus custom tokens. Literal brace: {{ produces { output. trimming leading whitespace — pharma indentation preserved Immutable pipeline: every verb returns modified spec, side effects fr_render()

title: arframe: A Regulatory Frame for TFLs — arframe-package
text: Maintainer: Vignesh Thanikachalam .vignesh@gmail.com

title: Combine Recipes — c.fr_recipe
text: Concatenates two fr_recipe objects single recipe. Later recipes override earlier ones verbs replace (verbs), append verbs accumulate (fr_spans(), fr_styles()).

title: Combine Recipes — c.fr_recipe
code: # S3 method for class 'fr_recipe' c(...)

title: Combine Recipes — c.fr_recipe
text: ... fr_recipe objects combine.

title: Combine Recipes — c.fr_recipe
text: combined fr_recipe object.

title: Combine Recipes — c.fr_recipe
code: # Compose two recipes base_style <- fr_recipe(fr_page(orientation = "landscape")) ae_style <- fr_recipe(fr_hlines("box")) combined <- c(base_style, ae_style) combined #>  #> ── fr_recipe (2 verbs)  #> 1. fr_page(orientation="landscape") #> 2. fr_hlines("box")  # Compose and apply in one pipeline layout <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(left = "{program}") ) rules <- fr_recipe(   fr_hlines("header"),   fr_header(bold = TRUE) ) full_style <- c(layout, rules) full_style   # 4 verbs #>  #> ── fr_recipe (4 verbs)  #> 1. fr_page(orientation="landscape", font_size=9) #> 2. fr_pagehead(left="{program}") #> 3. fr_hlines("header") #> 4. fr_header(bold=TRUE)  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "composed.rtf") tbl_tte |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(full_style) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.2.1", "Time-to-Event Analysis") |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)

text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). recipe fr_recipe object fr_recipe().

title: Apply a Recipe to a Spec — fr_apply
text: modified fr_spec.

title: Apply a Recipe to a Spec — fr_apply
code: # Basic application style <- fr_recipe(fr_hlines("header"), fr_header(bold = TRUE)) spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_apply(style)  # Apply a recipe and then override one parameter with a direct verb call spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(style) |>   fr_header(bold = FALSE)   # override the recipe's bold = TRUE  # Apply to different datasets with the same recipe demog_spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_apply(style) disp_spec  <- tbl_disp  |> fr_table() |> fr_apply(style)

title: List Registered Render Backends — fr_backends

title: List Registered Render Backends — fr_backends

title: List Registered Render Backends — fr_backends
code: # List built-in backends fr_backends() #>   format extensions                   description #> 1    rtf   rtf, doc              Rich Text Format #> 2  latex        tex     LaTeX source (tabularray) #> 3    pdf        pdf               PDF via XeLaTeX #> 4   html  html, htm HTML preview (self-contained)  # Register a custom backend, then view updated list fr_register_backend(   format = "csv",   extensions = "csv",   render_fn = function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) {     utils::write.csv(spec$data, path, row.names = FALSE)   },   description = "CSV export (data only)" ) fr_backends() #>   format extensions                   description #> 1    rtf   rtf, doc              Rich Text Format #> 2  latex        tex     LaTeX source (tabularray) #> 3    pdf        pdf               PDF via XeLaTeX #> 4   html  html, htm HTML preview (self-contained) #> 5    csv        csv        CSV export (data only) fr_unregister_backend("csv")

title: Bold Markup — fr_bold
text: Renders text span bold final output. Use inside glue-string expressions apply bold formatting portion title, footnote, column label, cell value — without bolding entire line. use fr_bold() vs bold = TRUE: fr_bold("text") — inline markup partial bold within string. fr_row_style(bold = TRUE) — bold entire row uniformly. fr_header(bold = TRUE) — bold column headers.

title: Bold Markup — fr_bold
code: fr_bold(x)

title: Bold Markup — fr_bold
text: x Character. Text render bold.

title: Bold Markup — fr_bold
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string, produces sentinel token resolved render time.

title: Bold Markup — fr_bold
text: RTF output, bold uses \b ... \b0 control words. future LaTeX output, maps \textbf{}. bold weight matches surrounding font family.

title: Bold Markup — fr_bold
code: # Bold a label keyword fr_col("{fr_bold('Total')} Subjects", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "{fr_bold('Total')} Subjects" [1.50in, left]  # Bold just the table number in a title: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_titles("{fr_bold('Table 14.1.1')} Summary of Demographics") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: Defines display properties single table column: label, width, alignment, visibility, N-count, spanning group. Used inside fr_cols() named argument configure individual columns. Columns explicitly configured receive auto-generated defaults .width, .align, .label_fn arguments fr_cols(). fr_col() single source truth column structure. Labels, widths, alignment, N-counts, spanning groups defined . fr_header() purely header presentation (bold, colours, font).

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
code: fr_col(   label = "",   width = NULL,   align = NULL,   header_align = NULL,   visible = NULL,   stub = FALSE,   space_mode = NULL,   n = NULL,   group = NULL )

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: label Character scalar. Display label shown column header. Supports {fr_*()} inline markup (e.g. "{fr_super('')}" superscript footnote markers). Default "" inherits data frame column name label, optionally transformed .label_fn argument fr_cols(). width Column width. Accepts: Numeric — fixed width inches (e.g. 2.5). Use stub columns need exact control. "auto" — auto-calculate content header widths using page font metrics. layout engine measures widest cell value header label, adds padding, converts inches. Columns proportionally scaled fit printable page width. fastest way get working table. Percentage string — e.g. "20%". Sets column width fraction printable page width. Resolved absolute inches render time. Must "0%" (exclusive) "100%" (inclusive). Useful responsive layouts adapt different page sizes orientations. NULL (default) — inherits .width argument fr_cols(). .width also NULL, package default 1.5 used. align Column alignment. One : "left" — left-aligned. Standard text stub columns. "center" — centered. Rare regulatory tables; used binary indicators (Yes/) short categorical values. "right" — right-aligned. Standard numeric columns (counts, percentages, p-values). "decimal" — decimal-point alignment. Aligns decimal point (last digit integers) across rows column. Standard continuous summary statistics (mean, SD, median). NULL (default) — auto-detects R column type: numeric/integer → "right", everything else → "left". header_align Horizontal alignment column header cell. One "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL (default). NULL, header inherits alignment align. Use center treatment-arm headers keeping body cells right-aligned. Can also set uniformly via fr_header(align = ...); per-column values take priority. fr_style(region = "header", align = ...) overrides . visible Logical NULL. Controls whether column appears rendered output. NULL (default): system decides — columns used fr_rows() page_by keys auto-hidden; others visible. TRUE: force column visible, even page_by key. FALSE: hide column. Useful structural columns (grouping keys, sort-order columns, row-type flags) remain data pagination styling logic appear output. stub Logical. Whether column stub column — repeated every panel fr_cols(.split = TRUE) splits table across multiple column pages. Default FALSE. Stub columns typically row-label parameter columns provide context panel. .split enabled columns stub = TRUE, stubs auto-inferred group_by/indent_by columns first column. space_mode handle leading spaces cell data. One : "indent" — convert leading spaces paragraph-level indent (RTF \li, LaTeX \leftskip). lines (including wrapped) maintain indent. correct approach proportional fonts pharma SOC/PT hierarchies. "preserve" — keep leading spaces literal characters. Use pre-formatted content exact spacing must retained. NULL (default) — inherits .space_mode argument fr_cols(), defaults "indent". n Per-column subject count. non-negative integer scalar (e.g. n = 45). Formatted column label render time using .n_format template fr_cols(). Takes highest priority N-count resolution — overrides bulk .n fr_cols(). Use common case know column's N definition time. NULL (default) means per-column N. group Character scalar. Assigns column spanning header group. columns sharing group value get auto-generated span level 1, ordered first column appearance. replaces fr_spans() 90\ structure grouping one place. Rules: Single-column groups create single-column sub-headers. Explicit fr_spans() label overrides auto-span. fr_spans() .level = 2L adds higher-level spans . NULL (default) means spanning group.

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: S3 object class fr_col components id, label, width, align, header_align, visible, stub, space_mode, n, group.

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: Landscape Letter paper (11 × 8.5 ) 1 margins gives 9 printable width. common pharma layout: Use width = "auto" let layout engine calculate content, set fixed widths exact control.

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: Standard pharma house styles (Roche, Novartis, Pfizer TFL guides): Stub / parameter column: left-aligned. Count percentage columns: right-aligned decimal-aligned. P-value columns: right-aligned decimal-aligned. Category columns (Yes/, Male/Female): centered left-aligned.

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: multiple N-count sources present, highest-priority source wins (double-apply):

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: group parameter provides inline spanning — separate fr_spans() call needed. Columns group value grouped single spanning header:
code: fr_cols(   stat      = fr_col("Statistic", width = 1.5),   pbo_base  = fr_col("Baseline", group = "Placebo"),   pbo_val   = fr_col("Value",    group = "Placebo"),   drg_base  = fr_col("Baseline", group = "Zomerane"),   drg_val   = fr_col("Value",    group = "Zomerane") )

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
text: Body alignment: fr_cols(.align=) < fr_col(align=) < fr_col_style(align=) < fr_style(align=) Header alignment: fr_header(align=) < fr_col(header_align=) < fr_style(region="header", align=)

title: Define a Column Specification — fr_col
code: ## ── Fixed width with explicit alignment ──────────────────────────────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = 2.5, align = "left") #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [2.50in, left]  ## ── Percentage width: 25% of printable area ──────────────────────────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = "25%") #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [25%, left]  ## ── Centered header over right-aligned body ──────────────────────────────  fr_col("Zomerane 50mg", width = 1.5, align = "right", header_align = "center") #> <fr_col> "Zomerane 50mg" [1.50in, right]  ## ── Per-column N-count ───────────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_col("Placebo", n = 45) #> <fr_col> "Placebo" [auto, left N=45]  ## ── N-count + spanning group ─────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_col("Baseline", group = "Placebo") #> <fr_col> "Baseline" [auto, left group=Placebo]  ## ── Percentage width in a pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = "30%"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg",  width = "17.5%", align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg", width = "17.5%", align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",         width = "17.5%", align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",           width = "17.5%", align = "right")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 30% left #> placebo "Placebo" 18% right #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50mg" 18% right #> zom_100mg "Zomerane 100mg" 18% right #> total "Total" 18% right #> group "group" 0.62in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Auto-width: let the engine measure content ───────────────────────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = "auto") #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [auto, left]  ## ── Markup in label: superscript unit ────────────────────────────────────  fr_col("BMI (kg/m{fr_super('2')})", width = 1.5, align = "decimal") #> <fr_col> "BMI (kg/m{fr_super('2')})" [1.50in, decimal]  ## ── Decimal alignment for continuous statistics ──────────────────────────  fr_col("Mean (SD)", width = 1.5, align = "decimal") #> <fr_col> "Mean (SD)" [1.50in, decimal]  ## ── Hidden structural column (grouping key for fr_rows) ─────────────────  fr_col(visible = FALSE) #> <fr_col> "" [auto, left]  ## ── Stub column (repeats in every panel during column splitting) ────────  fr_col("Parameter", width = 2.5, stub = TRUE) #> <fr_col> "Parameter" [2.50in, left stub]  ## ── Spanning group: columns grouped under one header ─────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",  group = "Zomerane"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg", group = "Zomerane"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo"),     total          = fr_col("Total")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo" 0.61in left #> zom_50mg "50 mg" 0.61in left #> zom_100mg "100 mg" 0.61in left #> total "Total" 0.61in left #> group "group" 0.62in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Per-column N-counts in a pipeline ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50 mg", n = 45),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",         n = 45),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo" 0.61in left #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50 mg" 0.97in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.70in left #> total "total" 0.61in left #> group "group" 0.62in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Space mode: preserve leading spaces as literal characters ──────────  fr_col("Statistic", space_mode = "preserve") #> <fr_col> "Statistic" [auto, left]  ## ── Space mode: explicit indent mode (default, but can be stated) ─────  fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5, space_mode = "indent") #> <fr_col> "Characteristic" [2.50in, left]

what: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: Creates column-level style object use fr_styles(). Column styles apply uniformly body cells targeted columns.

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
code: fr_col_style(   cols = NULL,   bold = NULL,   italic = NULL,   underline = NULL,   color = NULL,   background = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL )

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: cols Column selection. Accepts : character vector column names: cols = c("zom_50mg", "placebo") tidyselect expression: cols = starts_with("zom_") NULL (default) — targets columns. Tidyselect expressions captured unevaluated resolved style applied via fr_styles(). Supported helpers: tidyselect::starts_with(), tidyselect::ends_with(), tidyselect::contains(), tidyselect::matches(), tidyselect::everything(), tidyselect::(), . bold, italic, underline Logical NULL inherit. color Foreground (text) colour, NULL. background Background (fill) colour, NULL. font_size Font size points, NULL. align Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model. valign Vertical alignment: "top", "middle", "bottom", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model.

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: fr_cell_style object type = "col" use fr_styles().

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: Column styles broadest scope overridden row styles cell styles:   Column alignment set via fr_col(align = ...) fr_cols() base default. fr_col_style(align = ...) overrides body cells. fr_row_style(align = ...) overrides . Finally, fr_style(region = "body", align = ...) targeting specific cells wins.
code: fr_col_style  <  fr_row_style  <  fr_style (cell)

title: Define a Column Style Override — fr_col_style
text: Column alignment usually set fr_cols() via fr_col(align = ...). Use fr_col_style() need override alignment subset columns without reconfiguring full column spec. Use fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB") give Total column distinct background — common regulatory convention. Column styles apply body cells. style header cells, use fr_style(region = "header", cols = ...) fr_header().

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Configures display label, width, alignment, visibility, N-counts, spanning groups table columns. fr_cols() single source truth column structure; fr_header() owns header presentation (bold, colours, font size). Columns explicitly named receive auto-generated defaults: column name label (optionally transformed .label_fn), alignment inferred R column type (numeric → "right", everything else → "left"), width .width. Calling fr_cols() replaces entire column configuration.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
code: fr_cols(   spec,   ...,   .list = NULL,   .width = NULL,   .align = NULL,   .label_fn = NULL,   .space_mode = NULL,   .split = NULL,   .n = NULL,   .n_format = NULL )

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... Column specifications. Can provided two ways: Named arguments: argument name must match column name data frame. value can fr_col() object character scalar (label). Formulas (tidyselect): Use lhs ~ rhs lhs set columns selected via tidyselect::language (e.g., starts_with("col")), rhs fr_col() object character scalar applied selected columns. Powered tidyselect package. data column listed receives auto-generated defaults. .list Named list character vector pre-built labels. Allows construct column labels externally (e.g., using sprintf()) pass programmatically. Example: list(zom_50mg = "Zomerane 50 mg"). .width Default column width applied columns explicit width set. Accepts: Numeric — width inches (e.g. 1.5). Default NULL. "auto" — auto-calculate widths content header text using page font metrics. layout engine measures widest cell value header label column, adds padding, scales total exceeds printable page width. Columns already fit left -. "fit" — like "auto", always scales () total width exactly fills printable page width, preserving content-based ratio columns. Use want every table span full page width without manual tuning. "equal" — distribute printable page width equally among columns fixed width. Columns explicit numeric width fr_col() keep size; remaining space divided equally. Percentage string — e.g. "25%". Sets every column's default width fraction printable page width. Must "0%" (exclusive) "100%" (inclusive). Columns explicit width fr_col() affected. NULL (default) — "auto". Columns auto-size content header text. .align Default alignment applied columns explicit alignment set. One "left", "center", "right", "decimal". NULL auto-detects: "right" numeric columns, "left" everything else. .label_fn function (rlang-style lambda) applied auto-generated column labels. affects columns whose labels explicitly set via ... .list. Receives column name input, returns display label. Common transforms: ~ gsub("_", " ", .x) — replace underscores spaces. ~ tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", .x)) — title case. toupper — caps. .space_mode handle leading spaces cell data columns explicit space_mode set fr_col(). One : "indent" (default) — convert leading spaces paragraph-level indent. indent width measured page font metrics, renders correctly proportional monospace fonts. "preserve" — keep leading spaces literal characters. NULL — "indent". .split Logical NULL. Column splitting wide tables exceed printable page width: NULL (default) — splitting. columns one page. TRUE — split across multiple panels, stub columns repeated panel. Panel width behaviour follows .width: .width = "auto" → panels keep natural column widths. .width = "fit" → panels scale fill page width. .width = "equal" → unfixed columns share remaining space equally. FALSE — explicitly splitting. Stub columns (repeated every panel) designated via fr_col(stub = TRUE). .split enabled columns stub = TRUE, stubs auto-inferred group_by/indent_by columns first column. .n Bulk N-counts applied across columns spanning groups. Names matched case-insensitively using two-step lookup: first column display label, data column name fallback. means can use either form: c("Placebo" = 45) — matches label c(placebo = 45) — matches column name (label repetition) Accepts: Named numeric vector — names = display labels column names, matched case-insensitively column labels first, column names. Also matches spanning group names. Example: c(placebo = 45, zom_50mg = 44). Data frame (2-column) — column 1 = display labels, column 2 = counts. N every page. Example: data.frame(trt = c("Placebo", "Zom 50mg"), n = c(45, 44)). Data frame (3-column) — column 1 = page_by group values, column 2 = display labels, column 3 = counts. Different N per page_by group. Named list — keys = page_by group values, values = named numeric vectors (names = display labels). Example: list("Systolic BP" = c("Placebo" = 42, "Zom" = 40)). Auto-routing: label matches group name (spanning header), N goes span. matches column label, N goes column. Group matches take priority (double-apply). Per-column fr_col(n = ...) always takes highest priority. .n_format glue-style format string N-count labels. Available tokens: {label} (column display label) {n} (count). Default NULL inherits config (default: "{label}\\n(N={n})"). Override project level via columns.n_format _arframe.yml, session level via fr_theme(n_format = ...), per-table .

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: modified fr_spec. Column specs stored spec$columns, split settings spec$columns_meta.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Standard pharma house styles FDA/EMA submissions require: Active treatment arm(s) left, order dose escalation (lowest highest dose, left right). Comparator / placebo arm(s) rightmost among study arm columns. Total column (included): optional; covers active arms (pool active + placebo). Placed immediately rightmost active arm column, placebo column. Example column order 2-dose + placebo study:

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Column labels resolved priority (highest wins): N-count formatting (fr_col(n=) .n + .n_format) — dynamic label N counts, applied render time. Explicit fr_col(label = ...) ... arguments. .list — programmatic label map. .label_fn — transform function applied column name. Column name — raw data frame column name -.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Column order rendered table matches data frame column order, order list fr_cols(). reorder columns, reorder data frame first. Use visible = FALSE suppress column rendering keeping available grouping key fr_rows(). align = "decimal" aligns decimal points numeric columns. Width inches. Landscape Letter 1 margins gives 9 printable width. common pharma layout: stub column 2.5 + 4–5 data columns 1.3–1.5 . .width = "auto" fastest way get working table — layout engine calculates widths content. Use fixed widths need exact control. .label_fn transforms auto-generated labels (column names explicitly relabelled). lets set baseline transform override specific columns needed.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Configure Column Display — fr_cols

what: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: Loads study-level configuration _arframe.yml file. file path given, auto-discovers nearest _arframe.yml searching directory tree current working directory. loaded config stored internally automatically applied every subsequent fr_table() call. Config values lowest priority — overridden fr_theme() (session-level) per-table verbs like fr_page() fr_header(). arframe first pharma TFL package file-driven theming. Place single _arframe.yml project root every table study inherits fonts, margins, headers, footers, rules without R code.

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
code: fr_config(file = NULL)

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: file Character scalar NULL. Path _arframe.yml file. NULL (default) triggers auto-discovery: searches directory tree getwd() _arframe.yml found, falling back package-bundled defaults (inst/defaults/_arframe.yml).

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: Invisibly returns parsed config named list.

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: Settings resolved lowest highest priority:   Config _arframe.yml overrides package defaults. fr_theme() overrides config. Per-table verbs (e.g. fr_page(font_size = 10)) override everything.
code: Package defaults  < _arframe.yml  < fr_theme()  < per-table verbs

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
code: # _arframe.yml — place at project root page:   paper: letter   orientation: landscape   margins: [1.0, 0.75, 1.0, 0.75]   # top, right, bottom, left   font_family: "Times New Roman"   font_size: 9   col_gap: 4  columns:   split: false           # column splitting for wide tables   space_mode: indent     # indent | preserve — leading space handling  header:   align: ~               # inherit from column (user decides)   valign: bottom   bold: false  pagehead:   left: "{company}"   center: "{study_id}"   right: "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   font_size: 8  pagefoot:   left: "{program}"   right: "{datetime}"   font_size: 8  rules:   hlines: header         # header | booktabs | open | hsides | box | void   vlines: void           # box | all | inner | void  footnotes:   separator: true   font_size: 8  spacing:                 # blank lines between table sections   titles_after: 1        # after titles, before column header   footnotes_before: 1    # after body, before footnotes   pagehead_after: 0      # after page header, before titles   pagefoot_before: 0     # after footnotes, before page footer   page_by_after: 1       # after page_by label, before col headers  tokens:                  # custom token values for pagehead/pagefoot   company: "Pharma Corp"   study_id: "TFRM-2024-001"

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
text: file = NULL, fr_config() searches _arframe.yml starting current working directory walking parent directories. mirrors behaviour .Rprofile, _quarto.yml, _pkgdown.yml. typical project layout:
code: study-root/   _arframe.yml         <-- found here   R/     t_demog.R   output/     tbl_14_1_1.rtf

title: Load Configuration from a YAML File — fr_config
code: # Load the built-in package defaults config default_cfg <- system.file("defaults/_arframe.yml", package = "arframe") fr_config(default_cfg)  # Inspect what was loaded fr_config_get() #> $page #> $page$paper #> [1] "letter" #>  #> $page$orientation #> [1] "landscape" #>  #> $page$margins #> [1] 1.00 0.75 1.00 0.75 #>  #> $page$font_family #> [1] "Times New Roman" #>  #> $page$font_size #> [1] 9 #>  #> $page$continuation #> NULL #>  #> $page$col_gap #> [1] 4 #>  #>  #> $columns #> $columns$split #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $columns$space_mode #> [1] "indent" #>  #> $columns$n_format #> [1] "{label}\n(N={n})" #>  #>  #> $header #> $header$align #> NULL #>  #> $header$valign #> [1] "bottom" #>  #> $header$bold #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $header$span_gap #> [1] TRUE #>  #>  #> $pagehead #> $pagehead$left #> NULL #>  #> $pagehead$center #> NULL #>  #> $pagehead$right #> NULL #>  #> $pagehead$font_size #> NULL #>  #> $pagehead$bold #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> $pagefoot #> $pagefoot$left #> [1] "{program}" #>  #> $pagefoot$center #> NULL #>  #> $pagefoot$right #> [1] "{datetime}" #>  #> $pagefoot$font_size #> NULL #>  #> $pagefoot$bold #> [1] FALSE #>  #>  #> $rules #> $rules$hlines #> [1] "header" #>  #> $rules$vlines #> [1] "void" #>  #>  #> $footnotes #> $footnotes$separator #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $footnotes$font_size #> NULL #>  #> $footnotes$placement #> [1] "every" #>  #>  #> $spacing #> $spacing$titles_after #> [1] 1 #>  #> $spacing$footnotes_before #> [1] 1 #>  #> $spacing$pagehead_after #> [1] 0 #>  #> $spacing$pagefoot_before #> [1] 0 #>  #> $spacing$page_by_after #> [1] 1 #>  #>  #> $titles #> $titles$align #> [1] "center" #>  #> $titles$bold #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $titles$font_size #> NULL #>  #>  #> $tokens #> named list() #>   # Create a temporary custom config and load it yml <- file.path(tempdir(), "_arframe.yml") writeLines(c(   "page:",   "  orientation: landscape",   "  font_size: 8",   "  font_family: 'Times New Roman'",   "header:",   "  bold: true",   "tokens:",   "  company: 'Pharma Corp'" ), yml) fr_config(yml) fr_config_get()$page$font_size    # 8 #> [1] 8 fr_config_get()$tokens$company    # "Pharma Corp" #> [1] "Pharma Corp"  # Clean up fr_config_reset() unlink(yml)

title: Get the Current Configuration — fr_config_get
text: Returns current config named list. config loaded yet, auto-discovers loads nearest _arframe.yml.

title: Get the Current Configuration — fr_config_get
code: fr_config_get()

title: Get the Current Configuration — fr_config_get
text: named list config settings. Top-level keys mirror YAML structure: page, header, pagehead, pagefoot, rules, footnotes, spacing, tokens. Returns empty list config loaded _arframe.yml found.

title: Get the Current Configuration — fr_config_get
code: # Start clean — no config loaded fr_config_reset()  # Load config and inspect top-level keys default_cfg <- system.file("defaults/_arframe.yml", package = "arframe") fr_config(default_cfg) cfg <- fr_config_get() names(cfg)                    # page, header, pagehead, etc. #>  [1] "page"      "columns"   "header"    "pagehead"  "pagefoot"  "rules"     #>  [7] "footnotes" "spacing"   "titles"    "tokens"     # Access nested keys cfg$page$font_size            # font size from config #> [1] 9 cfg$page$orientation          # page orientation #> [1] "landscape" cfg$header$bold               # header bold setting #> [1] FALSE  # After reset, fr_config_get() auto-discovers defaults fr_config_reset()  # Load a custom config with specific tokens yml <- file.path(tempdir(), "_arframe.yml") writeLines(c(   "tokens:",   "  study_id: 'DEMO-001'" ), yml) fr_config(yml) fr_config_get()$tokens$study_id   # "DEMO-001" #> [1] "DEMO-001"  # Clean up fr_config_reset() unlink(yml)

title: Reset Configuration — fr_config_reset
text: Clears loaded YAML config. calling fr_config_reset(), fr_table() uses built-defaults (fr_theme() set). Call fr_config() reload.

title: Reset Configuration — fr_config_reset
code: fr_config_reset()

title: Reset Configuration — fr_config_reset
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Reset Configuration — fr_config_reset
code: # Load a config default_cfg <- system.file("defaults/_arframe.yml", package = "arframe") fr_config(default_cfg) fr_config_get()$page$orientation   # has a value #> [1] "landscape"  # Reset clears everything fr_config_reset()  # After reset and re-load, config is back to defaults fr_config(default_cfg) fr_config_get()$page$orientation #> [1] "landscape" fr_config_reset()                  # clean up

title: Dagger Symbol — fr_dagger
text: Inserts dagger symbol (U+2020). Shorthand fr_unicode(0x2020). regulatory tables dagger used mark treatment-related adverse events indicate secondary footnote series []/[b] series exhausted.

title: Dagger Symbol — fr_dagger
code: fr_dagger()

title: Dagger Symbol — fr_dagger
text: fr_markup object containing dagger character.

title: Dagger Symbol — fr_dagger
code: # Standalone dagger symbol fr_dagger() #> <fr_markup> †  # In a footnote: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes("{fr_dagger()} Treatment-related adverse event.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "† Treatment-related adverse event."  # Combined dagger and double dagger: spec |> fr_footnotes(   "{fr_dagger()} Treatment-related adverse event.",   "{fr_ddagger()} Serious adverse event." ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "† Treatment-related adverse event." #> 2. [left] "‡ Serious adverse event."

title: Double Dagger Symbol — fr_ddagger
text: Inserts double dagger symbol (U+2021). Shorthand fr_unicode(0x2021). regulatory tables double dagger marks serious adverse events acts secondary marker single dagger. See fr_dagger() full symbol convention table.

title: Double Dagger Symbol — fr_ddagger
code: fr_ddagger()

title: Double Dagger Symbol — fr_ddagger
text: fr_markup object containing double dagger character.

title: Double Dagger Symbol — fr_ddagger
code: # Standalone double dagger symbol fr_ddagger() #> <fr_markup> ‡  # In footnotes: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes(   "{fr_dagger()} Treatment-related adverse event.",   "{fr_ddagger()} Serious adverse event." ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "† Treatment-related adverse event." #> 2. [left] "‡ Serious adverse event."

title: Em Dash — fr_emdash
text: Inserts em dash (U+2014, "—"). Shorthand fr_unicode(0x2014). em dash used regulatory table titles footnotes separate clauses, e.g. "Safety Population — Randomized Subjects". Using fr_emdash() instead literal "—" character guarantees correct rendering RTF PDF output regardless file encoding.

title: Em Dash — fr_emdash
code: fr_emdash()

title: Em Dash — fr_emdash
text: fr_markup object containing em dash character.

title: Em Dash — fr_emdash
code: # In a title: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_titles("Safety Population {fr_emdash()} FAS") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Safety Population — FAS" #> Header: valign=bottom  # In a footnote: spec |> fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL {fr_emdash()} Safety Population") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Source: ADSL — Safety Population"

title: En Dash — fr_endash
text: Inserts en dash (U+2013, "–"). Shorthand fr_unicode(0x2013). en dash used regulatory tables numeric ranges (e.g. "18–65 years"), date ranges, confidence intervals. Using fr_endash() instead literal "–" character guarantees correct rendering RTF PDF output.

title: En Dash — fr_endash
code: fr_endash()

title: En Dash — fr_endash
text: fr_markup object containing en dash character.

title: En Dash — fr_endash
code: # Numeric range in a footnote: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes("[a] Age range: 18{fr_endash()}65 years.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Age range: 18–65 years."  # In a column label: fr_col("95% CI{fr_newline()}(Lower{fr_endash()}Upper)", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "95% CI{fr_newline()}(Lower{fr_endash()}Upper)" [1.50in, left]

what: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
text: Entry point figures — wraps plot object (ggplot2 base R) titling, footnoting, page chrome tables. plot embedded rendered output consistent regulatory formatting.

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
code: fr_figure(plot, width = NULL, height = NULL, meta = NULL)

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
text: plot plot object list plot objects. Supported types: ggplot object (ggplot2) recordedplot object (recordPlot()) list multi-page figures (one plot per page) width Numeric. Plot width inches within page. Default NULL uses full printable width. height Numeric. Plot height inches within page. Default NULL uses remaining height titles footnotes. meta data frame per-page metadata multi-page figures. Must exactly one row per plot. Column names become tokens available fr_titles() fr_footnotes() via {column_name} syntax, resolved dynamically page. Ignored single-plot figures.

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
text: fr_spec object type = "figure".

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
text: Figures support: fr_titles(), fr_footnotes(), fr_page(), fr_pagehead(), fr_pagefoot(), fr_spacing(), fr_render(). Column/header/row/style/span/rule verbs -ops figure specs.

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
text: PDF: Plot saved temporary PDF, included via \\includegraphics LaTeX titles/footnotes DeclareTblrTemplate. RTF: Plot saved temporary PNG, embedded via \\pict RTF picture group titles paragraphs.

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
text: Pass list plots optional meta data frame create multi-page figures. meta column becomes token titles footnotes, resolved per page:
code: plots <- list(p_adults, p_pediatrics, p_geriatrics) page_meta <- data.frame(subgroup = c("Adults", "Pediatrics", "Geriatrics"))  plots |>   fr_figure(meta = page_meta) |>   fr_titles("Figure 14.1.1 Kaplan-Meier Curve", "Subgroup: {subgroup}") |>   fr_render("km_multi.rtf")

title: Start a arframe Figure Pipeline — fr_figure
code: # fr_figure() requires a ggplot or recordedplot object if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(adtte, ggplot2::aes(x = AVAL, y = TRTA)) +     ggplot2::geom_point()    spec <- p |>     fr_figure() |>     fr_titles("Figure 14.2.1 Time-to-Event") |>     fr_footnotes("Source: ADTTE") |>     fr_page(orientation = "landscape")   spec } #>  #> ── fr_spec: Figure  #> Plot: 1 page #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Figure 14.2.1 Time-to-Event" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Source: ADTTE" #> Plot: ggplot2::ggplot  ## ── Explicit width and height ───────────────────────────────────────────── if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(adsl, ggplot2::aes(x = AGE, fill = TRT01A)) +     ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "dodge") +     ggplot2::labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Count", fill = "Treatment")    spec <- p |>     fr_figure(width = 7, height = 4.5) |>     fr_titles(       "Figure 14.1.2 Distribution of Age by Treatment Arm",       "Full Analysis Set"     ) |>     fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL") |>     fr_page(orientation = "landscape")   spec } #>  #> ── fr_spec: Figure  #> Plot: 1 page #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Figure 14.1.2 Distribution of Age by Treatment Arm" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Source: ADSL" #> Plot: ggplot2::ggplot  ## ── Base R plot with recordPlot() ───────────────────────────────────────── # Capture a base R plot as a recordedplot object. # Use a temporary PDF device to avoid creating Rplots.pdf. tmp_pdf <- tempfile(fileext = ".pdf") pdf(tmp_pdf) old_par <- par(mar = c(5, 4, 2, 1)) plot(adsl$AGE, adsl$BMIBL,      xlab = "Age (years)", ylab = "BMI (kg/m2)",      pch = 19, col = "#4472C4", cex = 0.7,      main = "") p_base <- recordPlot() par(old_par) dev.off() #> agg_record_6801ccd7c9dd  #>                       2  unlink(tmp_pdf)  spec <- p_base |>   fr_figure(width = 6, height = 4) |>   fr_titles("Figure 14.1.3 Age vs BMI at Baseline") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL") spec #>  #> ── fr_spec: Figure  #> Plot: 1 page #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Figure 14.1.3 Age vs BMI at Baseline" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Source: ADSL" #> Plot: recordedplot

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: Sets one footnote lines displayed table body. argument ... produces one footnote line. Calling fr_footnotes() replaces previously set footnotes. Footnotes support inline {fr_*()} markup, including fr_super() superscript footnote labels.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
code: fr_footnotes(   spec,   ...,   .align = "left",   .placement = "every",   .font_size = NULL,   .separator = FALSE )

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... One footnotes. may : character scalar — footnote text. list. first element footnote text (can unnamed). Optionally include align, placement, font_size override formatting. .align Default alignment. "left" (default) standard regulatory submissions. Override per line via list form. .placement print footnotes. "every" (default) repeats footnotes every page; "last" prints final page. Override per footnote via list form. .font_size Default font size points. NULL inherits page. .separator Logical. Whether draw horizontal separator rule footnote block. Default FALSE.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: modified fr_spec. Footnotes stored spec$meta$footnotes.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: ICH E3 common pharma submission standards specify: Labelling: Footnote labels use lower-case letters square brackets: [], [b], [c]. use numeric labels — [1], [2] reserved literature references. begin footnote section word "Note" "Notes:". Layout: footnote line, left-aligned, smaller font table body. RTF/SAS submissions: limit 8 footnotes per table full compatibility standard TLG macro footnote slots. Ordering: [], [b], … content footnotes first (order symbols appear reading left--right, top--bottom table). Abbreviation expansion line last (e.g. "AE = Adverse Event; FAS = Full Analysis Set; SD = Standard Deviation."). Source / data-cut note final page (placement = "last"). Common regulatory footnote patterns: Percentage denominator: "[] Percentages based N column header." — required whenever n (%) columns appear. MedDRA coding: "[] Coded using MedDRA Version XX.X." — AE, medical history, prior/concomitant medication tables. Subject counting: "[] subject counted multiple events within System Organ Class Preferred Term." — AE tables events > subjects possible. Dagger symbol: use {fr_dagger()} {fr_ddagger()} markup treatment-related serious AE markers.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: default, one blank line inserted last body row first footnote. Control fr_spacing():   can also set _arframe.yml:
code: spec |> fr_spacing(footnotes_before = 0L)   # no gap spec |> fr_spacing(footnotes_before = 2L)   # two blank lines spacing:   footnotes_before: 1

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: Use fr_super() superscript footnote markers inside cell values: "{fr_super('')} Value footnote". matching footnote line begin "[] ...". Set .separator = FALSE table ends bottom rule already visually separates footnotes. placement = "last" source note prevents repeating every continuation page. Note: works PDF output. RTF mechanism last-page-footer content ({\footer} repeats every page). RTF, footnotes appear every page regardless placement. warning emitted rendering RTF placement = "last" footnotes.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Set Table Footnotes — fr_footnotes
code: ## ── Demographics table: standard footnote set ─────────────────────────────  # Correct order: [a][b] content footnotes, abbreviations last, source final page tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages are based on N in the column header.",     "[b] P-value from two-sample t-test (continuous) or chi-squared (categorical).",     "FAS = Full Analysis Set; SD = Standard Deviation.",     list("Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024.", placement = "last")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (4): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages are based on N in the column header." #> 2. [left] "[b] P-value from two-sample t-test (continuous) or chi-sq..." #> 3. [left] "FAS = Full Analysis Set; SD = Standard Deviation." #> 4. [left [last]] "Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024."  ## ── AE table: MedDRA + subject-counting + source footnotes ───────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Coded using MedDRA Version 27.1.",     paste0("[b] A subject is counted only once for multiple events within ",            "the same System Organ Class or Preferred Term."),     "[c] Percentages are based on N in the column header.",     paste0("AE = Adverse Event; PT = Preferred Term; ",            "SOC = System Organ Class; TEAE = Treatment-Emergent AE."),     list("Source: ADAE; data cut 31DEC2024.", placement = "last")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (5): #> 1. [left] "[a] Coded using MedDRA Version 27.1." #> 2. [left] "[b] A subject is counted only once for multiple events wi..." #> 3. [left] "[c] Percentages are based on N in the column header." #> 4. [left] "AE = Adverse Event; PT = Preferred Term; SOC = System Org..." #> 5. [left [last]] "Source: ADAE; data cut 31DEC2024."  ## ── Source note on last page only ────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     list("Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024.", placement = "last")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header." #> 2. [left [last]] "Source: ADSL; data cut 31DEC2024."  ## ── Inline markup: dagger and double-dagger symbols ──────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "{fr_dagger()} Treatment-related adverse events.",     "{fr_ddagger()} Serious adverse events."   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "† Treatment-related adverse events." #> 2. [left] "‡ Serious adverse events."  ## ── Per-line font size override ───────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     list("Source: ADSL.", font_size = 7)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header." #> 2. [left] "Source: ADSL."  ## ── No separator rule ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("hsides") |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     .separator = FALSE   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── Right-aligned footnotes ───────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on N in column header.",     .align = "right"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [right] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── Clear all footnotes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_footnotes("[a] Old footnote.") spec <- spec |> fr_footnotes()  # removes all footnotes

what: Get Column Specifications from a Spec — fr_get_columns
title: Get Column Specifications from a Spec — fr_get_columns
text: Extracts named list fr_col objects configured via fr_cols(). element describes column's label, width, alignment, properties. columns explicitly configured, returns empty list (columns auto-generated render time finalize_spec()).

what: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
text: convenience wrapper calls fr_hlines() fr_vlines() one step. Use fr_grid() want set rule directions consistent styling (width, colour, linestyle). Prefer fr_grid() chaining fr_hlines() |> fr_vlines() : want uniform width/colour/linestyle directions. use slash shorthand concise one-liners: fr_grid("header/box"). Prefer separate fr_hlines() + fr_vlines() calls : Horizontal vertical rules need different widths, colours, styles. need fr_vlines(cols = ...) custom column positions.

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
code: fr_grid(   spec,   hpreset = "header",   vpreset = "box",   width = NULL,   color = NULL,   linestyle = NULL )

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). hpreset Horizontal rule preset passed fr_hlines(). Default "header" (single rule column header — standard TFL output). Alternatively, pass single slash-separated string combining presets: "header/box", "booktabs/", etc. / detected, left side used hpreset right side vpreset. vpreset Vertical rule preset passed fr_vlines(). Default "box" (outer border ). Ignored hpreset contains /. width Rule width applied horizontal vertical rules. Named shorthand ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick") numeric points. NULL uses preset's default. color Rule colour directions. NULL = black. linestyle Line style directions. NULL = "solid".

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
text: modified fr_spec horizontal vertical rules set.

title: Apply Horizontal and Vertical Rules Together — fr_grid
code: ## ── Standard pharma output (header + box) ─────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_grid() #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Slash shorthand ───────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Equivalent to fr_grid("booktabs", "box") tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_grid("booktabs/box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 3 hline(s)  ## ── Full grid (top/bottom + all column separators) ────────────────────  tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_grid("hsides/all") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  ## ── Custom width and colour applied uniformly ─────────────────────────  tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_grid("header/box", width = "medium") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # CSS named colour for both h and v rules tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_grid("header/box", color = "navy") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Full pipeline ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") |>   fr_grid("header/box") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: single owner header presentation settings: alignment, vertical alignment, bold, background/foreground colour, font size. defaults apply column header cells unless overridden per-column fr_col(header_align = ...) fr_style(region = "header", ...). fr_cols() owns column structure (labels, widths, body alignment, visibility, N-counts, spanning groups); fr_header() owns header presentation . N-count labels format set exclusively via fr_cols() .n / .n_format fr_col() n parameters. Calling fr_header() replaces previous header config (except spans, managed fr_spans()).

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
code: fr_header(   spec,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   bold = NULL,   background = NULL,   color = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   repeat_on_page = NULL )

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). align Scalar horizontal alignment header cells. One "left", "center", "right", NULL (inherit column body alignment). per-column header alignment, use fr_col(header_align = ...) instead. valign Default vertical alignment. One "top", "middle", "bottom" (default). Relevant header rows unequal height. bold Logical NULL. Whether header cells bold. Default NULL inherits built-default (FALSE). Set TRUE explicitly want bold headers. background Background colour header cells: hex string ("#003366") CSS named colour ("steelblue", "lightgray", etc.). NULL inherits. color Foreground (text) colour header cells: hex string CSS named colour. NULL inherits. font_size Font size points header cells. NULL inherits page font size. repeat_on_page Logical. Whether repeat column header every page. Default TRUE (standard regulatory tables).

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: modified fr_spec. Header config stored spec$header.

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
code: config header.align < fr_header(align) < fr_col(header_align) < fr_style(region="header", align)

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: Many pharma TFL outputs use bold, centered column headers valign = "bottom" short labels (e.g. "Characteristic") align bottom adjacent columns multi-line labels (e.g. "Placebo\\n(N=45)"). default, headers bold inherit alignment column's align setting — use fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") opt .

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
text: Header alignment priority (last wins): _arframe.yml < fr_theme(header=) < fr_header(align=) < fr_col(header_align=) < fr_style(region="header", align=) Header font size priority: fr_page(font_size=) < fr_header(font_size=) < fr_style(region="header", font_size=)

title: Configure Column Header Presentation — fr_header
code: ## ── Center all headers with bottom valign ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_header(align = "center", valign = "bottom", bold = TRUE) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: bold, valign=bottom, align=center  ## ── Per-column header alignment via fr_col ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", header_align = "left"),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50 mg", header_align = "center"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",         header_align = "center"),     total          = fr_col("Total",           header_align = "right")   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 1.85in left #> placebo "Placebo" 0.61in left #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50 mg" 0.97in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.70in left #> total "Total" 0.61in left #> group "group" 0.62in left #> Header: bold, valign=bottom  ## ── Header background colour (hex or CSS named colour) ──────────────────  # Hex colour tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_header(background = "#E0E0E0", bold = TRUE) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: bold, valign=bottom  # CSS named colour (148 available: lavender, aliceblue, gainsboro, etc.) tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_header(background = "lavender", color = "midnightblue", bold = TRUE) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: bold, valign=bottom  ## ── N-counts now on fr_cols(), not fr_header() ────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50 mg", n = 45),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",         n = 45),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo" 0.61in left #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50 mg" 0.97in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.70in left #> total "total" 0.61in left #> group "group" 0.62in left #> Header: bold, valign=bottom, align=center

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: Sets horizontal rules (lines) table using named preset custom rule properties. Calling fr_hlines() replaces previously set horizontal rules.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
code: fr_hlines(   spec,   preset = "header",   width = NULL,   color = NULL,   linestyle = NULL )

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). preset Named rule preset. One : "header" — single rule column header . Default common TFL outputs (ICH E3). top bottom border; footnotes follow last body row directly. "open" — rule header + rule header; bottom border. Use table bottom immediately followed footnotes. "hsides" — rule top (header) bottom (body) . "" — single rule column header . "" — single rule last body row . "box" — full outer border four sides. "booktabs" — thick top (1 pt) + thin mid (0.5 pt) + thick bottom (1 pt). standard PDF publication output. "void" — horizontal rules. Clears previously set rules. width Rule width. Named shorthand numeric points: "hairline" = 0.25 pt "thin" = 0.5 pt (default preset width) "medium" = 1.0 pt "thick" = 1.5 pt Numeric, e.g. width = 0.75 (positive, pt) supplied, overrides preset's default widths rules created call. NULL uses preset's default widths. color Rule colour: hex string ("#003366") CSS named colour ("navy"). NULL = black ("#000000"). linestyle Rule line style. One "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "dashdot", "double".

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: modified fr_spec. Rules stored spec$rules.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: pharma TFL outputs use "header": single thin solid rule separating column headers body. top bottom border. Footnotes placed directly last body row short separator line drawn footnote block (controlled .separator argument fr_footnotes()). "booktabs" standard PDF/publication output matches LaTeX \toprule / \midrule / \bottomrule pattern.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: Combine fr_hlines() fr_vlines() independently — manages set rules. fr_hlines("void") clears horizontal rules; vertical rules fr_vlines() affected. width, color, linestyle apply uniformly rules created preset call. mixed widths (e.g. booktabs thick/thin), omit width let preset define widths.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Apply Horizontal Rules — fr_hlines
code: ## ── Common presets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Standard TFL output: single rule under column header tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Open: header rules, no bottom border tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("open") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  # Booktabs: publication-quality thick/thin/thick tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("booktabs") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 3 hline(s)  # Top and bottom rules only tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("hsides") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  # Box: full outer border tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Remove all horizontal rules tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("void") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom width ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Hairline rules (0.25 pt) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", width = "hairline") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Thick rules (1.5 pt) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("hsides", width = "thick") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)  # Exact width in points tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", width = 0.75) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Custom colour (hex or CSS named colour) ────────────────────────────────  # Hex colour tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("booktabs", color = "#003366") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 3 hline(s)  # CSS named colour tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", color = "steelblue") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ##
