── Custom linestyle ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Dashed rule below header tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", linestyle = "dashed") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Double rule below header tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", linestyle = "double") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Dash-dot (SAS ODS equivalent) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_hlines("header", linestyle = "dashdot") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Full pipeline ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics", "Full Analysis Set") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on N in column header.") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."

title: Install Required LaTeX Packages — fr_install_latex_deps
text: Proactively installs LaTeX packages needed arframe's PDF backend. Checks packages missing, installs using three-tier strategy:

title: Install Required LaTeX Packages — fr_install_latex_deps
code: fr_install_latex_deps(repository = NULL)

title: Install Required LaTeX Packages — fr_install_latex_deps
text: repository Character NULL. CTAN mirror URL system tlmgr fallback (Tier 2). Default NULL uses "https://mirror.ctan.org/systems/texlive/tlnet". Set corporate networks require specific mirror.

title: Install Required LaTeX Packages — fr_install_latex_deps
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect installing packages.

title: Install Required LaTeX Packages — fr_install_latex_deps
text: TinyTeX — tinytex::is_tinytex() TRUE, uses tinytex::tlmgr_install() (user-writable, auto-updates). System tlmgr — TinyTeX fails absent system tlmgr PATH, calls tlmgr --repository <mirror> install directly. handles corporate RHEL/Ubuntu systems system TeX Live. Error — TeX distribution found, provides install instructions. Missing packages detected via tinytex::check_installed() available, falling back kpsewhich command line.

title: Install Required LaTeX Packages — fr_install_latex_deps
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Standard usage fr_install_latex_deps()  # Corporate network with specific mirror fr_install_latex_deps(   repository = "https://ctan.mirror.garr.it/mirrors/ctan/systems/texlive/tlnet" ) } # }

title: Italic Markup — fr_italic
text: Renders text span italic final output. Use inside glue-string expressions italicize portion title, footnote, column label, cell value. Common regulatory tables : P-value annotations: "{fr_italic('P')}-value" renders P-value. Statistical method notes: Fisher's exact, Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel. Latin abbreviations: vs., et al., vitro.

title: Italic Markup — fr_italic
code: fr_italic(x)

title: Italic Markup — fr_italic
text: x Character. Text render italic.

title: Italic Markup — fr_italic
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string, produces sentinel token resolved render time.

title: Italic Markup — fr_italic
text: RTF output, italic uses \... \i0 control words. future LaTeX output, maps \textit{}.

title: Italic Markup — fr_italic
code: # Italic P-value annotation fr_italic("P") #> <fr_markup> _P_  # In footnotes: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes("[a] {fr_italic('P')}-value from Fisher's exact test.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] P-value from Fisher's exact test." spec |> fr_footnotes("Comparison {fr_italic('vs.')} placebo.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Comparison vs. placebo."

title: List Required LaTeX Packages — fr_latex_deps
text: Returns CTAN package names arframe's PDF backend requires. packages loaded LaTeX preamble generated fr_render() producing .pdf .tex output.

title: List Required LaTeX Packages — fr_latex_deps
code: fr_latex_deps()

title: List Required LaTeX Packages — fr_latex_deps
text: character vector CTAN package names.

title: List Required LaTeX Packages — fr_latex_deps
text: packages ship full TeX Live installation, tabularray often missing minimal distributions like tinytex. Use fr_install_latex_deps() install everything .

title: List Required LaTeX Packages — fr_latex_deps
code: fr_latex_deps() #> [1] "tabularray" "fontspec"   "geometry"   "xcolor"     "colortbl"   #> [6] "fancyhdr"   "lastpage"   "booktabs"

what: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
title: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
text: Entry point clinical listings — long-format data displays show individual records (e.g., adverse event listings, concomitant medication listings, lab data listings). Listings differ summary tables every row data record, summary statistic. fr_listing() creates fr_spec listing-appropriate defaults: landscape orientation, smaller font (8pt), left-aligned columns, header-rules, column splitting enabled.

title: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
code: fr_listing(data)

title: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
text: data data frame. row maps one listing row.

title: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
text: fr_spec object type = "listing" listing defaults.

title: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
text: sort_by: Pass fr_rows() sort data rendering suppress: Pass fr_rows() suppress repeated values wrap: Auto-enabled long text columns

title: Start a arframe Listing Pipeline — fr_listing
code: ## ── Minimal listing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────  adae |> fr_listing() #>  #> ── fr_spec: Listing  #> Data: 758 rows x 27 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 8pt Times New Roman #> Columns (27 visible of 27): #> STUDYID "STUDYID" auto left #> USUBJID "USUBJID" auto left #> ARM "ARM" auto left #> TRTA "TRTA" auto left #> TRTAN "TRTAN" auto left #> AGE "AGE" auto left #> SEX "SEX" auto left #> RACE "RACE" auto left #> ... and 19 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: wrap #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Full listing pipeline ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  adae |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.2),     AEDECOD = fr_col("Preferred Term"),     AESEV   = fr_col("Severity", width = 1.0)   ) |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT"),           suppress = "USUBJID") |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.7 Adverse Events") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADAE") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Listing  #> Data: 758 rows x 27 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 8pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Listing 16.2.7 Adverse Events" #> Columns (27 visible of 27): #> STUDYID "STUDYID" 0.82in left #> USUBJID "Subject ID" 1.20in left #> ARM "ARM" 0.84in left #> TRTA "TRTA" 0.84in left #> TRTAN "TRTAN" 0.50in right #> AGE "AGE" 0.50in right #> SEX "SEX" 0.50in left #> RACE "RACE" 2.17in left #> ... and 19 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,ASTDT, wrap #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Source: ADAE"  ## ── Listing with wrap = TRUE for long text ─────────────────────────────── # wrap is TRUE by default in fr_listing(), but can also be set via fr_rows().  adcm |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID = fr_col("Subject", width = 1.2),     CMDECOD = fr_col("Medication", width = 2.0),     CMCAT   = fr_col("Category", width = 1.5),     CMSTDT  = fr_col("Start Date", width = 1.0),     CMENDT  = fr_col("End Date", width = 1.0)   ) |>   fr_rows(wrap = TRUE) |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.4 Concomitant Medications") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADCM") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Listing  #> Data: 597 rows x 13 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 8pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Listing 16.2.4 Concomitant Medications" #> Columns (13 visible of 13): #> STUDYID "STUDYID" 0.82in left #> USUBJID "Subject" 1.20in left #> ARM "ARM" 0.84in left #> TRTA "TRTA" 0.84in left #> TRTAN "TRTAN" 0.50in right #> AGE "AGE" 0.50in right #> SEX "SEX" 0.50in left #> SAFFL "SAFFL" 0.50in left #> ... and 5 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: wrap #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Source: ADCM"  ## ── Listing with page_by pagination ────────────────────────────────────── # Each treatment arm starts on a new page.  adae |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID  = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.2),     AEBODSYS = fr_col("System Organ Class", width = 2.0),     AEDECOD  = fr_col("Preferred Term", width = 1.5),     AESEV    = fr_col("Severity", width = 0.8),     TRTA     = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "TRTA") |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.7.1 Adverse Events by Treatment Arm") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Listing  #> Data: 758 rows x 27 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 8pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Listing 16.2.7.1 Adverse Events by Treatment Arm" #> Columns (26 visible of 27): #> STUDYID "STUDYID" 0.82in left #> USUBJID "Subject ID" 1.20in left #> ARM "ARM" 0.84in left #> TRTAN "TRTAN" 0.50in right #> AGE "AGE" 0.50in right #> SEX "SEX" 0.50in left #> RACE "RACE" 2.17in left #> SAFFL "SAFFL" 0.50in left #> ... and 18 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=TRTA, wrap #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── sort_by + suppress together ──────────────────────────────────────── # Sort by subject and start day, suppress repeated subject IDs.  adae |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.3),     AEDECOD = fr_col("Preferred Term", width = 2.0),     AESEV   = fr_col("Severity", width = 0.8),     ASTDY   = fr_col("Study Day", width = 0.8, align = "right"),     ADURN   = fr_col("Duration (days)", width = 1.0, align = "right")   ) |>   fr_rows(     sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDY"),     suppress = "USUBJID"   ) |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.7.2 Adverse Events Sorted by Subject and Study Day") |>   fr_footnotes("Repeated subject IDs are suppressed for readability.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Listing  #> Data: 758 rows x 27 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 8pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Listing 16.2.7.2 Adverse Events Sorted by Subject and Stu..." #> Columns (27 visible of 27): #> STUDYID "STUDYID" 0.82in left #> USUBJID "Subject ID" 1.30in left #> ARM "ARM" 0.84in left #> TRTA "TRTA" 0.84in left #> TRTAN "TRTAN" 0.50in right #> AGE "AGE" 0.50in right #> SEX "SEX" 0.50in left #> RACE "RACE" 2.17in left #> ... and 19 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,ASTDY, wrap #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "Repeated subject IDs are suppressed for readability."

what: Line Break Within a Text Element — fr_newline
title: Line Break Within a Text Element — fr_newline
text: Inserts line break within single title line, footnote line, column label. Use need multi-line label logically one element (e.g. column header wraps two lines). cases can use literal "\\n" string instead. fr_newline() provided use inside glue expressions literal newline awkward ambiguous.

title: Line Break Within a Text Element — fr_newline
code: fr_newline()

title: Line Break Within a Text Element — fr_newline
text: fr_markup object resolves line break render time.

title: Line Break Within a Text Element — fr_newline
text: RTF output, newline resolves \\line (soft line break within paragraph). future LaTeX output, maps \\\\. layout engine accounts newlines calculating row height.

title: Line Break Within a Text Element — fr_newline
code: # Multi-line column header via newline markup fr_col("Treatment{fr_newline()}Arm", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "Treatment{fr_newline()}Arm" [1.50in, left]  # Equivalent using literal newline (simpler — preferred) fr_col("Treatment\nArm", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "Treatment #> Arm" [1.50in, left]

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: Sets page-level properties: paper size, orientation, margins, font, pagination controls. arguments explicitly supply changed — others retain current values. allows incremental updates anywhere pipeline.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
code: fr_page(   spec,   orientation = NULL,   paper = NULL,   margins = NULL,   font_family = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   orphan_min = NULL,   widow_min = NULL,   continuation = NULL,   col_gap = NULL,   tokens = NULL )

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). orientation "landscape" (default, 11 × 8.5 Letter) "portrait" (8.5 × 11 ). paper "letter" (default, 8.5 × 11 ), "a4" (210 × 297 mm), "legal" (8.5 × 14 ). margins Margin(s) inches. Accepts: Scalar: 1 — four sides equal. Length 2: c(vertical, horizontal) — top/bottom vs left/right. Length 4: c(top, right, bottom, left) — CSS order. Named list: list(top=1, bottom=1, left=0.75, right=0.75). font_family Font family name. Default: "Times New Roman" (FDA recommended submission documents). font_size Font size points. Default 9. Typical pharma range: 7–10 pt. orphan_min Minimum body rows keep bottom page forcing page break. Default 3L. Set 1L disable. widow_min Minimum body rows carry top next page. Default 3L. Set 1L disable. continuation Character scalar appended column header block continuation pages, e.g. "(continued)". NULL (default) disables. col_gap Inter-column padding points (total gap adjacent columns). Default 4 (2 pt left + 2 pt right per cell). Increase 6 8 long text adjacent columns runs together. Set 0 zero padding (cells flush, legacy behaviour). Applied symmetrically half value side every cell RTF PDF output. tokens Named list custom {token} values use page headers/footers. E.g. list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", pop = "FAS"). Use tokens fr_pagehead() fr_pagefoot() strings.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: arframe resolves fonts priority: FDA-recommended — Times New Roman (serif), Calibri/Arial (sans-serif), Courier New (monospace) ARFRAME_FONT_DIR — custom fonts directory specified environment variable (.ttf/.otf files). Ideal Docker, CI, project-local fonts. Adobe open-source (SIL OFL, free) — Source Serif 4 (serif), Source Sans 3 (sans-serif), Source Code Pro (monospace). Install Google Fonts https://github.com/adobe-fonts. CSS generic — serif, sans-serif, monospace (HTML ; RTF/PDF viewers substitute closest available font). Three font families supported: Serif (roman): "Times New Roman", "Source Serif 4", "Georgia", etc. Sans-serif (swiss): "Calibri", "Arial", "Source Sans 3", etc. Monospace (modern): "Courier New", "Source Code Pro", "Consolas", etc.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: modified fr_spec. Page settings stored spec$page.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: fr_page() uses replace semantics: rebuilds entire page spec via new_fr_page(), carrying forward current value argument supply. effect explicitly supplied arguments change — internally full page object replaced. contrast, fr_pagehead() fr_pagefoot() use merge semantics: read existing pagehead/pagefoot object update fields supply, preserving others previous call. means can call fr_pagehead() twice — left right — values retained. fr_spacing() also merges: call updates spacing fields supply, leaving others unchanged.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: FDA/US submissions (eCTD): landscape Letter (8.5 × 11 ), Times New Roman 9 pt, 1 margins sides. common regulatory RTF standard package default. EMA/European submissions: A4 paper (210 × 297 mm) acceptable; landscape A4 common wide safety tables. Database cutoff date mandatory element every CSR table. standard practice define custom token:   Continuation label: multi-page tables must indicate continuation. Regulatory convention append "(continued)" column header block pages first:
code: fr_page(tokens = list(cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |> fr_pagehead(left  = "Study: {study}",             right = "Database Cutoff: {cutoff}") fr_page(continuation = "(continued)")

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: tokens clean way inject study-level metadata (study number, cutoff date, population) running headers without repeating value every fr_pagehead() call. orphan_min = 3 means: fewer 3 rows remain bottom page group_by group, move group next page.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
text: font_size applies table text unless overridden: fr_page(font_size=) < fr_header(font_size=) < fr_pagehead(font_size=) / fr_pagefoot(font_size=) < fr_style(font_size=)

title: Set Page Layout Options — fr_page
code: ## ── Standard pharma RTF setup ────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(     orientation = "landscape",     paper       = "letter",     font_family = "Times New Roman",     font_size   = 9,     margins     = 1   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── A4 portrait for European submissions ─────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "portrait", paper = "a4", font_size = 9) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: portrait a4, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Narrow margins to fit wide table ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(margins = c(1, 0.5))   # top/bottom = 1in, left/right = 0.5in #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom tokens for running header ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", pop = "FAS")) |>   fr_pagehead(left  = "Study: {study}  Population: {pop}",               right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Continuation label on multi-page tables ──────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(continuation = "(continued)") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Tight orphan/widow control ────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orphan_min = 2L, widow_min = 2L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Wider inter-column padding ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(col_gap = 8)   # 4 pt each side (generous spacing) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman, col_gap=8pt #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Legal paper size (8.5 x 14 in) ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(paper = "legal", orientation = "landscape", margins = 1) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape legal, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom fonts via ARFRAME_FONT_DIR (Docker/CI) ────────────────────  # Set ARFRAME_FONT_DIR to a directory of .ttf/.otf files; # XeLaTeX discovers them by name --- no system install needed. # Sys.setenv(ARFRAME_FONT_DIR = "/path/to/fonts")

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: Sets running footer printed bottom every page. footer uses three-zone (left, center, right) system fr_pagehead(). Supports {token} placeholders, evaluated render time: {program} — R script filename (rstudioapi sys.call). {datetime} — run datetime DDMONYYYY HH:MM:SS format (e.g. "05MAR2026 14:24:25"). {thepage}, {total_pages}, custom tokens also supported. Calling fr_pagefoot() merges previous footer: arguments explicitly supply changed.

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
code: fr_pagefoot(   spec,   left = NULL,   center = NULL,   right = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   bold = NULL )

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). left Character scalar NULL. Left zone text. center Character scalar NULL. Center zone text. right Character scalar NULL. Right zone text. three zones support token placeholders evaluated render time. font_size Font size points. NULL inherits page. bold Logical. Default NULL (bold).

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: modified fr_spec. Footer stored spec$pagefoot.

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: Standard pharma table shells specify following footer content every table page: Left zone: Program path filename. Use {program} token, resolves R script filename render time. Center zone: Confidentiality label needed. Right zone: Data source name, data extract date, run datetime. Full submission-standard footer:   {datetime} token resolves DDMONYYYY HH:MM:SS render time (e.g. "05MAR2026 14:24:25").
code: fr_page(tokens = list(datasource = "ADSL", extract = "01JAN2025")) |> fr_pagefoot(   left  = "{program}",   right = "Data Source: {datasource}  Extract: {extract}  Run: {datetime}" )

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Set Running Page Footer — fr_pagefoot
code: ## ── Program path + run datetime (most common footer pattern) ─────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Full submission-standard footer: program + data source + runtime ──────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(datasource = "ADSL", extract = "01JAN2025")) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "Data Source: {datasource}  Extract: {extract}  Run: {datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Program + confidentiality + datetime (3-zone) ─────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(     left   = "{program}",     center = "CONFIDENTIAL",     right  = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Minimal: page number only in footer ───────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Full page chrome: pagehead + pagefoot together ────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Cutoff: {cutoff}"   ) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: Sets running header printed top every page (every page first). header divided three independent zones: left, center, right. Supports {token} placeholders, evaluated render time: {thepage} — current page number. {total_pages} — total page count. Custom tokens defined via fr_page(tokens = list(...)). Calling fr_pagehead() merges previous header: arguments explicitly supply changed.

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
code: fr_pagehead(   spec,   left = NULL,   center = NULL,   right = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   bold = NULL )

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). left Character scalar, character vector, NULL. Left zone text. Vectors collapsed newlines produce multi-line output. center Character scalar, character vector, NULL. Center zone text. right Character scalar, character vector, NULL. Right zone text. three zones support token placeholders (e.g. thepage) evaluated render time. font_size Font size header text points. NULL inherits page font size (fr_page(font_size = ...)). bold Logical. Whether header text bold. Default NULL (bold).

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: modified fr_spec. Header stored spec$pagehead.

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: ICH E3 specifies 3-part running header every table page: Left zone: Study/protocol identifier. Center zone: Compound name, document type, left blank. Right zone: "Page X Y". database cutoff date mandatory audit trail element. Standard table shells show right running header every page:   Implement :   Date format RTF submissions DDMONYYYY (e.g. "31DEC2024"), ISO 8601, match SAS ODS date conventions.
code: Study/Protocol: TFRM-2024-001          Database Cutoff Date: 31DEC2024 fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |> fr_pagehead(   left  = "Study/Protocol: {study}",   right = "Database Cutoff Date: {cutoff}" )

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
text: default, gap inserted page header. Add one fr_spacing():   can also set _arframe.yml:
code: spec |> fr_spacing(pagehead_after = 1L)   # one blank line spec |> fr_spacing(pagehead_after = 2L)   # two blank lines spacing:   pagehead_after: 1

title: Set Running Page Header — fr_pagehead
code: ## ── Standard: Study/Protocol + Database Cutoff Date ──────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study  = "TFRM-2024-001",                         cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Study/Protocol: {study}",     right = "Database Cutoff Date: {cutoff}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── 3-part header: study | CONFIDENTIAL | Page X of Y ────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left   = "Study/Protocol: {study}",     center = "CONFIDENTIAL",     right  = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Multi-token header with compound and population ───────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(     study    = "TFRM-2024-001",     compound = "Zomerane",     pop      = "FAS"   )) |>   fr_pagehead(     left   = "{study}  {compound}",     center = "Population: {pop}",     right  = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Smaller header font size ──────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagehead(     left      = "TFRM-2024-001",     right     = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}",     font_size = 7   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Combined pagehead + pagefoot in one pipeline ──────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(tokens = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024")) |>   fr_pagehead(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Cutoff: {cutoff}"   ) |>   fr_pagefoot(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom

what: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: Captures sequence fr_*() verb calls fr_recipe object can applied fr_spec. Recipes enable team-wide standardisation: define company style , reuse across hundreds tables. Apply recipe spec fr_apply(). Compose multiple recipes c(): c(recipe_a, recipe_b) creates combined recipe recipe_b overrides recipe_a verbs replace (verbs), appends verbs accumulate (fr_spans, fr_styles).

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
code: fr_recipe(...)

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: ... Unevaluated fr_*() verb calls. call must pipeline verb takes fr_spec first argument. spec argument omitted — injected recipe applied.

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: fr_recipe object (list captured call expressions).

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: fr_apply(): spec |> fr_apply(recipe) — pipe-friendly c(): c(recipe1, recipe2) — compose two recipes

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
text: Recipes store R language objects (calls), closures, survive saveRDS() / readRDS() roundtrips:
code: saveRDS(company_style, "company_style.rds") company_style <- readRDS("company_style.rds")

title: Create a Reusable Table Recipe — fr_recipe
code: ## ── Recipe for page layout ────────────────────────────────────────────────  page_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9),   fr_pagehead(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}"),   fr_pagefoot(center = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") ) page_recipe #>  #> ── fr_recipe (3 verbs)  #> 1. fr_page(orientation="landscape", font_size=9) #> 2. fr_pagehead(left="{program}", right="{datetime}") #> 3. fr_pagefoot(center="Page {thepage} of {total_pages}")  ## ── Recipe for styling ────────────────────────────────────────────────────  style_recipe <- fr_recipe(   fr_hlines("header"),   fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = "center") ) style_recipe #>  #> ── fr_recipe (2 verbs)  #> 1. fr_hlines("header") #> 2. fr_header(bold=TRUE, align="center")  ## ── Apply a recipe then add per-table customisation ───────────────────────  company_style <- c(page_recipe, style_recipe)  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(company_style) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Demographics") spec #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1" #> 2. [center] "Demographics" #> Header: bold, valign=bottom, align=center #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Compose: base + override for AE tables ────────────────────────────────  ae_style <- c(company_style, fr_recipe(   fr_hlines("box"),   fr_page(font_size = 8) )) ae_spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_apply(ae_style) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE Summary by SOC")

what: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
text: Registers new output format backend fr_render() can produce output format. Third-party packages can use add HTML, DOCX, formats without modifying arframe source.

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
code: fr_register_backend(format, extensions, render_fn, description = "")

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
text: format Character scalar. Format identifier (e.g., "html"). extensions Character vector. File extensions map format (e.g., c("html", "htm")). render_fn Function. render function signature function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path). description Character scalar. Human-readable description.

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Register a Custom Render Backend — fr_register_backend
code: # Register a custom CSV backend fr_register_backend(   format = "csv",   extensions = "csv",   render_fn = function(spec, page_groups, col_panels, path) {     utils::write.csv(spec$data, path, row.names = FALSE)   },   description = "CSV export (data only)" )  # Verify it appears in the backend list fr_backends() #>   format extensions                   description #> 1    rtf   rtf, doc              Rich Text Format #> 2  latex        tex     LaTeX source (tabularray) #> 3    pdf        pdf               PDF via XeLaTeX #> 4   html  html, htm HTML preview (self-contained) #> 5    csv        csv        CSV export (data only)  # Use the custom backend out <- file.path(tempdir(), "demo.csv") tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_render(out) readLines(out, n = 3) #> [1] "\"characteristic\",\"placebo\",\"zom_50mg\",\"zom_100mg\",\"total\",\"group\"" #> [2] "\"Subjects, n\",\"45\",\"45\",\"45\",\"135\",\"n\""                            #> [3] "\"Age (years)\",\"\",\"\",\"\",\"\",\"age_cont\""                              unlink(out) fr_unregister_backend("csv")

what: Register a Custom Decimal Alignment Stat Type — fr_register_stat_type
title: Register a Custom Decimal Alignment Stat Type — fr_register_stat_type
text: Adds custom statistical format type decimal alignment engine. Use data contains formatted statistics match 18 built-types (see fr_env$stat_type_registry full list).

title: Register a Custom Decimal Alignment Stat Type — fr_register_stat_type
code: fr_register_stat_type(name, pattern, family = "custom", richness = 3L)

title: Register a Custom Decimal Alignment Stat Type — fr_register_stat_type
text: name Character scalar. Unique name type (e.g., "ratio_ci"). Must conflict built-type names. pattern Character scalar. Perl-compatible regex matches formatted string. Must match entire string (anchored ^...$). family Character scalar. Alignment family: "compound", "estimate", "range", "count", "float", "custom". Determines type aligns relative types column. Default "custom". richness Integer scalar. Component count (higher = complex). Used tie-breaking multiple types match. Default 3L.

title: Register a Custom Decimal Alignment Stat Type — fr_register_stat_type
text: Invisible NULL. Called side effect registering type.

title: Register a Custom Decimal Alignment Stat Type — fr_register_stat_type
code: # Register a custom "ratio (lower, upper)" format fr_register_stat_type(   name = "ratio_ci",   pattern = "^-?\\d+\\.?\\d*\\s*\\(-?\\d+\\.?\\d*,\\s*-?\\d+\\.?\\d*\\)$",   family = "compound",   richness = 4L )

what: Render a Table to File — fr_render
title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: terminal verb every arframe pipeline. Takes fully configured fr_spec object writes rendered table file. Supports RTF, LaTeX, PDF output. Row pagination within RTF output delegated Word/LibreOffice rendering engine via native RTF properties (\trhdr repeating headers). LaTeX backend uses tabularray's longtblr environment rowhead repeating headers. R handles page_by group splitting (section/page breaks groups) col_split (column panel calculation wide tables).

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
code: fr_render(spec, path, format = NULL, ...)

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(), configured fr_*() pipeline verbs. path Character scalar. Output file path. file extension determines format unless format specified: .rtf — Rich Text Format. .html / .htm — Self-contained HTML preview. .tex — LaTeX source (tabularray/XeLaTeX). .pdf — PDF via XeLaTeX compilation (requires XeLaTeX PATH tinytex). First-time setup: call fr_install_latex_deps() install required LaTeX packages (notably tabularray). custom fonts without system-wide installation, set ARFRAME_FONT_DIR environment variable (see fr_page()). format Character scalar NULL. Output format: "rtf", "html", "latex", "pdf". NULL (default), detected file extension path. ... Reserved future backend-specific options.

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: Invisibly returns path (output file path).

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: fr_rows(page_by = "col_name") set, data split unique values column. group rendered separate section table, separated \sect (section break) RTF. page-column value printed bold label group's table.

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
text: fr_cols(.split = TRUE) set total column width exceeds printable page area, columns automatically split panels. Stub columns (set via fr_col(stub = TRUE)) repeated every panel. panel rendered separate section.

title: Render a Table to File — fr_render
code: # Render to a temporary RTF file out <- file.path(tempdir(), "demog.rtf") tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)  # Full pipeline (in study programs, hlines/pagehead/pagefoot go in fr_theme()) out <- file.path(tempdir(), "demog_full.rtf") tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1", "Summary of Demographics") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL", "Program: t_demog.R") |>   fr_cols(.width = "auto") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape") |>   fr_pagehead(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}") |>   fr_pagefoot(center = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)  # Multiple format rendering in sequence (RTF then LaTeX source) rtf_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "ae_soc.rtf") tex_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "ae_soc.tex") spec <- tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "Adverse Events by SOC") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape") fr_render(spec, rtf_out) fr_render(spec, tex_out) unlink(c(rtf_out, tex_out))  # Listing rendering — individual AE records out <- file.path(tempdir(), "ae_listing.rtf") adae |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_cols(     USUBJID = fr_col("Subject ID", width = 1.2),     AEDECOD = fr_col("Preferred Term"),     AESEV   = fr_col("Severity", width = 1.0)   ) |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT"),           suppress = "USUBJID") |>   fr_titles("Listing 16.2.7", "Adverse Events") |>   fr_footnotes("Source: ADAE") |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)  # Figure rendering (requires ggplot2) if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(adsl, ggplot2::aes(x = AGE)) +     ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 5) +     ggplot2::labs(title = NULL, x = "Age (years)", y = "Count")   out <- file.path(tempdir(), "age_dist.rtf")   p |>     fr_figure() |>     fr_titles("Figure 14.1.1", "Distribution of Age") |>     fr_footnotes("Source: ADSL") |>     fr_page(orientation = "landscape") |>     fr_render(out)   unlink(out) }

what: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: Creates row-level style object use fr_styles(). Row styles apply uniformly across cells targeted rows.

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
code: fr_row_style(   rows = NULL,   bold = NULL,   italic = NULL,   underline = NULL,   color = NULL,   background = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   height = NULL )

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: rows Integer vector body row positions, "", NULL (body rows), special selector. Row indices 1-based. Multiple indices supported: rows = c(1L, 3L, 5L). Group header selectors (resolved render time): "group_headers" — group header rows (group_by label leaf). "group_headers:<level>" — headers specific hierarchy level (e.g., "group_headers:soc"). leaf hierarchies . Page_by selector: "page_by" — page_by section labels (rendered column headers page group). Page_by labels plain text default across backends (RTF, HTML, PDF). Supports: bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_size, align. support height valign (page_by labels section headers, table rows). bold, italic, underline Logical NULL inherit. color Foreground (text) colour, NULL. background Background (fill) colour, NULL. font_size Font size points, NULL. align Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model. valign Vertical alignment: "top", "middle", "bottom", NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model. height Row height inches, NULL (auto).

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: fr_cell_style object type = "row" use fr_styles().

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: multiple styles target cell, narrower scopes win:   Within scope level, later styles (later fr_styles() call later fr_styles() calls) override earlier ones. fr_row_style() overrides fr_col_style() property row styles narrower column styles.
code: fr_col_style  <  fr_row_style  <  fr_style (cell)

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
text: Use fr_row_style(rows = "", background = "#F5F5F5") subtle background body rows (zebra striping requires alternating calls: rows odd/even index). Row height regulatory tables normally controlled fr_page(font_size = ...) layout engine. Set height need specific row taller (e.g. summary row). Use fr_rows_matches() instead hard-coded row indices content-based row targeting (e.g. bold every "Total" row).

title: Define a Row Style Override — fr_row_style
code: ## ── Standalone: store and reuse ───────────────────────────────────────── bold_first <- fr_row_style(rows = 1L, bold = TRUE) bold_first  # inspect #> $type #> [1] "row" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Bold the first body row (e.g. total row) ─────────────────────────────  fr_row_style(rows = 1L, bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "row" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Light grey background on all body rows ────────────────────────────────  fr_row_style(rows = "all", background = "#F5F5F5") #> $type #> [1] "row" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] "all" #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#F5F5F5" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Highlight multiple specific rows ──────────────────────────────────────  # Pass a vector of row indices to style multiple disparate rows at once: fr_row_style(rows = c(2L, 4L, 6L), background = "#E8F4FD") #> $type #> [1] "row" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 2 4 6 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#E8F4FD" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Highlight last row (totals) in a different colour ─────────────────────  fr_row_style(rows = nrow(tbl_demog), background = "#FFF3CD", bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "row" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 28 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#FFF3CD" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Increase height of header-adjacent row ────────────────────────────────  fr_row_style(rows = 1L, height = 0.3) #> $type #> [1] "row" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> [1] 0.3 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Full pipeline with row styles ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "all", background = "#FAFAFA"),     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, background = "#E0E0E0")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Bold all group header rows ────────────────────────────────────────  data.frame(   variable = c("Sex", "Sex", "Age", "Age"),   stat = c("Female", "Male", "Mean (SD)", "Median"),   value = c("27 (60.0)", "18 (40.0)", "75.0 (6.8)", "74.0"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(variable = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat")) |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "group_headers", bold = TRUE, background = "#E8E8E8")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 4 rows x 3 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (2 visible of 3): #> stat "stat" 0.62in left #> value "value" 0.55in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=variable (label=stat), indent_by=stat #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Bold page_by labels ───────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_vs[tbl_vs$timepoint == "Week 24", ] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(param = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           timepoint = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(page_by = "param") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = "page_by", bold = TRUE)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 20 rows x 12 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (10 visible of 12): #> statistic "statistic" 0.62in left #> placebo_base "placebo_base" 0.77in left #> placebo_value "placebo_value" 0.82in left #> placebo_chg "placebo_chg" 0.72in left #> zom_50mg_base "zom_50mg_base" 0.94in left #> zom_50mg_value "zom_50mg_value" 0.99in left #> zom_50mg_chg "zom_50mg_chg" 0.89in left #> zom_100mg_base "zom_100mg_base" 1.00in left #> ... and 2 more #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=param

what: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: Controls data rows grouped, paginated, indented, spaced. Calling fr_rows() merges previous row configuration: arguments explicitly supply changed. arframe uses two-level greedy pagination algorithm (identical RTF PDF output): page_by forces page break whenever named column(s) change value. group_by keeps rows value named column(s) page via \trkeep (RTF). group large single page, breaks normally "(continued)" header row repeats top next page.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
code: fr_rows(   spec,   page_by = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   indent_by = NULL,   blank_after = NULL,   group_keep = TRUE,   group_style = NULL,   sort_by = NULL,   suppress = NULL,   wrap = FALSE )

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). page_by Page break specification. Accepts two forms: Simple (character): Character vector column name(s). new page begins whenever columns change value. column value rendered group label column headers, column automatically hidden table body render time. List form: named list elements: cols — character vector column name(s) (required) visible — logical; whether page-label displayed column headers (default TRUE). Set FALSE get page breaks group boundaries without visible label. Style page-labels (bold, alignment, colour) via fr_styles() rows = "page_by", set defaults via fr_theme(). group_by Group specification. Accepts two forms: Simple (character): Character vector column name(s). Rows sharing value visually grouped (kept together page possible). group spans pages, "(continued)" header row repeats top next page. List form: named list elements: cols — character vector column name(s) (required). multiple columns supplied, groups defined combination column values (e.g., cols = c("PARAMCD", "direction") groups unique PARAMCD + direction pair). label — character scalar; column name group header values injected. header row inserted start group, containing value first cols column. label set indent_by , indent_by automatically inferred label, indenting detail rows bold group header. leaf — character scalar; lowest-level column multi-level hierarchy. Must one cols values. set, arframe collapses hierarchy columns single __display__ column adds __row_level__ column containing source column name row (e.g., "soc" "pt"). Use __row_level__ fr_rows_matches() style specific levels independently — example, bold SOC header rows without affecting PT rows. Indentation auto-set hierarchy depth. Source columns auto-hidden. group_by affect decimal alignment — values align globally across entire column regardless group boundaries. page_by creates separate alignment contexts (since pages physically separate). Style group header rows (bold, etc.) via fr_styles() targeting group header rows fr_rows_matches(). single-level label groups, match empty stat column (e.g., fr_rows_matches("total", "")). multi-level leaf hierarchies, match __row_level__ column (e.g., fr_rows_matches("__row_level__", "soc")). indent_by Row indentation specification. Accepts two forms: Simple (single level): character vector column name(s). detail rows (non-header, non-blank) columns receive one indent level. Typically used group_by SOC/PT tables: indent_by = "pt". Multi-level (SAS-style): named list three elements: key — column name containing row type markers (e.g., "row_type") col — column name(s) apply indent (e.g., "term") levels — named numeric vector mapping key values indent multipliers (e.g., c(soc = 0, hlt = 1, pt = 2)) indent level = 2 space-character widths (~0.17 9pt). Rows whose key value levels receive indent. blank_after Character vector column name(s). blank row inserted group boundary columns. group_keep Logical. Whether group_by groups kept together page via RTF \keepn / LaTeX keep--next. Default TRUE. Set FALSE visual-grouping (blank_after, indent) without page-keeping — useful long groups want renderer break freely. group_style Style properties group header rows (injected group_by label leaf). Accepts two forms: Uniform style (group headers): named list style properties: bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_size, align. Example: group_style = list(bold = TRUE, background = "#F0F0F0"). Per-level style (leaf hierarchies ): named list names match hierarchy column names values style property lists. Example: group_style = list(soc = list(bold = TRUE), hlt = list(italic = TRUE)). Levels listed receive special styling. group_style lowest precedence — explicit fr_styles() calls override . Set study-wide defaults via fr_theme() _arframe.yml. Alternatively, use rows = "group_headers" fr_row_style() fr_style() full styling control via fr_styles(). sort_by Character vector column name(s). Sorts data columns rendering. listings, controls display order (e.g., sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT")). Sorting applied finalize_spec(). suppress Character vector column name(s). Suppresses repeated consecutive values columns — first occurrence run displayed. Standard listings subject ID appears per block. Suppression applied finalize_spec(). wrap Logical. TRUE, enables text wrapping body cells. Default FALSE. listings long text fields (e.g., verbatim terms, medical history), wrapping prevents cell content overflowing.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: modified fr_spec. Row config stored spec$body.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: Standard pharma AE tables (MedDRA SOC/PT hierarchy) follow row conventions: Sort order: SOC rows sorted descending incidence across arms; within SOC, PT rows sorted descending incidence (alphabetical tiebreaker). standard regulatory sort order. "TOTAL SUBJECTS EVENT" always first summary row, first SOC. Pre-compute data frame. Subject counting: subject counted per SOC per PT, even multiple events. Document fr_footnotes(). Page breaks SOC: System Organ Class starts new page (page_by = "soc"). PT rows stay SOC header via group_by. Indentation: PT rows indented SOC header (indent_by = "pt_label"). Indent: 0.1667 (2 spaces / 240 twips).

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: demographics continuous endpoint tables, standard summary statistic row order : n, Mean, SD (SE), Median, Q1/Q3, Min/Max Pre-sort long-form data match order. Use blank_after insert visual separation characteristic blocks (e.g. Age Sex sections).

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: pagination engine respects orphan_min widow_min settings fr_page(). Orphan control prevents lone row stranded bottom page; widow control prevents lone row starting new page. apply within group_by groups: placing group leave fewer orphan_min rows page break, group moved next page instead.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: page_by columns automatically hidden render time — values appear group labels section header, need appear table body. Override fr_cols(col = fr_col(visible = TRUE)) needed (set fr_rows()). group_by, indent_by, etc. columns structural — hide via fr_cols(col = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) appear. Use group_by (page_by) want rows travel together necessarily start new page. blank_after simplest way add visual group separation without bold headers. Style page-labels group header rows via fr_styles() full control bold, alignment, formatting.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Configure Row Grouping and Pagination — fr_rows
code: ## ── AE table paginated by System Organ Class ─────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc  ## ── Demographics table: blank row after each group ────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(blank_after = "group") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Indented hierarchy (SOC bold header + indented PT rows) ──────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = "pt"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=soc, indent_by=pt  ## ── Combined: page_by + group_by + indent ──────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(soc = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     page_by   = "soc",     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = "pt"   ) #> Warning: `page_by` and `group_by` share column: "soc". #> ℹ `group_by` grouping is applied within each `page_by` page. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (5 visible of 7): #> pt "pt" 2.67in left #> placebo "placebo" 0.52in left #> zom_50mg "zom_50mg" 0.64in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.70in left #> total "total" 0.58in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc, group_by=soc, indent_by=pt  ## ── page_by with hidden label (page breaks only, no visible label) ───────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(page_by = list(cols = "soc", visible = FALSE)) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc (visible=FALSE)  ## ── Multi-level indent (SOC / HLT / PT hierarchy) ───────────────────── ## Uses a named list: key column determines indent level per row  # Given data with columns: soc, term, row_type, placebo, ... # where row_type is "soc", "hlt", or "pt" spec <- data.frame(   soc = c("GI disorders", "GI disorders", "GI disorders"),   term = c("GI disorders", "GI signs", "Nausea"),   row_type = c("soc", "hlt", "pt"),   result = c("72 (53.3)", "54 (40.0)", "24 (17.8)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(soc = fr_col(visible = FALSE),           row_type = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     group_by  = "soc",     indent_by = list(       key    = "row_type",       col    = "term",       levels = c(soc = 0, hlt = 1, pt = 2)     )   )  ## ── Multi-level hierarchy with leaf + bold SOC only ─────────────────  data.frame(   soc    = c("GI disorders", "GI disorders", "GI disorders",              "Nervous system", "Nervous system"),   pt     = c("Nausea", "Vomiting", "Diarrhoea",              "Headache", "Dizziness"),   total  = c("24 (17.8)", "18 (13.3)", "12 ( 8.9)",              "30 (22.2)", "15 (11.1)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     group_by    = list(cols = c("soc", "pt"), leaf = "pt"),     blank_after = "soc",     group_style = list(soc = list(bold = TRUE))   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 7 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=soc, indent_by=__row_level__,__display__,c(soc = 0, pt = 1)  ## ── group_by list form: auto-inject group headers ──────────────────── ## label injects header rows from group_by values into the named column. ## indent_by is auto-inferred — no need to specify both. ## ## Result: ##   stat           value ##   Sex                       <- header row (from group column) ##     Female       27 (60.0)  <- indented detail ##     Male         18 (40.0)  <- indented detail ## ##   Age                       <- header row ##     Mean (SD)    75.0 (6.8) <- indented detail ##     Median       74.0 ##     Min, Max     65, 88  data.frame(   group = c("Sex", "Sex", "Age", "Age", "Age"),   stat  = c("Female", "Male", "Mean (SD)", "Median", "Min, Max"),   value = c("27 (60.0)", "18 (40.0)", "75.0 (6.8)", "74.0", "65, 88"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     group_by = list(cols = "group", label = "stat")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 5 rows x 3 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (2 visible of 3): #> stat "stat" 0.62in left #> value "value" 0.55in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=group (label=stat), indent_by=stat  ## ── group_by with label + bold group headers ──────────────────────  data.frame(   PARAMCD    = c("ALB", "ALB", "ALT", "ALT"),   stat_label = c("High", "Low", "High", "Low"),   total      = c("6 ( 2.4)", "35 (13.8)", "30 (11.8)", "6 ( 2.4)"),   placebo    = c("4 ( 4.7)", "17 (19.8)", "9 (10.5)", "1 ( 1.2)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(PARAMCD = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) |>   fr_rows(     group_by    = list(cols = "PARAMCD", label = "stat_label"),     blank_after = "PARAMCD",     group_style = list(bold = TRUE)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 4 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (3 visible of 4): #> stat_label "stat_label" 0.58in left #> total "total" 0.52in left #> placebo "placebo" 0.52in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=PARAMCD (label=stat_label), indent_by=stat_label  ## ── Per-level styling (leaf hierarchies) ─────────────────────────────  data.frame(   soc   = c("GI disorders", "GI disorders",             "Nervous system", "Nervous system"),   pt    = c("Nausea", "Vomiting", "Headache", "Dizziness"),   total = c("24 (17.8)", "18 (13.3)", "30 (22.2)", "15 (11.1)"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     group_by    = list(cols = c("soc", "pt"), leaf = "pt"),     blank_after = "soc",     group_style = list(soc = list(bold = TRUE, background = "#F0F0F0"))   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 6 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=soc, indent_by=__row_level__,__display__,c(soc = 0, pt = 1)  ## ── sort_by: order a listing by subject and start date ────────────────  adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "ASTDT", "AESEV")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(sort_by = c("USUBJID", "ASTDT")) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 20 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,ASTDT  ## ── suppress: suppress repeated subject IDs in a listing ───────────  adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AESEV")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     sort_by     = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS"),     suppress = "USUBJID"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 20 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,AEBODSYS  ## ── wrap = TRUE: enable text wrapping for long verbatim terms ─────────  adae[1:10, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AEOUT")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(wrap = TRUE) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 10 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: wrap  ## ── Combined: sort_by + suppress on adverse event listing ──────────  adae[1:30, c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD", "AESEV", "ASTDT")] |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(     sort_by     = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS", "AEDECOD"),     suppress = c("USUBJID", "AEBODSYS"),     wrap        = TRUE   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 30 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: sort_by=USUBJID,AEBODSYS,AEDECOD, wrap

what: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
text: Creates row selector object use rows argument fr_row_style() fr_style(). style-application time (inside fr_styles()), selector evaluated table data frame produce matching row positions. avoids hard-coding integer row numbers styling rows content — example, bolding every "Total" row colouring every p-value row red. selector data-driven: rows reordered filtered, styles automatically track correct rows.

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
code: fr_rows_matches(col, value = NULL, pattern = NULL, ignore.case = FALSE)

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
text: col Character scalar. Name data column match . Must exist data frame passed fr_table(). value Scalar. Exact value match (using ==). Mutually exclusive pattern. Supports atomic type (character, numeric, logical). pattern Character scalar. regular expression passed grep() (Perl-compatible). Mutually exclusive value. regex matched character representation column values. ignore.case Logical. Whether pattern matching case-insensitive. Default FALSE. Ignored value used.

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
text: fr_rows_selector object use fr_row_style() fr_style().

title: Select Rows by Column Value or Pattern — fr_rows_matches
code: ## ── Bold every "Total" row ────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = fr_rows_matches("characteristic", "Total"), bold = TRUE)   ) #> Warning: `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "characteristic". #> ℹ Selector: value Total. #> ℹ Styles targeting these rows will have no effect. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Red text for p-value rows (regex) ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("characteristic", pattern = "^p[- .]?value",                               ignore.case = TRUE),       color = "#CC0000", italic = TRUE     )   ) #> Warning: `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "characteristic". #> ℹ Selector: pattern ^p[- .]?value. #> ℹ Styles targeting these rows will have no effect. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Combine with cell styles ──────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     # Shade every SOC header row     fr_row_style(rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", "soc"), background = "#F0F0F0", bold = TRUE),     # Red text for all PT rows with a p-value pattern     fr_row_style(rows = fr_rows_matches("row_type", "pt"), color = "#333333")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Alternation pattern: match "Total" or "Subtotal" ────────────────────  fr_rows_matches("characteristic", pattern = "Total|Subtotal") #> $col #> [1] "characteristic" #>  #> $value #> NULL #>  #> $pattern #> [1] "Total|Subtotal" #>  #> $ignore.case #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_rows_selector"  ## ── Character class pattern: rows starting with uppercase letter ───────  fr_rows_matches("characteristic", pattern = "^[A-Z]") #> $col #> [1] "characteristic" #>  #> $value #> NULL #>  #> $pattern #> [1] "^[A-Z]" #>  #> $ignore.case #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_rows_selector"  ## ── Combined with fr_style in a pipeline ──────────────────────────────────  tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(       rows = fr_rows_matches("category", pattern = "Completed|Discontinued"),       bold = TRUE, background = "#F0F0F0"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 8 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override

what: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: Sets number blank lines inserted table sections. gaps provide visual separation running header, titles, group label, column headers, body rows, footnotes, running footer. five controllable junctions table anatomy:   Calling fr_spacing() replaces specified values. Unspecified arguments keep current value.
code: [Page Header]   --- pagehead_after --- Titles   --- titles_after --- Page-by Label   --- page_by_after --- Column Headers / Body Rows   --- footnotes_before --- Footnotes   --- pagefoot_before --- [Page Footer]

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
code: fr_spacing(   spec,   titles_after = NULL,   footnotes_before = NULL,   pagehead_after = NULL,   pagefoot_before = NULL,   page_by_after = NULL )

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). titles_after Integer. Blank lines last title column header rule (page-label page_by set). Default 1L. Set 0L gap. footnotes_before Integer. Blank lines last body row (bottom rule) first footnote. Default 1L. Set 0L gap. pagehead_after Integer. Blank lines running page header first title. Default 0L (gap). Set 1L one blank line. takes effect page header set via fr_pagehead(). pagefoot_before Integer. Blank lines last footnote running page footer. Default 0L. Set 1L add breathing room footer. takes effect page footer set via fr_pagefoot(). page_by_after Integer. Blank lines page-label column header row. Default 1L. takes effect page_by set via fr_rows(). Set 0L gap.

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: modified fr_spec. Spacing stored spec$spacing.

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: Standard pharma submission tables (ICH E3, FDA/EMA) typically use one blank line titles footnotes. package defaults reflect minimal layout: titles_after = 1L — one blank line titles footnotes_before = 1L — one blank line footnotes pagehead_after = 0L — gap (user decides) pagefoot_before = 0L — gap (user decides) page_by_after = 1L — one blank line page-label

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: five spacing values can set _arframe.yml:
code: spacing:   titles_after: 1   footnotes_before: 1   pagehead_after: 0   pagefoot_before: 0   page_by_after: 1

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Control Spacing Between Table Sections — fr_spacing
code: ## ── Default spacing (1 blank line at each gap) ───────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics", "Safety Population") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on N in column header.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Safety Population" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── No gap after titles (compact layout) ─────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spacing(titles_after = 0L) |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Two blank lines before footnotes ─────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spacing(footnotes_before = 2L) |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on N in column header.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on N in column header."  ## ── Space after group label (page_by) ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc") |>   fr_spacing(page_by_after = 1L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc  ## ── Space before page footer ──────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}") |>   fr_spacing(pagefoot_before = 1L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── All five spacing parameters in one call ──────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events by SOC") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] MedDRA v26.1.") |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc") |>   fr_pagehead(left = "TFRM-2024-001") |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}") |>   fr_spacing(     titles_after     = 1L,     footnotes_before = 1L,     pagehead_after   = 1L,     pagefoot_before  = 1L,     page_by_after    = 1L   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events by SOC" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] MedDRA v26.1."  ## ── Tight layout: zero spacing everywhere ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") |>   fr_footnotes("[a] N = number of subjects.") |>   fr_spacing(     titles_after     = 0L,     footnotes_before = 0L,     pagehead_after   = 0L,     pagefoot_before  = 0L,     page_by_after    = 0L   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] N = number of subjects."

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Adds one spanning headers — labels sit group columns additional header row. named argument creates one span: argument name span label; value character vector column names covered span. Calling fr_spans() appends spans already defined. Call multiple times different .level values build multi-level spanning headers.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
code: fr_spans(spec, ..., .level = 1L, .hline = TRUE, .gap = NULL)

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... Named column selections. name span label; value either character vector column names tidyselect expression: Character vector: "Treatment" = c("col1", "col2") Tidyselect: "Treatment" = starts_with("zom_") Supported helpers: tidyselect::starts_with(), tidyselect::ends_with(), tidyselect::contains(), tidyselect::matches(), . .level Integer. Vertical level span row (1L = immediately column header row; higher numbers ). Default 1L. .hline Logical. Whether draw thin horizontal line spanned columns. Default TRUE. Set FALSE suppress border. .gap Logical NULL. Whether insert narrow gap columns adjacent spanning headers level. Gap columns create clean visual break span groups RTF PDF output, without relying border trimming. NULL (default) inherits current setting (default TRUE). Set FALSE disable gap insertion produce continuous span hlines.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: modified fr_spec. Spans appended spec$header$spans.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Standard pharma house styles recommend: Use spanning headers necessary. Avoid simple two-arm tables column label already identifies treatment group. used, span label typically carries treatment arm name timepoint period, individual column labels carry dose level N (e.g. "10 mg\n(N=45)"). Common patterns: Single-level span grouping dose arms compound name: "Zomerane" = c("low_dose", "high_dose") Two-level span: dose arms level 1, compound level 2. Timepoint spans: "Week 12" = c("w12_n", "w12_pct").

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Build inner outer (lowest level first):   Level 1 spans sit directly column labels; level 2 spans sit level 1.
code: spec |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("low_n", "high_n"), .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("Active Treatment" = c("low_n", "high_n"), .level = 2L)

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
text: Span labels support {fr_*()} inline markup (e.g. "{fr_bold('Treatment')}"). column can appear one span per level. Overlapping spans level produce undefined layout behaviour — avoid . Columns covered span given level display empty cell span row. .hline border helps visually separate span column labels . Set .hline = FALSE spanning purely decorative (e.g. grouping timepoints without visible divider). Span alignment inherits fr_header() align set, otherwise defaults center.

title: Add Spanning Column Headers — fr_spans
code: ## ── Single-level treatment group span ────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic",    width = 2.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("50 mg",             width = 1.5, align = "right"),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("100 mg",            width = 1.5, align = "right"),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo",            width = 1.5, align = "right"),     total          = fr_col("Total",              width = 1.5, align = "right"),     .n = c(zom_50mg = 45, zom_100mg = 45, placebo = 45, total = 135),     .n_format = "{label}\n(N={n})"   ) |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (6 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo" 1.50in right #> zom_50mg "50 mg" 1.50in right #> zom_100mg "100 mg" 1.50in right #> total "Total" 1.50in right #> group "group" 0.62in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Two-level spanning header ─────────────────────────────────────────────  # Build a small example with dose-level columns dose_data <- data.frame(   param = "Weight", low_n = "43", low_pct = "31.9%",   high_n = "47", high_pct = "34.8%" ) dose_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans("10 mg"    = c("low_n",  "low_pct"),  .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("25 mg"    = c("high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 1L) |>   fr_spans("Zomerane" = c("low_n",  "low_pct",                            "high_n", "high_pct"), .level = 2L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 1 row x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 3  ## ── Markup in span label ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "{fr_bold('Zomerane')}" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Span without bottom border (.hline = FALSE) ────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),     .hline = FALSE   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Single-column span (acts as a group label above one column) ────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),     "Reference" = "placebo"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 2  ## ── Tidyselect: span columns by pattern ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = starts_with("zom_")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Spans: 1  ## ── Span + fr_header bold interaction ──────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE) |>   fr_spans(     "Zomerane" = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: bold, valign=bottom #> Spans: 1

what: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: Creates cell-level style object use fr_styles(). Targets individual cells row column position (region keyword). properties explicitly set override base style; NULL means "inherit base style". row-level column-level styling, prefer fr_row_style() fr_col_style() simpler interface uniform row/column formatting.

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
code: fr_style(   region = "body",   rows = NULL,   cols = NULL,   bold = NULL,   italic = NULL,   underline = NULL,   color = NULL,   background = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL,   indent = NULL,   colspan = NULL,   rowspan = NULL )

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
