text: region Region target. One : "body" (default) — data body rows. "header" — column header row(s) spanner row(s). "stub" — first (row-label) column across body rows. multiple stub columns, use region = "body" cols = c("lab_name", "visit") instead. rows Integer vector row positions, "" (rows region), NULL (rows). Row indices 1-based relative region. Multiple indices supported: rows = c(1L, 3L, 5L). cols Column selection. Accepts : character vector column names: cols = c("zom_50mg", "placebo") tidyselect expression: cols = starts_with("zom_") NULL (default) — targets columns. Tidyselect expressions captured unevaluated resolved style applied via fr_styles(). means can create style objects tidyselect expressions data available. Supported helpers: tidyselect::starts_with(), tidyselect::ends_with(), tidyselect::contains(), tidyselect::matches(), tidyselect::everything(), tidyselect::(), . bold, italic, underline Logical NULL inherit. color Foreground (text) colour: hex string ("#003366") 148 CSS named colours ("navy", "steelblue", "tomato", etc.). NULL inherit. background Background (fill) colour: hex string CSS named colour. NULL inherit. font_size Font size points, NULL inherit. align Horizontal alignment override: "left", "center", "right", "decimal", NULL. Controls text flows within cell (left--right positioning). RTF maps \ql, \qc, \qr; LaTeX l, c, r column types. See Alignment model . valign Vertical alignment override: "top", "middle", "bottom", NULL. Controls content sits within cell row taller content (e.g., due multi-line labels adjacent cells). RTF maps \clvertalt, \clvertalc, \clvertalb; LaTeX p (top), m (middle), b (bottom). See Alignment model . indent Indentation inches, NULL. colspan Integer. Number columns cell spans (horizontal merge). NULL = span. rowspan Integer. Number rows cell spans (vertical merge). NULL = span.

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: fr_cell_style object use fr_styles().

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
text: arframe separates alignment two orthogonal axes, following model RTF LaTeX/tabularray: align (horizontal) paragraph-level property — controls line text positioned within cell width. cells need . valign (vertical) cell-level property — controls content block sits cell taller needed. matters rows unequal content height (e.g., single-line label next multi-line label). single-line body rows, valign visible effect. combine freely: cell can align = "right", valign = "bottom" (text right-aligned, sitting bottom tall row).

title: Define a Cell Style Override — fr_style
code: ## ── Style objects are standalone — create, inspect, reuse ──────────────  # Create style objects independently header_bold <- fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE) total_background    <- fr_style(cols = "total", background = "aliceblue")  # Inspect the object str(header_bold) #> List of 17 #>  $ type      : chr "cell" #>  $ region    : chr "header" #>  $ rows      : NULL #>  $ cols      : NULL #>  $ bold      : logi TRUE #>  $ italic    : NULL #>  $ underline : NULL #>  $ color     : NULL #>  $ background: NULL #>  $ font      : NULL #>  $ font_size : NULL #>  $ align     : NULL #>  $ valign    : NULL #>  $ indent    : NULL #>  $ colspan   : NULL #>  $ rowspan   : NULL #>  $ height    : NULL #>  - attr(*, "class")= chr "fr_cell_style"  # Apply to any spec via fr_styles() tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(header_bold, total_background) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides  # Reuse the same styles across different tables tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(header_bold, total_background) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 8 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Bold the entire column header ────────────────────────────────────────  fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "header" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Red text for specific body rows ──────────────────────────────────────  fr_style(region = "body", rows = c(1L, 3L), color = "#CC0000") #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 1 3 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> [1] "#CC0000" #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── CSS named colour (148 colours available) ──────────────────────────── fr_style(cols = "total", background = "aliceblue") #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> [1] "total" #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#F0F8FF" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style" fr_style(region = "body", rows = 3L, color = "crimson", bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> [1] "#DC143C" #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Highlight the Total column header ────────────────────────────────────  fr_style(region = "header", cols = "total", background = "#D0E4FF", bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "header" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> [1] "total" #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#D0E4FF" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Stub column: bold row labels ─────────────────────────────────────────  fr_style(region = "stub", bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "stub" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> NULL #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Tidyselect: style columns by pattern ───────────────────────────────────  # Use tidyselect helpers instead of hard-coding column names: fr_style(cols = starts_with("zom_"), background = "#F5F5F5") #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> <quosure> #> expr: ^starts_with("zom_") #> env:  0x612becc27400 #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#F5F5F5" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style" fr_style(cols = contains("mg"), italic = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> <quosure> #> expr: ^contains("mg") #> env:  0x612becc27400 #>  #> $bold #> NULL #>  #> $italic #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Multi-stub layout (e.g. multiple row-label columns) ──────────────────  # The "stub" region targets exactly one column (the first dataset column). # When your table has multiple row-label columns (like System Organ Class # followed by Preferred Term), target them by name using `region = "body"`: fr_style(region = "body", cols = c("soc", "pt"), align = "left", bold = TRUE) #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "body" #>  #> $rows #> NULL #>  #> $cols #> [1] "soc" "pt"  #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> NULL #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> [1] "left" #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> NULL #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"  ## ── Merged spanning title cell ────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_style(region = "header", rows = 1L, cols = "characteristic",          colspan = 5L, bold = TRUE, background = "#F0F0F0") #> $type #> [1] "cell" #>  #> $region #> [1] "header" #>  #> $rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $cols #> [1] "characteristic" #>  #> $bold #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $italic #> NULL #>  #> $underline #> NULL #>  #> $color #> NULL #>  #> $background #> [1] "#F0F0F0" #>  #> $font #> NULL #>  #> $font_size #> NULL #>  #> $align #> NULL #>  #> $valign #> NULL #>  #> $indent #> NULL #>  #> $colspan #> [1] 5 #>  #> $rowspan #> NULL #>  #> $height #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fr_cell_style"

what: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: Diagnostic tool reports cell styles affect specific cell table body, order applied, final resolved properties. Use cell look expected rendered output — shows exactly style layers active combine.

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
code: fr_style_explain(spec, row, col)

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). row Integer scalar. Body row index (1-based). col Character scalar integer. Column name column index.

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: Invisibly returns list : $final — named list resolved properties (bold, italic, color, background, align, valign, indent, font_size). $layers — ordered list matching styles index spec$cell_styles, type, overridden properties. Prints human-readable summary console showing: cell content value. matching style layer index, type (col/row/cell), properties sets. final resolved properties layers applied.

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
text: [1], [2] indices refer position spec$cell_styles. Properties later layers override earlier ones. example, column style sets background; row style adds bold.
code: -- Style explain: row 1, col "total" -- Content: "135" 2 matching styles:   [1] col: background="#EBF5FB"   [2] row: bold=TRUE  Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, color=#000000, background=#EBF5FB, ...

title: Explain Style Resolution for a Cell — fr_style_explain
code: spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB"),     fr_row_style(rows = 1L, bold = TRUE)   )  # See which styles affect row 1, column "total" fr_style_explain(spec, row = 1L, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total"  #> Content: "135" #> 2 matching styles: #> [1] col: background="#EBF5FB" #> [2] row: bold=TRUE #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, color=#000000, background=#EBF5FB, align=left, #> valign=top, indent=0  # Programmatic access to the resolved properties result <- fr_style_explain(spec, row = 1L, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total"  #> Content: "135" #> 2 matching styles: #> [1] col: background="#EBF5FB" #> [2] row: bold=TRUE #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=FALSE, color=#000000, background=#EBF5FB, align=left, #> valign=top, indent=0 result$final$bold   # TRUE (from row style) #> [1] TRUE result$final$background     # "#EBF5FB" (from col style) #> [1] "#EBF5FB"  ## ── Multiple overlapping styles: see precedence in action ─────────────────  spec2 <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB"),     fr_row_style(rows = 1L, background = "#FFF3CD", bold = TRUE),     fr_style(region = "body", rows = 1L, cols = "total",              color = "#CC0000", italic = TRUE)   )  # Cell (1, "total") has three overlapping layers — cell wins for color/italic, # row wins for background/bold (narrower scope), col style is overridden: fr_style_explain(spec2, row = 1L, col = "total") #>  #> ── Style explain: row 1, col "total"  #> Content: "135" #> 3 matching styles: #> [1] col: background="#EBF5FB" #> [2] row: bold=TRUE, background="#FFF3CD" #> [3] cell: italic=TRUE, color="#CC0000" #>  #> Final: bold=TRUE, italic=TRUE, color=#CC0000, background=#FFF3CD, align=left, #> valign=top, indent=0  ## ── Inspect a header cell ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  spec3 <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, background = "#E0E0E0"),     fr_style(region = "header", cols = "total", background = "#D0E4FF")   )  # Note: fr_style_explain inspects body cells. For header-region styles, # review spec3$cell_styles directly: str(spec3$cell_styles) #> List of 2 #>  $ :List of 17 #>   ..$ type      : chr "cell" #>   ..$ region    : chr "header" #>   ..$ rows      : NULL #>   ..$ cols      : NULL #>   ..$ bold      : logi TRUE #>   ..$ italic    : NULL #>   ..$ underline : NULL #>   ..$ color     : NULL #>   ..$ background: chr "#E0E0E0" #>   ..$ font      : NULL #>   ..$ font_size : NULL #>   ..$ align     : NULL #>   ..$ valign    : NULL #>   ..$ indent    : NULL #>   ..$ colspan   : NULL #>   ..$ rowspan   : NULL #>   ..$ height    : NULL #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "fr_cell_style" #>  $ :List of 17 #>   ..$ type      : chr "cell" #>   ..$ region    : chr "header" #>   ..$ rows      : NULL #>   ..$ cols      : chr "total" #>   ..$ bold      : NULL #>   ..$ italic    : NULL #>   ..$ underline : NULL #>   ..$ color     : NULL #>   ..$ background: chr "#D0E4FF" #>   ..$ font      : NULL #>   ..$ font_size : NULL #>   ..$ align     : NULL #>   ..$ valign    : NULL #>   ..$ indent    : NULL #>   ..$ colspan   : NULL #>   ..$ rowspan   : NULL #>   ..$ height    : NULL #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr "fr_cell_style"

what: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
text: Creates data-driven style evaluated cell values application time (inside fr_styles()). Rows matching condition receive specified style properties. avoids hard-coding row indices styling based content.

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
code: fr_style_if(   condition,   cols = NULL,   apply_to = "cell",   bold = NULL,   italic = NULL,   underline = NULL,   color = NULL,   background = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   align = NULL,   valign = NULL )

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
text: condition one-sided formula function returns logical vector. formula uses .x pronoun column values (converted via rlang::as_function()): ~ .x == "Total" — match exact text ~ .numeric(.x) < 0.05 — numeric comparison ~ grepl("^N=", .x) — pattern matching cols = NULL, .x receives seq_len(nrow(data)) (row indices), useful zebra striping: ~ (.x %% 2) == 0. cols Column selection condition. Accepts : character vector column names: cols = c("zom_50mg", "placebo") tidyselect expression: cols = starts_with("zom_") NULL (default) — row-index-based conditions (e.g., zebra striping). condition applied values selected column independently. Tidyselect expressions resolved style applied via fr_styles(). apply_to Character scalar. Controls scope matched rows: "cell" (default): style cells condition TRUE. "row": style columns rows condition TRUE. bold, italic, underline Logical NULL leave unchanged. color Foreground (text) colour: hex string CSS named colour, NULL. background Background (fill) colour: hex string CSS named colour, NULL. font_size Font size points, NULL. align Horizontal alignment ("left", "center", "right", "decimal"), NULL. valign Vertical alignment ("top", "middle", "bottom"), NULL. See fr_style() Alignment model.

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
text: fr_conditional_style object use fr_styles().

title: Create a Conditional Style Override — fr_style_if
code: ## ── Bold "Total" rows ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "characteristic",       condition = ~ .x == "Total",       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Zebra striping ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       condition = ~ (.x %% 2) == 0,       background = "#F5F5F5",       apply_to = "row"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Numeric condition: highlight small p-values ──────────────────────────  # Create a table with a p-value column pval_data <- data.frame(   characteristic = c("Age", "Sex", "Weight"),   treatment = c("50 (23.5)", "30 (14.1)", "45 (21.1)"),   placebo   = c("55 (25.8)", "28 (13.1)", "52 (24.4)"),   pvalue    = c("0.042", "0.310", "0.003"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  pval_data |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "pvalue",       condition = ~ as.numeric(.x) < 0.05,       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, color = "#CC0000"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 3 rows x 4 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── Pattern matching with grepl ────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "soc",       condition = ~ grepl("SKIN|GASTROINTESTINAL", .x, ignore.case = TRUE),       apply_to = "row",       background = "#FFF3CD"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 1 override  ## ── apply_to = "cell": colour only the matching cell ───────────────────────  tbl_disp |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = c("placebo", "zom_50mg", "zom_100mg"),       condition = ~ grepl("0", .x),       apply_to = "cell",       color = "#999999", italic = TRUE     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 8 rows x 5 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 3 overrides  ## ── Function form (not formula) for the condition ──────────────────────────  is_total <- function(x) x == "Total"  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = "characteristic",       condition = is_total,       apply_to = "row",       bold = TRUE, background = "#E8E8E8"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Multiple cols: evaluate condition on several columns ───────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_style_if(       cols = c("placebo", "zom_50mg", "zom_100mg", "total"),       condition = ~ grepl("^0", .x),       apply_to = "cell",       color = "#999999"     )   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides

what: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: Appends one style override objects — created fr_style(), fr_row_style(), fr_col_style() — table specification. Multiple calls fr_styles() accumulate: styles applied order, later styles overriding earlier ones target cells.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
code: fr_styles(spec, ...)

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... One style objects created fr_style(), fr_row_style(), fr_col_style().

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: modified fr_spec. Styles appended spec$cell_styles.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: Styles applied order passed. precedence (lowest highest) : Column styles (fr_col_style) — widest scope, applied first. Row styles (fr_row_style) — override column styles. Cell styles (fr_style) — narrowest scope, applied last. Within type, later styles override earlier ones cell properties. means can set broad default selectively override specific cells.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: idiomatic pass styles single fr_styles() call, ordering broad specific. fr_styles() appends — call fr_table() start fresh discard accumulated styles. number styles negligible performance impact — render engine applies single pass cell grid.

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
text: multiple styles target cell, later styles fr_styles() call override earlier ones. Scope precedence: fr_col_style() < fr_row_style() < fr_style() (cell-level)

title: Apply Style Overrides to a Table — fr_styles
code: ## ── Create reusable style objects, then apply ───────────────────────────── header_style <- fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, background = "lavender") total_col    <- fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "aliceblue") bold_totals  <- fr_row_style(   rows = fr_rows_matches("characteristic", "Total"),   bold = TRUE )  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(header_style, total_col, bold_totals) #> Warning: `fr_rows_matches()`: no rows matched in column "characteristic". #> ℹ Selector: value Total. #> ℹ Styles targeting these rows will have no effect. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 3 overrides  ## ── Bold header + highlighted Total column ────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE),     fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Zebra striping (alternating row shading) ─────────────────────────────  n <- nrow(tbl_demog) odd_rows  <- seq(1, n, by = 2) even_rows <- seq(2, n, by = 2)  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = odd_rows,  background = "#FFFFFF"),     fr_row_style(rows = even_rows, background = "#F5F5F5")   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Multi-layer: col → row → cell ────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(     # Broad: light background for all data columns     fr_col_style(cols = c("zom_50mg", "zom_100mg", "placebo"), background = "#FAFAFA"),     # Mid: bold entire header     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE),     # Narrow: red text for high-risk row     fr_style(region = "body", rows = 3L, color = "#CC0000", bold = TRUE)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Styles: 3 overrides  ## ── Stub column bold + header background ─────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("box") |>   fr_styles(     fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE, background = "#E8E8E8"),     fr_style(region = "stub",   bold = TRUE)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Styles: 2 overrides  ## ── Accumulating styles across multiple calls ─────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_styles(fr_style(region = "header", bold = TRUE))  # Later: add Total column highlight (accumulates, does not replace) spec <- spec |>   fr_styles(fr_col_style(cols = "total", background = "#EBF5FB"))

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: Renders content subscript final output. Use inside glue-string expressions titles, footnotes, column labels, cell values. Common clinical statistics chemical formulas (H₂O), statistical notation (beta-1), indexed variables (x_i).

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
code: fr_sub(x)

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: x Character numeric. Content render subscript. Coerced character internally.

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string, produces sentinel token resolved render time.

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
text: RTF output, subscript uses \sub (half font size, lowered). future LaTeX output, maps \textsubscript{}. subscript text renders roughly half surrounding font size.

title: Subscript Markup — fr_sub
code: # Chemical formula: H₂O fr_col("H{fr_sub(2)}O", width = 1.0) #> <fr_col> "H{fr_sub(2)}O" [1.00in, left]  # Statistical subscript: x_i fr_col("x{fr_sub('i')}", width = 1.0) #> <fr_col> "x{fr_sub('i')}" [1.00in, left]  # In a footnote: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes("{fr_sub('n')} = number of subjects with data") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "n = number of subjects with data"

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
text: Renders content superscript final output. Use inside glue-string expressions titles, footnotes, column labels, cell values. render time backend converts markup appropriate format (RTF control words LaTeX \textsuperscript{}). common use regulatory tables footnote markers: "[]{fr_super('')}" cell value, paired "[] Percentages based N." fr_footnotes().

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
code: fr_super(x)

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
text: x Character numeric. Content render superscript. Coerced character internally.

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
text: fr_markup object. interpolated inside glue string (e.g. "{fr_super(1)}"), produces sentinel token (\x01SUPER:1\x02) resolved render time.

title: Superscript Markup — fr_super
code: # Footnote marker fr_super("a") #> <fr_markup> ^{a}  # Unit with exponent: kg/m² fr_col("BMI kg/m{fr_super(2)}", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "BMI kg/m{fr_super(2)}" [1.50in, left]  # Standalone usage (inspect the sentinel) format(fr_super(1)) #> [1] "\001SUPER:1\002"  # In a pipeline: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() n <- 1 spec |> fr_footnotes("{fr_super(n)} Pearson chi-square test") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "1 Pearson chi-square test"

what: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: fr_table() entry point every arframe pipeline. wraps presentation-ready data frame fr_spec object, configure fr_*() verbs finally render fr_render(). data frame already contain rows columns want display — arframe summarise reshape data. Use packages gt, Tplyr, rtables, tidytlg, tfrmt summarise data, hand summary data frame fr_table().

title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
code: fr_table(data)

title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: data data frame (tibble). Must already presentation-ready: row maps one table row, column one table column. summarisation performed arframe.

title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: fr_spec object. Pass fr_*() verb via |>.

title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
code: data |>   fr_table()                        # start   |> fr_titles(...)                 # titles above the table   |> fr_footnotes(...)              # footnotes below   |> fr_cols(col = fr_col(...), .n = ...) # columns / N-counts   |> fr_header(bold = TRUE, align = .)   # header presentation   |> fr_spans(...)                  # spanning headers   |> fr_rows(page_by = ...)         # pagination / grouping   |> fr_page(orientation = ...)     # page layout   |> fr_pagehead(left = ...)        # running header   |> fr_pagefoot(right = ...)       # running footer   |> fr_hlines("header")         # horizontal rules   |> fr_vlines("box")               # vertical rules   |> fr_styles(fr_row_style(...))   # cell / row / column styling   |> fr_render("output.rtf")        # render to file

title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
text: fr_*() verb can called multiple times; replace (append) previous setting. Exceptions: fr_spans() fr_styles() append repeated calls. Column order rendered table matches column order data. Reorder columns data calling fr_table() needed. Invisible columns (e.g. grouping keys) can hidden via fr_cols(col = fr_col(visible = FALSE)) rather dropping data.

title: Start a arframe Table Pipeline — fr_table
code:
## ── Minimal: one-step pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |> fr_table() #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Typical regulatory pipeline ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) |>   fr_footnotes("[a] Percentages based on the number of subjects in each arm.") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Charact..." #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on the number of subjects in each arm."  ## ── AE by SOC/PT with pagination ─────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",             "Safety Analysis Set") |>   fr_rows(page_by = "soc", group_by = "soc") |>   fr_hlines("header") #> Warning: `page_by` and `group_by` share column: "soc". #> ℹ `group_by` grouping is applied within each `page_by` page. #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Pre..." #> 2. [center] "Safety Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: page_by=soc, group_by=soc #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Store spec, reuse, branch ────────────────────────────────────────────  base_spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9)  # Branch A: portrait with smaller font spec_a <- base_spec |> fr_page(orientation = "portrait", font_size = 8)  # Branch B: add titles and render spec_b <- base_spec |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics") |>   fr_hlines("booktabs")  ## ── Complete 10-verb pipeline ───────────────────────────────────────────── # Demonstrates every major pipeline verb in a single specification. # In study programs, hlines/header/pagehead/pagefoot go in fr_theme().  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) |>   fr_footnotes(     "[a] Percentages based on subjects in each arm.",     "Source: ADSL"   ) |>   fr_cols(     characteristic = fr_col("Characteristic", width = 2.5),     placebo        = fr_col("Placebo
(N=45)", width = 1.5),     zom_50mg       = fr_col("Zomerane 50mg
(N=45)", width = 1.5),     zom_100mg      = fr_col("Zomerane 100mg
(N=45)", width = 1.5),     total          = fr_col("Total
(N=135)", width = 1.5),     group          = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_header(bold = TRUE, background = "#D9E2F3") |>   fr_spans("Treatment Arm" = c("placebo", "zom_50mg", "zom_100mg")) |>   fr_rows(group_by = "group", blank_after = "group", indent_by = "group") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("inner") |>   fr_styles(     fr_row_style(rows = 1, bold = TRUE)   ) |>   fr_page(orientation = "landscape", font_size = 9) |>   fr_pagehead(left = "Study TFRM-2024-001", right = "Page {thepage} of {total_pages}") |>   fr_pagefoot(left = "{program}", right = "{datetime}") |>   fr_spacing(titles_after = 1, footnotes_before = 1) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Charact..." #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Columns (5 visible of 6): #> characteristic "Characteristic" 2.50in left #> placebo "Placebo (N=45)" 1.50in left #> zom_50mg "Zomerane 50mg (N=45)" 1.50in left #> zom_100mg "Zomerane 100mg (N..." 1.50in left #> total "Total (N=135)" 1.50in left #> Header: bold, valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=group, indent_by=group #> Rules: 1 hline(s) #> Spans: 1 #> Styles: 1 override #> Footnotes (2): #> 1. [left] "[a] Percentages based on subjects in each arm." #> 2. [left] "Source: ADSL"  ## ── fr_table() vs fr_listing() on the same data ────────────────────────── # fr_table() uses auto-detected defaults; fr_listing() sets listing defaults.  ae_subset <- adae[1:20, c("USUBJID", "AEDECOD", "AESEV", "ASTDT")]  # Table view: auto-detected alignment, 9pt, no split spec_tbl <- ae_subset |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table View of AE Records") spec_tbl$page$font_size    # 9 (default) #> [1] 9  # Listing view: left-aligned, 8pt, split = TRUE, wrap = TRUE spec_lst <- ae_subset |>   fr_listing() |>   fr_titles("Listing View of AE Records") spec_lst$page$font_size          # 8 (listing default) #> [1] 8 spec_lst$columns_meta$split      # TRUE (listing default) #> [1] TRUE  ## ── Hidden column used for grouping ────────────────────────────────────── # The "group" column drives indent_by but is not displayed.  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_cols(     group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   fr_rows(indent_by = "group") |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics (grouped, hidden key)") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (1): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics (grouped, hidden key)" #> Columns (5 visible of 6): #> characteristic "characteristic" 1.85in left #> placebo "placebo" 0.61in left #> zom_50mg "zom_50mg" 0.64in left #> zom_100mg "zom_100mg" 0.70in left #> total "total" 0.61in left #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: indent_by=group

what: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: fr_theme() sets study-level defaults automatically applied every subsequent fr_table() call. Define font size, page layout, tokens, running header/footer, line rules top program; tables inherit settings without repeating .

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
code: fr_theme(   orientation = NULL,   paper = NULL,   margins = NULL,   col_gap = NULL,   font_family = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   space_mode = NULL,   split = NULL,   stub = NULL,   pagehead = NULL,   pagefoot = NULL,   tokens = NULL,   hlines = NULL,   vlines = NULL,   spacing = NULL,   n_format = NULL,   continuation = NULL,   group_keep = NULL,   group_style = NULL,   page_by_style = NULL,   header = NULL,   footnote_separator = NULL )  fr_theme_set(   orientation = NULL,   paper = NULL,   margins = NULL,   col_gap = NULL,   font_family = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   space_mode = NULL,   split = NULL,   stub = NULL,   pagehead = NULL,   pagefoot = NULL,   tokens = NULL,   hlines = NULL,   vlines = NULL,   spacing = NULL,   n_format = NULL,   continuation = NULL,   group_keep = NULL,   group_style = NULL,   page_by_style = NULL,   header = NULL,   footnote_separator = NULL )

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: orientation "landscape" "portrait". NULL leaves unchanged. paper "letter", "a4", "legal". NULL leaves unchanged. margins Margin(s) inches (formats fr_page()). NULL leaves unchanged. col_gap Inter-column padding points (integer). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_page() details. font_family Font family name. NULL leaves unchanged. font_size Font size points. NULL leaves unchanged. space_mode handle leading spaces cell data. One "indent" (convert paragraph-level indent) "preserve" (keep literal spaces). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_cols() .space_mode parameter details. split Logical. TRUE enable column splitting wide tables, FALSE disable. NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_cols() .split parameter details. stub Character vector column names mark stub columns (repeated every panel column splitting). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_col() stub parameter details. pagehead Named list left, center, right, font_size, bold elements — fr_pagehead() arguments. NULL leaves unchanged. pagefoot Named list left, center, right, font_size, bold elements — fr_pagefoot() arguments. NULL leaves unchanged. tokens Named list {token} values. NULL leaves unchanged. hlines Horizontal rule preset string (e.g. "header"). NULL leaves unchanged. vlines Vertical rule preset string (e.g. "box"). NULL leaves unchanged. spacing Named list titles_after, footnotes_before, pagehead_after, pagefoot_before, page_by_after (integer blank lines). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_spacing(). n_format glue-style format string N-count labels. Applied tables use fr_col(n = ...) fr_cols(.n = ...) without explicit .n_format. Available tokens: {label} {n}. NULL leaves unchanged. continuation Character scalar appended column headers continuation pages (e.g. "(continued)"). NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_page() details. group_keep Logical. Whether group_by groups kept together page. NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_rows() details. group_style Named list style properties group header rows. Applied tables use group_by label leaf. NULL leaves unchanged. See fr_rows() details examples. page_by_style Named list style properties page_by section labels. Applied tables use fr_rows(page_by = ...). Supports: bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_size, align. NULL leaves unchanged. Page_by labels plain text default. Example: page_by_style = list(bold = TRUE, align = "left"). header Named list header defaults. Supports fr_header() parameters: bold, align, valign, background, color, font_size, repeat_on_page, plus span_gap (logical, insert gap columns adjacent spans, default TRUE). NULL leaves unchanged. footnote_separator Logical. Whether draw separator footnote block. NULL leaves unchanged.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: follows pattern ggplot2 theme management. Calling fr_theme(...) (alias fr_theme_set(...)) merges new settings current global theme without discarding keys mention. analogous ggplot2::theme_update(). Use fr_theme_reset() clear settings return built-defaults, fr_theme_get() inspect current state.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
text: fr_table() called, automatically applies stored theme new fr_spec. can still override setting per-table calling corresponding verb fr_table(). Per-table overrides always win. Theme settings stored package's internal environment persist duration R session. Call fr_theme_reset() clear settings fr_theme_get() inspect current theme.

title: Set or Update Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme
code: ## ── Define study theme at the top of your program ────────────────────────  fr_theme(   orientation = "landscape",   paper       = "letter",   font_size   = 9,   tokens      = list(study = "TFRM-2024-001", cutoff = "31DEC2024"),   pagehead    = list(     left  = "Study: {study}",     right = "Database Cutoff: {cutoff}"   ),   pagefoot    = list(     left  = "{program}",     right = "{datetime}"   ),   hlines = "header",   vlines = "box" )  # All subsequent fr_table() calls inherit the theme — no need to repeat tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.1.1 Demographics", "Full Analysis Set") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events", "Safety Analysis Set") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events" #> 2. [center] "Safety Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Set section spacing (blank lines between table parts) ─────────────────  fr_theme(spacing = list(titles_after = 1L, footnotes_before = 1L))  ## ── Merge additional settings (does not discard existing ones) ────────────  fr_theme(font_size = 9, hlines = "header") fr_theme(vlines = "box")          # adds vlines; font_size and hlines kept fr_theme_get()                    # inspect #> $orientation #> [1] "landscape" #>  #> $paper #> [1] "letter" #>  #> $font_size #> [1] 9 #>  #> $pagehead #> $pagehead$left #> [1] "Study: {study}" #>  #> $pagehead$right #> [1] "Database Cutoff: {cutoff}" #>  #>  #> $pagefoot #> $pagefoot$left #> [1] "{program}" #>  #> $pagefoot$right #> [1] "{datetime}" #>  #>  #> $tokens #> $tokens$study #> [1] "TFRM-2024-001" #>  #> $tokens$cutoff #> [1] "31DEC2024" #>  #>  #> $hlines #> [1] "header" #>  #> $vlines #> [1] "box" #>  #> $spacing #> $spacing$titles_after #> [1] 1 #>  #> $spacing$footnotes_before #> [1] 1 #>  #>   ## ── Override a theme setting for one specific table ───────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_page(orientation = "portrait")   # overrides the landscape theme default #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: portrait letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  ## ── Theme with spacing configuration ──────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(   font_size = 9,   spacing   = list(titles_after = 2L, footnotes_before = 2L,                    pagehead_after = 1L) ) fr_theme_get()$spacing   # check the spacing settings #> $titles_after #> [1] 2 #>  #> $footnotes_before #> [1] 2 #>  #> $pagehead_after #> [1] 1 #>   ## ── Theme with leading-space handling ────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(space_mode = "indent")   # default: leading spaces → paragraph indent fr_theme_get()$space_mode         # "indent" #> [1] "indent"  fr_theme(space_mode = "preserve") # keep leading spaces as literal characters fr_theme_get()$space_mode         # "preserve" #> [1] "preserve"  ## ── Theme with footnote_separator ────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(footnote_separator = FALSE, hlines = "header") fr_theme_get()$footnote_separator   # FALSE #> [1] FALSE  ## ── Theme auto-applied: inspect spec after fr_table() ────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme(font_size = 8, orientation = "portrait", hlines = "header") spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec$page$font_size      # 8 (inherited from theme) #> [1] 8 spec$page$orientation    # "portrait" (inherited from theme) #> [1] "portrait"  ## ── Reset all theme settings ──────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset()

what: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
text: Returns current study-level theme named list. Use inspect defaults set via fr_theme().

title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
code: fr_theme_get()

title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
text: named list current theme settings, empty list theme set. Possible keys (optional): orientation — "landscape" "portrait" paper — "letter", "a4", "legal" font_family — e.g. "Times New Roman" font_size — numeric (points) margins — numeric vector (inches) tokens — named list token values pagehead — list left, center, right, font_size, bold pagefoot — list left, center, right, font_size, bold hlines — preset name (e.g. "header") vlines — preset name (e.g. "box") spacing — list titles_after, footnotes_before, pagehead_after, pagefoot_before, page_by_after (integer blank lines) col_gap — integer (points) split — logical (TRUE/FALSE) column splitting stub — character vector (stub column names) group_keep — logical; whether group_by groups kept together group_style — named list; style properties group header rows page_by_style — named list; style properties page_by labels footnote_separator — logical

title: Get the Current Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_get
code: fr_theme(font_size = 9, hlines = "header", orientation = "landscape") fr_theme_get() #> $orientation #> [1] "landscape" #>  #> $font_size #> [1] 9 #>  #> $hlines #> [1] "header" #>  # $font_size # [1] 9 # $hlines # [1] "header" # $orientation # [1] "landscape"  # Programmatic access theme <- fr_theme_get() theme$font_size   # 9 #> [1] 9  ## ── Empty state after reset ───────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme_reset() fr_theme_get()     # list() — no theme settings active #> list() length(fr_theme_get()) == 0L   # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
text: Clears study-level defaults previously set fr_theme(). calling fr_theme_reset(), fr_table() returns built-defaults (YAML config defaults fr_config() loaded). Call test runs, top scripts, switching studies R session.

title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
code: fr_theme_reset()

title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Reset the Study-Level Table Theme — fr_theme_reset
code: ## ── Before / after effect ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  fr_theme(font_size = 9, hlines = "header", orientation = "landscape") fr_theme_get()$font_size        # 9 #> [1] 9 fr_theme_get()$hlines           # "header" #> [1] "header"  fr_theme_reset()                # clear everything fr_theme_get()                  # list() — empty #> list() length(fr_theme_get()) == 0L    # TRUE #> [1] TRUE  ## ── Reset does not affect YAML config ─────────────────────────────────────  # fr_theme_reset() only clears the session theme. # To also clear YAML config, call fr_config_reset() separately. fr_theme(font_size = 8) fr_theme_reset() fr_theme_get()   # list() #> list()

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: Sets one title lines displayed table body. argument ... produces one title line rendered output. Calling fr_titles() replaces previously set titles. Titles support inline {fr_*()} markup — see fr_bold(), fr_italic(), fr_super(), fr_unicode().

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
code: fr_titles(spec, ..., .align = "center", .bold = FALSE, .font_size = NULL)

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). ... One title lines. may : character scalar — plain text, optionally {fr_*()} markup. list. first element text content (can unnamed). Optionally include align, bold, font_size override per-line styling. .align Default alignment title lines. One "left", "center" (default), "right". Can overridden per line via list form. .bold Default bold title lines. FALSE default. .font_size Default font size points. NULL inherits page font size set fr_page().

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: modified fr_spec. Titles stored spec$meta$titles.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: ICH E3 §10 specifies table carry: Line 1 — Table number title (e.g. "Table 14.1.1 Summary Demographics Baseline Characteristics"). Line 2 — Population qualifier (e.g. "Full Analysis Set" "Safety Population"). Line 3 — Study identifier qualifier (optional, e.g. "Study TFRM-2024-001" "Database Cutoff: 31DEC2024"). Standard table numbering scheme (ICH E3 / FDA/EMA submissions): TFL outputs, titles typically left-aligned, 9 pt, bold. Use "center" alignment PDF journal output.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: default, one blank line inserted last title column header. Control fr_spacing():   can also set _arframe.yml:
code: spec |> fr_spacing(titles_after = 0L)   # no gap spec |> fr_spacing(titles_after = 2L)   # two blank lines spacing:   titles_after: 1

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: Pass 0 arguments (fr_titles(spec)) clear titles. Markup titles: use "{fr_bold('Table 14.1.1')}" bold just table number. glue {...} syntax evaluated spec-build time. fr_titles() always replaces — chain multiple calls want progressively build pipeline, last call takes effect.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Set Table Titles — fr_titles
code: ## ── Standard two-line ICH E3 title ──────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics and Baseline Charact..." #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Three-line title with study identifier ───────────────────────────────  tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",     "Safety Analysis Set",     "Study TFRM-2024-001"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 96 rows x 7 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (3): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.3.2 Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Pre..." #> 2. [center] "Safety Analysis Set" #> 3. [center] "Study TFRM-2024-001" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Inline markup in a title ─────────────────────────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "{fr_bold('Table 14.1.1')} Summary of Demographics",     "Full Analysis Set"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> 2. [center] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Per-line styling with list form ──────────────────────────────────────  # List form for per-line styling overrides tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     list("Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics", bold = TRUE, font_size = 10),     list("Full Analysis Set", align = "left", font_size = 9)   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [center] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> 2. [left] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Left-aligned titles (common in pharma RTF) ───────────────────────────  tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles(     "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics",     "Full Analysis Set",     .align = "left"   ) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Titles (2): #> 1. [left] "Table 14.1.1 Summary of Demographics" #> 2. [left] "Full Analysis Set" #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Clear all titles ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_titles("Old Title") spec <- spec |> fr_titles()  # removes all titles

code: # Plus-minus symbol in a label fr_col("Mean {fr_unicode(0x00B1)} SD", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "Mean {fr_unicode(0x00B1)} SD" [1.50in, left]  # Degree symbol fr_col("Temperature ({fr_unicode(0x00B0)}C)", width = 1.5) #> <fr_col> "Temperature ({fr_unicode(0x00B0)}C)" [1.50in, left]  # In a footnote: spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() spec |> fr_footnotes("[a] P {fr_unicode(0x2264)} 0.05 considered significant.") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Footnotes (1): #> 1. what: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: Checks configured fr_spec common misconfigurations cause errors unexpected output render time. Returns spec invisibly valid, making pipeline-friendly. strict mode, issue raises error.

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
code: fr_validate(x, ...)  # Default S3 method fr_validate(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'fr_spec' fr_validate(x, ..., strict = FALSE)

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: x fr_spec object fr_table(). ... Additional arguments (currently unused). strict Logical. TRUE, validation issue raises error. FALSE (default), issues reported warnings spec returned invisibly.

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: Invisibly returns spec valid (strict = FALSE). Raises error strict mode issues found.

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
text: Column names fr_cols() exist data page_by, group_by, indent_by, blank_after columns exist stub_cols exist data N-count column names match column specs Span columns exist contiguous Column widths sum printable area (warning >110%) Style row/col indices valid range Font family recognised sort_by / suppress columns exist (listings)

title: Validate a Table Specification Before Rendering — fr_validate
code: ## ── Clean spec passes validation silently ──────────────────────────────────  spec <- tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header")  spec |> fr_validate()  ## ── Strict mode on a clean spec (no error) ────────────────────────────────  spec |> fr_validate(strict = TRUE)  ## ── Spec with a validation issue: bad column in column specs ────────────────  # Manually inject a column spec for a non-existent column # (fr_cols() validates eagerly, so we set it directly for demo) bad_spec <- tbl_demog |> fr_table() bad_spec$columns[["nonexistent"]] <- fr_col("Bad Column")  # Non-strict mode: warns but returns the spec bad_spec |> fr_validate() #> Warning: ! 1 validation issue found: #> • Column in `fr_cols()` not found in data: "nonexistent".  # Strict mode errors (wrapped in tryCatch for safety) tryCatch(   bad_spec |> fr_validate(strict = TRUE),   error = function(e) message("Caught: ", conditionMessage(e)) ) #> Caught: ! 1 validation issue found: #> • Column in `fr_cols()` not found in data: "nonexistent".  ## ── Validate before rendering in a pipeline ───────────────────────────────  out <- file.path(tempdir(), "validated.rtf") tbl_ae_soc |>   fr_table() |>   fr_titles("Table 14.3.1", "AE by SOC") |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_validate() |>   fr_render(out) unlink(out)

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: Sets vertical rules (column separators) table. Calling fr_vlines() replaces previously set vertical rules. Horizontal rules set fr_hlines() affected.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
code: fr_vlines(   spec,   preset = "box",   cols = NULL,   width = NULL,   color = NULL,   linestyle = NULL,   abovepos = NULL,   belowpos = NULL )

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: spec fr_spec object fr_table(). preset Named rule preset "void" (vertical rules): "box" — rules leftmost rightmost table edges (outer vertical borders). Default. "" — rules every column outer edges. "inner" — rules columns ; outer edges. "void" — vertical rules. Clears previously set vlines. cols Integer vector column gap positions rule. Position j draws rule right column j. Use alongside preset = "void" add individual column separators. NULL uses preset. width Rule width. Named shorthand ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick") numeric points. Default NULL (thin, 0.5 pt). color Rule colour: hex string CSS named colour. NULL = black. linestyle One "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "dashdot", "double". abovepos, belowpos Fractions (0–1) controlling partial vertical extent rule within row. NULL = full height (default).

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: modified fr_spec. Vertical rules stored spec$rules.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: pharma TFL outputs use vertical rules ("void") just outer box border ("box"). Full grids ("") uncommon regulatory submissions may used dense listing tables shift tables column boundaries aid readability.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: fr_vlines("box") common use — clean outer border. Combine fr_hlines("header") typical pharma style: box outer border + single rule column header. cols = c(1L, 3L) draws rule right columns 1 3 — useful separating stub column data columns. fr_vlines() fr_hlines() independent — manages rules. set , use fr_grid().

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
text: Settings resolve four tiers (lowest highest priority): package defaults < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < function. parameters explicitly supply override previous tiers.

title: Apply Vertical Rules — fr_vlines
code: ## ── Common presets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  # Outer box border only (most common) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Rules between every column tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("all") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Inner column separators only (no outer edges) tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("inner") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Remove all vertical rules tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("void") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom  ## ── Custom column positions ───────────────────────────────────────────────  # Rule after the stub column (column 1) only tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_vlines("void") |>   fr_vlines(cols = 1L) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  ## ── Custom width, style, and colour ────────────────────────────────────────  # Thick outer box tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("box", width = "thick") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # Dashed inner separators tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("all", linestyle = "dashed") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  # CSS named colour tbl_demog |> fr_table() |> fr_vlines("box", color = "slategray") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules:  ## ── Combined with fr_hlines ───────────────────────────────────────────────  # Typical regulatory pharma style tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("header") |>   fr_vlines("box") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 1 hline(s)  # Full grid: all borders tbl_demog |>   fr_table() |>   fr_hlines("hsides") |>   fr_vlines("all") #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 28 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rules: 2 hline(s)

what: Convert ARD (Long) to Wide Summary Data Frame — fr_wide_ard
title: Convert ARD (Long) to Wide Summary Data Frame — fr_wide_ard
text: Converts Analysis Results Data (ARD) packages like cards wide summary data frame suitable fr_table(). ARD stores one row per statistic; fr_wide_ard() combines statistics using format strings, pivots treatment arms columns, returns display-ready data frame. function dependency cards — accepts data frame standard ARD columns (variable, stat_name, stat). Handles common post-ard_stack() pipeline shapes: Raw ard_stack() output (list columns) unlist_ard_columns() (atomic columns) rename_ard_columns(columns = all_ard_groups("names")) (group columns renamed e.g. ARM) default rename_ard_columns() (groups variables renamed) Multi-group .= c(ARM, SEX) — extra groups preserved output .(ungrouped)

title: Convert ARD (Long) to Wide Summary Data Frame — fr_wide_ard
code: fr_wide_ard(   data,   statistic = list(continuous = "{mean} ({sd})", categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"),   column = NULL,   label = NULL,   overall = "Total",   decimals = NULL,   fmt = NULL,   big_n = NULL,   pct_display = NULL )

title: Convert ARD (Long) to Wide Summary Data Frame — fr_wide_ard
text: data data frame ARD columns. minimum, requires stat_name stat. Standard ARD also variable, group1/group1_level, variable_level, context. Renamed ARD (rename_ard_columns()) also accepted. Typically produced cards::ard_stack(). statistic Format strings combining statistics display cells. Accepts three forms: Form 1: Named list context (common)   Categorical: one format string produces one row per category level. Continuous: named character vector produces one row per entry, names becoming row labels (e.g., "Mean (SD)", "Median"). Form 2: Named list variable (per-variable override)   Form 3: Single string (applied everything)   Default: list(continuous = "{mean} ({sd})", categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"). column Character scalar. grouping variable becomes display columns (e.g., "ARM", "DOSGRP"). raw ARD, value group1 (auto-detected NULL). renamed ARD, column name data frame. NULL (default), auto-detected data. label Named character vector mapping variable names display labels. E.g., c(AGE = "Age (years)", SEX = "Sex, n (%)"). hierarchical ARD, also renames sentinel variables: c("..ard_hierarchical_overall.." = "Patients TEAE"). NULL, uses variable values -. overall Character scalar. Column header overall/total rows (group1 NA). Default "Total". Set NULL exclude overall rows. decimals Decimal precision stat formatting. Accepts two forms: Global defaults — named integer vector:   Per-variable overrides — named list:   Overrides built-defaults. Default: NULL. fmt Named list custom format functions keyed stat_name. function takes numeric value returns character string. E.g., list(p.value = function(x) (x < 0.001) "<0.001" else sprintf("%.3f", x)). Default: NULL. big_n Character scalar. stat_name value used population counts (BigN) ARD (e.g., "BigN", "bigN", "popn"). specified, rows stat_name extracted body attached attr(result, "n_counts") — named numeric vector suitable fr_cols(.n =). Default: NULL (extraction). pct_display Named list controlling percentage display rules. Partial overrides supported — supplied keys change, others keep defaults. Keys: zero (logical): Display percentage count zero? FALSE (default): n=0 shows "0" . TRUE: n=0 shows full format e.g. "0 (0.0%)". threshold (logical): Apply pharma threshold rules? TRUE (default): extreme percentages display "<0.1" / ">99.9" (dynamic based decimals precision). FALSE: show exact rounded value. Default: NULL (uses list(zero = FALSE, threshold = TRUE)).
code: list(   categorical = "{n} ({p}%)",   continuous  = c("Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})", Median = "{median}") ) list(   AGE = c(n = "{N}", "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})"),   SEX = "{n} ({p}%)",   default = "{n} ({p}%)" ) "{n} ({p}%)" c(mean = 1, sd = 2, p = 0, median = 1) list(   AGE  = c(mean = 1, sd = 2),   BMIBL = c(mean = 2, sd = 3),   .default = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, p = 0) )

title: Convert ARD (Long) to Wide Summary Data Frame — fr_wide_ard
text: data frame columns: Extra group columns (multi-group) e.g., SEX, PARAMCD variable Grouping variable (use fr_rows(group_by =)) stat_label Row label (statistic name category level) One column per treatment arm Formatted display values Total (overall present) Overall column hierarchical ARD (SOC/PT), additional columns: soc System organ class value pt Preferred term value row_type "soc" "pt" big_n specified, attr(result, "n_counts") contains named numeric vector population counts per arm. Pipe directly fr_table():
code: ard |> fr_wide_ard() |> fr_table() |> fr_cols(...) |> fr_render("out.pdf")

title: Convert ARD (Long) to Wide Summary Data Frame — fr_wide_ard
code: if (requireNamespace("cards", quietly = TRUE)) {   ard <- cards::ard_stack(     data = arframe::adsl[arframe::adsl$SAFFL == "Y", ],     .by = "ARM",     cards::ard_continuous(variables = "AGE"),     cards::ard_categorical(variables = "SEX"),     .overall = TRUE   )    # --- Scenario 1: Multi-row continuous output ---   wide <- fr_wide_ard(ard,     statistic = list(       continuous = c(         n           = "{N}",         "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})",         Median      = "{median}",         "Min, Max"  = "{min}, {max}"       ),       categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"     ),     label = c(AGE = "Age (years)", SEX = "Sex"),     decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, p = 0)   )    print(wide)    # --- Scenario 2: Per-variable decimals ---   wide2 <- fr_wide_ard(ard,     statistic = list(       continuous = c("Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})"),       categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"     ),     decimals = list(AGE = c(mean = 2, sd = 3), .default = c(p = 1))   )    # --- Scenario 3: Single string statistic ---   cat_ard <- cards::ard_stack(     data = arframe::adsl[arframe::adsl$SAFFL == "Y", ],     .by = "ARM",     cards::ard_categorical(variables = "SEX")   )   wide3 <- fr_wide_ard(cat_ard, statistic = "{n} ({p}%)")    # --- Scenario 4: Pipe into arframe ---   wide |>     fr_table() |>     fr_cols(       variable   = fr_col(visible = FALSE),       stat_label = fr_col("", width = 2.5),       .align = "decimal"     ) |>     fr_rows(group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat_label")) } #>      variable  stat_label     Placebo Zomerane 100mg Zomerane 50mg       Total #> 1 Age (years)           n          45             45            45         135 #> 2 Age (years)   Mean (SD) 75.0 (6.75)    75.3 (7.09)   73.1 (8.43) 74.4 (7.46) #> 3 Age (years)      Median        74.0           73.0          74.0        74.0 #> 4 Age (years)    Min, Max  65.0, 88.0     55.0, 88.0    55.0, 88.0  55.0, 88.0 #> 5         Sex           F    27 (60%)       20 (44%)      28 (62%)    75 (56%) #> 6         Sex           M    18 (40%)       25 (56%)      17 (38%)    60 (44%) #>  #> ── fr_spec: Table  #> Data: 6 rows x 6 columns #> Page: landscape letter, 9pt Times New Roman #> Columns (5 visible of 6): #> stat_label "" 2.50in decimal #> Placebo "Placebo" 0.61in decimal #> Zomerane 100mg "Zomerane 100mg" 1.00in decimal #> Zomerane 50mg "Zomerane 50mg" 0.93in decimal #> Total "Total" 0.61in decimal #> Header: valign=bottom #> Rows: group_by=variable (label=stat_label), indent_by=stat_label

title: Overall Adverse Event Summary Table (Table 14.3.1.1) — tbl_ae_summary
text: Pre-summarized overall AE summary table showing counts percentages subjects TEAEs category: TEAE, related, serious, leading discontinuation, leading death, maximum severity grade. Matches pharma standard "Overall Summary Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events" shell. Column order follows industry convention: active treatments first, placebo last.

what: Demographics and Baseline Characteristics Table (Table 14.1.1) — tbl_demog
title: Demographics and Baseline Characteristics Table (Table 14.1.1) — tbl_demog
text: Pre-summarized demographics table wide format, ready use fr_table(). One row per characteristic line (headers, summary statistics, category counts). Columns treatment arms.

title: Demographics and Baseline Characteristics Table (Table 14.1.1) — tbl_demog
code: tbl_demog

title: Demographics and Baseline Characteristics Table (Table 14.1.1) — tbl_demog
text: data frame columns: characteristic Row label (e.g. "Age (years)", "  Mean (SD)") placebo Placebo arm summary zom_50mg Zomerane 50mg arm summary zom_100mg Zomerane 100mg arm summary total subjects summary group Characteristic group key. Age uses separate keys continuous ("age_cont") categorical ("age_cat") summaries; blocks use "n", "sex", "race", "bmi", "mmse", "completion". Use fr_rows(blank_after = "group") insert visual separation blocks. Hide fr_cols(group = fr_col(visible = FALSE)).

title: Time-to-Event Summary Table (Table 14.2.1.1) — tbl_tte
text: Pre-summarized time--withdrawal table matching pharma reference shell Kaplan-Meier survival analysis output. Includes four sections: Event counts: events censored n (%) per arm KM percentile estimates: 25th percentile, median, 75th percentile 95% confidence intervals Log-rank test: two-sided p-value Hazard ratios: active vs placebo 95% CI KM estimates hazard ratios pre-computed synthetic values (survival package dependency). Values realistic 24-week trial low event rates.

title: Time-to-Event Summary Table (Table 14.2.1.1) — tbl_tte
code: tbl_tte

title: Time-to-Event Summary Table (Table 14.2.1.1) — tbl_tte
text: data frame 12 rows 5 columns: section Section grouping key: "Time Study Withdrawal", "KM Estimates", "Log-Rank Test", "Hazard Ratio". Use fr_rows(group_by = "section", blank_after = "section") visual separation blocks statistic Row label indentation (e.g. "  Median (95% CI) []", "  Zom 50mg vs Placebo"). Section headers indent; detail rows indented two spaces zom_50mg Zomerane 50mg arm value zom_100mg Zomerane 100mg arm value placebo Placebo arm value

what: Vital Signs Change from Baseline Table (Table 14.3.5.1) — tbl_vs
title: Vital Signs Change from Baseline Table (Table 14.3.5.1) — tbl_vs
text: Pre-summarized vital signs table wide format per-arm sub-columns Baseline, Value, Change Baseline. Multiple timepoints (Baseline, Week 12, Week 24) per parameter. Designed demonstrate advanced arframe features: page_by per-parameter pages, fr_spans() two-level spanning headers, fr_cols() .n data frame per-group N-counts, fr_cols(.split = TRUE) wide layouts. Pair advs via data frame fr_cols() .n get per-parameter N-counts column headers.

title: Vital Signs Change from Baseline Table (Table 14.3.5.1) — tbl_vs
code: tbl_vs

title: Vital Signs Change from Baseline Table (Table 14.3.5.1) — tbl_vs

arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_wide_ard() converts ARD (Analysis Results Data) cards/cardx wide summary data frames fr_table(). Supports multi-row continuous output (named vector format specs), per-variable decimal precision, hierarchical SOC/PT structures, custom format functions (including p.value < 0.001 threshold), 60+ cards/cardx stat types. Zero dependency cards — duck-types column names. fr_rows() refactored 14 params 9. group_label moves group_by list form: group_by = list(cols = "variable", label = "stat_label"). page_by_visible moves page_by list form: page_by = list(cols = "PARAM", visible = FALSE). page_by_bold, page_by_align, group_bold removed — style via fr_styles() instead.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_rows(group_by) longer auto-implies blank_after. get blank rows groups, set blank_after explicitly: fr_rows(group_by = "col", blank_after = "col").

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: Fixed decimal alignment across pages: page 2+ showing page 1’s values geometry indices used per-page row numbers instead global indices. three backends (RTF, HTML, LaTeX) now correctly map page-local rows global decimal geometry. Fixed decimal alignment mixed integer + decimal columns: columns mixing pure integers (e.g., “143”) decimal stats (e.g., “75.7 (8.19)”) now align correctly. Fixed n_only decimal alignment: widened integer zone columns wider n values (e.g., 3-digit counts). Added pharma percentage boundary rules integer percentages (d=0): handles edge cases like “100%” “0” alignment. Unified internal decimal parser: eliminated parallel implementations single stat-type detection + alignment engine. Injected group header rows blank-rows now inherit page_by column values, fixing incorrect page splits page_by group_by/blank_after used together.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: Default font changed Courier New Times New Roman (FDA recommended submission documents). Font resolution order: FDA-recommended → ARFRAME_FONT_DIR → Adobe open-source (Source Serif 4, Source Sans 3, Source Code Pro) → CSS generic. Latin Modern Liberation fonts removed fallback chain. New vignettes("fonts") documents full font resolution order FDA guidance.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: HTML output now respects fr_spacing() settings: titles_after, footnotes_before, pagehead_after, pagefoot_before, page_by_after. col_gap wired HTML cell padding (matching RTF). pagehead/pagefoot font_size applied HTML output. Knitr/pkgdown output uses web-optimized sans-serif font (Source Sans 3). Bootstrap table style reset prevents pkgdown overriding arframe table borders. File output uses plain white background (matching R HTML widget preview). Fixed pkgdown “page” sidebar displacement adding overflow: hidden outer container. CSS font stack deduplication (repeated font names).

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: New HTML output format: fr_render(spec, "output.html") produces self-contained HTML document paper simulation (orientation-aware page dimensions, margins, flexbox page footer push-). print(spec) auto-previews RStudio/Positron Viewer panel via htmltools::browsable() — just type spec see , like gt. knit_print.fr_spec() renders tables inline R Markdown, Quarto, pkgdown vignettes scoped CSS (multiple tables one page don’t conflict). vignettes now show live rendered tables instead static PNG screenshots.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_figure() now accepts list plot objects optional meta data frame. column meta becomes {token} resolved titles footnotes dynamically per page, enabling figure programmes loop parameters (e.g. one page per biomarker visit).

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_rows(group_by = list(cols = ..., label = ...)) auto-injects header row group boundary using values specified column. standard demographics pattern group variable label appears row indented summary statistics, without requiring pre-formatted data.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_rows(indent_by = ) now accepts named list specifying key, col, levels SOC/HLT/PT three-level hierarchies. row indented natural depth, replacing previous single-column form handled two levels.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_rows(group_keep = FALSE) disables RTF \keepn visual-grouping (blank rows indentation without page-keep constraints). Useful groups short default keep behaviour wastes space. fr_rows(page_by = list(cols = ..., visible = FALSE)) inserts page breaks page_by boundaries hiding label row . fr_page(orphan_min = , widow_min = ) sets minimum body-row counts bottom top page respectively, preventing isolated rows page breaks.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_rows(wrap = ) controls whether long cell content wraps within column truncated, complementing existing suppress behaviour.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: measure_text_width_twips(): multi-byte UTF-8 characters (em dashes, arrows, etc.) now measured correctly via utf8ToInt() instead misindexing raw-byte lookup table. resolve_borders(): early return table body empty prevents index---bounds crashes zero-row tables. escape_and_resolve(): guard malformed sentinel tokens fewer three capture groups (subscript---bounds crash). fr_rows(): error group_by$label set without group_by$cols. fr_rows(): warning page_by group_by share column. LaTeX hline dedup regex anchored ^ prevent row 10 matching row 100. Decimal alignment: round() replaces .integer() center-offset calculation, removing systematic 1-twip leftward bias. paginate_rows(): consecutive trailing blank rows captured, just first.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_table(), fr_listing(), fr_figure() entry points tables, patient listings, embedded figures. 10 pipeline verbs: fr_cols(), fr_titles(), fr_footnotes(), fr_header(), fr_rows(), fr_hlines(), fr_vlines(), fr_spans(), fr_styles(), fr_page(). fr_render() produces RTF PDF spec. Verb order irrelevant – resolution deferred render time.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_col() constructor width, alignment, visibility, grouping. Width modes: fixed inches, percentages, "auto" (AFM font metrics), "fit", "equal". Decimal alignment engine (15 stat-display types) pharma summary tables. N-count injection via .n .n_format fr_cols(). Column splitting (.split = TRUE) wide tables exceeding page width.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: Native RTF 1.9.1 backend field codes page numbering. LaTeX/tabularray backend compiled via XeLaTeX PDF output. R-side pagination group-aware page breaks. Token system ({thepage}, {total_pages}, {program}, {datetime}) running headers footers. Adobe open-source font fallback PDF/RTF Linux/Docker without Microsoft fonts. ARFRAME_FONT_DIR environment variable: point directory .ttf/.otf files project-local fonts without system-wide installation. Ideal Docker/CI pipelines.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_style(), fr_row_style(), fr_col_style() cell-level formatting. fr_style_if() data-driven conditional styles. fr_rows_matches() pattern-based row selectors. fr_style_explain() debugging style cascade resolution. Inline markup: fr_super(), fr_sub(), fr_bold(), fr_italic(), fr_dagger(), fr_emdash(), .

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: Four-tier defaults: package < _arframe.yml < fr_theme() < per-table verbs. fr_recipe() fr_apply() reusable pipeline fragments. c.fr_recipe() composing recipes (company + study + table-type layers).

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: fr_validate() pre-render checks columns, widths, styles, spans, fonts. fr_get_*() accessors programmatic QC spec internals.

title: arframe 0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: Synthetic CDISC ADaM datasets (study TFRM-2024-001, 135 subjects): adsl, adae, adtte, adcm, advs. Pre-summarized TFL-ready tables: tbl_demog, tbl_ae_soc, tbl_ae_summary, tbl_disp, tbl_tte, tbl_cm, tbl_vs.
